```text
1	package store
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"database/sql"
6		"time"
7	)
8	
9	type WaterBalloon struct {
10		ID            int64
11		FromUserID    int64
12		FromUserIDStr string
13		ToUserID      int64
14		Body          string
15		DeliveredLive bool
16		ReadAt        sql.NullTime
17		CreatedAt     time.Time
18	}
19	
20	// WBCounterparty is one row of the per-counterparty inbox roll-up. The fields
21	// describe the OTHER user (not the viewer): UserID/UserIDStr is the canonical
22	// identity from the users table (so a renamed handle reads correctly),
23	// LastBody/LastFromMe/LastAt summarise the latest message exchanged with them,
24	// and UnreadCount is how many they've sent the viewer that aren't read yet.
25	type WBCounterparty struct {
26		UserID      int64
27		UserIDStr   string
28		LastBody    string
29		LastFromMe  bool
30		LastAt      time.Time
31		UnreadCount int64
32	}
33	
34	type WaterBalloonRepo struct{ s *Store }
35	
36	const wbColumns = `id, from_user_id, from_userid, to_user_id, body, delivered_live, read_at, created_at`
37	
38	func scanWB(row interface{ Scan(...any) error }) (*WaterBalloon, error) {
39		w := &WaterBalloon{}
40		var deliveredLive int64
41		if err := row.Scan(&w.ID, &w.FromUserID, &w.FromUserIDStr, &w.ToUserID, &w.Body, &deliveredLive, &w.ReadAt, &w.CreatedAt); err != nil {
42			return nil, err
43		}
44		w.DeliveredLive = deliveredLive != 0
45		return w, nil
46	}
47	
48	func (r *WaterBalloonRepo) Insert(ctx context.Context, fromID int64, fromUserID string, toID int64, body string, deliveredLive bool) (*WaterBalloon, error) {
49		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
50		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
51		live := int64(0)
52		if deliveredLive {
53			live = 1
54		}
55		res, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
56			`INSERT INTO water_balloons (from_user_id, from_userid, to_user_id, body, delivered_live) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)`,
57			fromID, fromUserID, toID, body, live,
58		)
59		if err != nil {
60			return nil, err
61		}
62		id, err := res.LastInsertId()
63		if err != nil {
64			return nil, err
65		}
66		row := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, `SELECT `+wbColumns+` FROM water_balloons WHERE id = ?`, id)
67		return scanWB(row)
68	}
69	
70	// ListUnreadFor returns unread water balloons for the recipient, oldest
71	// first, so the TUI can replay them on reconnect.
72	func (r *WaterBalloonRepo) ListUnreadFor(ctx context.Context, toUserID int64) ([]*WaterBalloon, error) {
73		rows, err := r.s.db.QueryContext(ctx,
74			`SELECT `+wbColumns+` FROM water_balloons WHERE to_user_id = ? AND read_at IS NULL ORDER BY id ASC`,
75			toUserID,
76		)
77		if err != nil {
78			return nil, err
79		}
80		defer rows.Close()
81		var out []*WaterBalloon
82		for rows.Next() {
83			w, err := scanWB(rows)
84			if err != nil {
85				return nil, err
86			}
87			out = append(out, w)
88		}
89		return out, rows.Err()
90	}
91	
92	// ListInboxFor returns the recipient's inbox: unread first (newest unread
93	// up top), then read messages newest-first.
94	func (r *WaterBalloonRepo) ListInboxFor(ctx context.Context, toUserID int64, limit int) ([]*WaterBalloon, error) {
95		if limit <= 0 {
96			limit = 50
97		}
98		rows, err := r.s.db.QueryContext(ctx,
99			`SELECT `+wbColumns+` FROM water_balloons WHERE to_user_id = ?
100			 ORDER BY (read_at IS NULL) DESC, id DESC LIMIT ?`,
101			toUserID, limit,
102		)
103		if err != nil {
104			return nil, err
105		}
106		defer rows.Close()
107		var out []*WaterBalloon
108		for rows.Next() {
109			w, err := scanWB(rows)
110			if err != nil {
111				return nil, err
112			}
113			out = append(out, w)
114		}
115		return out, rows.Err()
116	}
117	
118	func (r *WaterBalloonRepo) MarkRead(ctx context.Context, id int64) error {
119		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
120		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
121		_, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
122			`UPDATE water_balloons SET read_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP WHERE id = ? AND read_at IS NULL`, id,
123		)
124		return err
125	}
126	
127	func (r *WaterBalloonRepo) MarkAllReadFor(ctx context.Context, toUserID int64) error {
128		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
129		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
130		_, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
131			`UPDATE water_balloons SET read_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP WHERE to_user_id = ? AND read_at IS NULL`,
132			toUserID,
133		)
134		return err
135	}
136	
137	// GetByID fetches a single water balloon. Used by the thread screen's
138	// live-append path so a freshly-arrived WBIncomingMsg can be turned into
139	// a canonical row (with read_at, created_at) rather than reconstructed.
140	func (r *WaterBalloonRepo) GetByID(ctx context.Context, id int64) (*WaterBalloon, error) {
141		row := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, `SELECT `+wbColumns+` FROM water_balloons WHERE id = ?`, id)
142		return scanWB(row)
143	}
144	
145	// ListConversation returns every water balloon between viewerID and
146	// counterpartyID, both directions, oldest-first so the chat-style view can
147	// append at the bottom. Phase 1 doesn't paginate; the limit clips oldest.
148	// TODO: when "load older" lands, switch to ORDER BY id DESC + offset and
149	// reverse client-side.
150	func (r *WaterBalloonRepo) ListConversation(ctx context.Context, viewerID, counterpartyID int64, limit int) ([]*WaterBalloon, error) {
151		if limit <= 0 {
152			limit = 200
153		}
154		rows, err := r.s.db.QueryContext(ctx,
155			`SELECT `+wbColumns+` FROM water_balloons
156			 WHERE (from_user_id = ? AND to_user_id = ?) OR (from_user_id = ? AND to_user_id = ?)
157			 ORDER BY id ASC LIMIT ?`,
158			viewerID, counterpartyID, counterpartyID, viewerID, limit,
159		)
160		if err != nil {
161			return nil, err
162		}
163		defer rows.Close()
164		var out []*WaterBalloon
165		for rows.Next() {
166			w, err := scanWB(rows)
167			if err != nil {
168				return nil, err
169			}
170			out = append(out, w)
171		}
172		return out, rows.Err()
173	}
174	
175	// ListCounterpartiesFor returns one row per counterparty the viewer has
176	// exchanged water balloons with, sorted unread-first then by recency. The
177	// counterparty handle (UserIDStr) is read via JOIN on users.id so it tracks
178	// the user's current handle rather than the (potentially stale) from_userid
179	// snapshot stored on the row at insert time.
180	//
181	// MAX(id) is used to resolve the latest row per counterparty (not
182	// MAX(created_at)) because SQLite's CURRENT_TIMESTAMP only has 1-second
183	// resolution and rapid-fire inserts within the same second would tie.
184	//
185	// TODO: when user delete lands, the inner JOIN to users will silently drop
186	// conversations whose counterparty no longer exists. Switch to LEFT JOIN
187	// with a fallback handle (e.g., COALESCE(u.user_id, '[deleted]')) at that
188	// point.
189	func (r *WaterBalloonRepo) ListCounterpartiesFor(ctx context.Context, viewerID int64, limit int) ([]*WBCounterparty, error) {
190		if limit <= 0 {
191			limit = 50
192		}
193		// last_at is r.created_at from the JOIN'd latest row (resolved via
194		// MAX(id), since SQLite's CURRENT_TIMESTAMP only has 1-second resolution
195		// and would tie under rapid-fire inserts). We deliberately don't return
196		// MAX(created_at) from agg — modernc/sqlite drops the time.Time type hint
197		// across aggregates and returns it as a string, which would fail Scan.
198		const q = `
199			WITH related AS (
200				SELECT id, from_user_id, to_user_id, body, read_at, created_at,
201				       CASE WHEN from_user_id = ? THEN to_user_id ELSE from_user_id END AS cp_id
202				FROM water_balloons
203				WHERE from_user_id = ? OR to_user_id = ?
204			),
205			agg AS (
206				SELECT cp_id,
207				       MAX(id) AS last_id,
208				       SUM(CASE WHEN to_user_id = ? AND read_at IS NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS unread_count
209				FROM related
210				GROUP BY cp_id
211			)
212			SELECT a.cp_id, u.user_id, r.body, (r.from_user_id = ?), r.created_at, a.unread_count
213			FROM agg a
214			JOIN related r ON r.id = a.last_id
215			JOIN users    u ON u.id = a.cp_id
216			ORDER BY (a.unread_count > 0) DESC, r.created_at DESC
217			LIMIT ?`
218		rows, err := r.s.db.QueryContext(ctx, q,
219			viewerID, viewerID, viewerID, viewerID, viewerID, limit,
220		)
221		if err != nil {
222			return nil, err
223		}
224		defer rows.Close()
225		var out []*WBCounterparty
226		for rows.Next() {
227			c := &WBCounterparty{}
228			var lastFromMe int64
229			if err := rows.Scan(&c.UserID, &c.UserIDStr, &c.LastBody, &lastFromMe, &c.LastAt, &c.UnreadCount); err != nil {
230				return nil, err
231			}
232			c.LastFromMe = lastFromMe != 0
233			out = append(out, c)
234		}
235		return out, rows.Err()
236	}
237	
238	// MarkConversationRead marks every inbound water balloon from counterpartyID
239	// to viewerID as read in one statement. Idempotent (the read_at IS NULL
240	// filter makes the second call a no-op). Outbound rows (viewer→counterparty)
241	// are deliberately untouched: those are already read by the sender.
242	func (r *WaterBalloonRepo) MarkConversationRead(ctx context.Context, viewerID, counterpartyID int64) error {
243		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
244		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
245		_, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
246			`UPDATE water_balloons SET read_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
247			 WHERE to_user_id = ? AND from_user_id = ? AND read_at IS NULL`,
248			viewerID, counterpartyID,
249		)
250		return err
251	}
252
```

1	package tui
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"database/sql"
6		"errors"
7		"fmt"
8		"strings"
9		"time"
10	
11		"github.com/charmbracelet/bubbles/textarea"
12		"github.com/charmbracelet/bubbles/textinput"
13		tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"
14	
15		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store"
16	)
17	
18	// =====================================================================
19	// Inbox
20	// =====================================================================
21	
22	type wbInboxModel struct {
23		deps    Deps
24		items   []*store.WBCounterparty
25		cursor  int
26		width   int
27		height  int
28		loadErr error
29	}
30	
31	func newWBInboxModel(deps Deps) wbInboxModel {
32		items, err := deps.Store.WaterBalloons().ListCounterpartiesFor(context.Background(), deps.User.ID, 100)
33		// NOTE: we deliberately do NOT mark anything as read here. Mark-read
34		// now happens on entering a specific thread (see wbThreadModel).
35		return wbInboxModel{deps: deps, items: items, loadErr: err}
36	}
37	
38	func (m wbInboxModel) Init() tea.Cmd { return nil }
39	
40	func (m wbInboxModel) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
41		switch msg := msg.(type) {
42		case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
43			m.width, m.height = msg.Width, msg.Height
44			return m, nil
45		case tea.KeyMsg:
46			switch msg.String() {
47			case "esc", "backspace", "left", "h":
48				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenMainMenu} }
49			case "up", "k":
50				if m.cursor > 0 {
51					m.cursor--
52				}
53			case "down", "j":
54				if m.cursor < len(m.items)-1 {
55					m.cursor++
56				}
57			case "c":
58				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenWBCompose} }
59			case "r", "enter", " ", "right", "l":
60				if len(m.items) == 0 {
61					return m, nil
62				}
63				it := m.items[m.cursor]
64				return m, func() tea.Msg {
65					return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenWBThread, CounterpartyUserID: it.UserID}
66				}
67			}
68		}
69		return m, nil
70	}
71	
72	func (m wbInboxModel) View() string {
73		var b strings.Builder
74		b.WriteString("\n")
75		b.WriteString(StyleHeader.Render(" 水球 Water Balloons "))
76		b.WriteString("\n\n")
77	
78		if m.loadErr != nil {
79			b.WriteString("  " + StyleError.Render("⚠ "+m.loadErr.Error()) + "\n")
80			return b.String()
81		}
82		if len(m.items) == 0 {
83			b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("(no messages — press c to compose)") + "\n")
84			b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleHelp.Render("c compose · Esc/←/h back"))
85			return b.String()
86		}
87	
88		const (
89			dateW   = 16
90			nameW   = 14
91			statusW = 5
92		)
93		previewW := max(20, m.width-2-dateW-1-nameW-1-statusW-3)
94		header := fmt.Sprintf(" %s  %s  %s  %s",
95			PadRight("Last", dateW),
96			PadRight("With", nameW),
97			PadRight("New", statusW),
98			PadRight("Preview", previewW),
99		)
100		b.WriteString(StyleDim.Render(header) + "\n")
101	
102		viewerID := int64(0)
103		if m.deps.User != nil {
104			viewerID = m.deps.User.ID
105		}
106		for i, it := range m.items {
107			status := "·"
108			if it.UnreadCount > 0 {
109				status = fmt.Sprintf("%d", it.UnreadCount)
110			}
111			nameStr := it.UserIDStr
112			preview := it.LastBody
113			if it.UserID == viewerID {
114				// Self-thread shows as "📝 yourself"; the preview drops the
115				// redundant "you:" prefix since both sender and recipient
116				// are the viewer.
117				nameStr = "📝 yourself"
118			} else if it.LastFromMe {
119				preview = "you: " + preview
120			}
121			row := fmt.Sprintf(" %s  %s  %s  %s",
122				PadRight(it.LastAt.Format("2006-01-02 15:04"), dateW),
123				PadRight(nameStr, nameW),
124				PadRight(status, statusW),
125				Truncate(preview, previewW),
126			)
127			if i == m.cursor {
128				b.WriteString(StyleHighlight.Render("▸"+row[1:]) + "\n")
129			} else {
130				b.WriteString(" " + row + "\n")
131			}
132		}
133	
134		b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleHelp.Render("↑/↓ j/k move · Enter/→/l/r open · c compose · Esc/←/h back"))
135		b.WriteString("\n")
136		return b.String()
137	}
138	
139	// =====================================================================
140	// Compose
141	// =====================================================================
142	
143	const (
144		wbFocusTo = iota
145		wbFocusBody
146	)
147	
148	type wbComposeModel struct {
149		deps   Deps
150		to     textinput.Model
151		body   textarea.Model
152		focus  int
153		width  int
154		height int
155		err    string
156		sent   string // success: recipient userid
157	}
158	
159	func newWBComposeModel(deps Deps, recipient string) wbComposeModel {
160		to := textinput.New()
161		to.Placeholder = "alice"
162		to.CharLimit = 12
163		to.Width = 30
164		to.SetValue(recipient)
165	
166		body := textarea.New()
167		body.Placeholder = "your one-line message…"
168		body.CharLimit = 240
169		body.SetWidth(60)
170		body.SetHeight(4)
171	
172		m := wbComposeModel{deps: deps, to: to, body: body}
173		if recipient == "" {
174			m.focus = wbFocusTo
175			m.to.Focus()
176		} else {
177			m.focus = wbFocusBody
178			m.body.Focus()
179		}
180		return m
181	}
182	
183	func (m wbComposeModel) Init() tea.Cmd { return textinput.Blink }
184	
185	func (m wbComposeModel) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
186		switch msg := msg.(type) {
187		case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
188			m.width, m.height = msg.Width, msg.Height
189			m.body.SetWidth(max(40, msg.Width-12))
190			return m, nil
191	
192		case tea.KeyMsg:
193			if m.sent != "" {
194				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenWBInbox} }
195			}
196			switch msg.String() {
197			case "esc":
198				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenWBInbox} }
199			case "tab":
200				m.toggleFocus()
201				return m, nil
202			case "ctrl+s":
203				return m.submit()
204			}
205	
206			if m.focus == wbFocusTo {
207				if k := msg.String(); k == "enter" {
208					m.toggleFocus()
209					return m, nil
210				}
211				var cmd tea.Cmd
212				m.to, cmd = m.to.Update(msg)
213				return m, cmd
214			}
215			var cmd tea.Cmd
216			m.body, cmd = m.body.Update(msg)
217			return m, cmd
218		}
219		return m, nil
220	}
221	
222	func (m *wbComposeModel) toggleFocus() {
223		if m.focus == wbFocusTo {
224			m.focus = wbFocusBody
225			m.to.Blur()
226			m.body.Focus()
227		} else {
228			m.focus = wbFocusTo
229			m.body.Blur()
230			m.to.Focus()
231		}
232	}
233	
234	func (m wbComposeModel) submit() (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
235		to := strings.TrimSpace(m.to.Value())
236		body := strings.TrimSpace(m.body.Value())
237		if to == "" {
238			m.err = "recipient required"
239			return m, nil
240		}
241		if body == "" {
242			m.err = "body required"
243			return m, nil
244		}
245	
246		ctx := context.Background()
247		target, err := m.deps.Store.Users().GetByUserID(ctx, to)
248		if err != nil {
249			if errors.Is(err, store.ErrUserNotFound) {
250				m.err = "no such user: " + to
251			} else {
252				m.err = err.Error()
253			}
254			return m, nil
255		}
256		from := m.deps.User
257	
258		wb, err := m.deps.Store.WaterBalloons().Insert(ctx, from.ID, from.UserID, target.ID, body, false)
259		if err != nil {
260			m.err = err.Error()
261			return m, nil
262		}
263		if target.ID == from.ID {
264			// Self-WB (memo). Broker.Send to the caller's own session would
265			// deadlock the unbuffered bubbletea msgs channel — see
266			// pitfalls/water-balloon-self-send-hangs-server.md. Skip the
267			// notification (you typed it, you saw it) and mark read so the
268			// row doesn't replay as a toast on next reconnect.
269			_ = m.deps.Store.WaterBalloons().MarkRead(ctx, wb.ID)
270		} else if m.deps.Broker != nil {
271			delivered := m.deps.Broker.Send(target.ID, WBIncomingMsg{
272				ID:         wb.ID,
273				FromUserID: from.UserID,
274				Body:       body,
275			})
276			if delivered {
277				// The recipient saw it live, so we can mark it read immediately.
278				_ = m.deps.Store.WaterBalloons().MarkRead(ctx, wb.ID)
279			}
280		}
281		m.sent = target.UserID
282		return m, nil
283	}
284	
285	func (m wbComposeModel) View() string {
286		var b strings.Builder
287		b.WriteString("\n")
288		b.WriteString(StyleHeader.Render(" 丟水球 Send Water Balloon "))
289		b.WriteString("\n\n")
290	
291		if m.sent != "" {
292			b.WriteString("  " + StyleSuccess.Render("✓ sent to "+m.sent) + "\n\n")
293			b.WriteString("  " + StyleHelp.Render("any key returns to inbox"))
294			return b.String()
295		}
296	
297		b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("To (userid):") + "\n")
298		b.WriteString("  " + m.to.View() + "\n\n")
299		b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("Body:") + "\n")
300		b.WriteString("  " + indent(m.body.View(), "  ") + "\n")
301	
302		if m.err != "" {
303			b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleError.Render("⚠ "+m.err) + "\n")
304		}
305		b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleHelp.Render("Tab switch field · Enter (in To) → body · Ctrl+S send · Esc cancel"))
306		return b.String()
307	}
308	
309	// =====================================================================
310	// Thread (1-to-1 DM-style conversation with one counterparty)
311	//
312	// Layout: scrollback on top, single-line textinput at the bottom. Default
313	// focus is the input (chat-app convention). Tab toggles focus to the
314	// scrollback for vim-style scrolling. Sending Enter on a non-empty input
315	// goes through the same Insert + Broker.Send path as wbComposeModel so
316	// persistence and replay stay identical.
317	// =====================================================================
318	
319	type wbThreadModel struct {
320		deps       Deps
321		cpID       int64  // counterparty user.id
322		cpUserID   string // counterparty current handle (from users JOIN, not from_userid snapshot)
323		items      []*store.WaterBalloon
324		scroll     int
325		width      int
326		height     int
327		loadErr    error
328		input      textinput.Model
329		focusInput bool   // true = textinput receives keystrokes; false = scrollback nav
330		err        string // last send error, cleared on next keystroke
331	}
332	
333	func newWBThreadModel(deps Deps, counterpartyID int64) wbThreadModel {
334		ctx := context.Background()
335		cp, err := deps.Store.Users().GetByID(ctx, counterpartyID)
336		if err != nil {
337			return wbThreadModel{deps: deps, cpID: counterpartyID, loadErr: err}
338		}
339		items, err := deps.Store.WaterBalloons().ListConversation(ctx, deps.User.ID, counterpartyID, 200)
340		if err == nil {
341			// Mark only the inbound side as read on entering the thread.
342			// Replaces the old "open inbox = ack everything" behaviour.
343			_ = deps.Store.WaterBalloons().MarkConversationRead(ctx, deps.User.ID, counterpartyID)
344			// Reflect the mark in our local copy so View doesn't show stale "NEW".
345			now := time.Now()
346			for _, w := range items {
347				if w.ToUserID == deps.User.ID && !w.ReadAt.Valid {
348					w.ReadAt = sql.NullTime{Time: now, Valid: true}
349				}
350			}
351		}
352	
353		in := textinput.New()
354		in.Placeholder = "type a message…"
355		in.CharLimit = 240
356		in.Width = 60
357		in.Prompt = "> "
358		in.Focus()
359	
360		return wbThreadModel{
361			deps:       deps,
362			cpID:       counterpartyID,
363			cpUserID:   cp.UserID,
364			items:      items,
365			loadErr:    err,
366			input:      in,
367			focusInput: true,
368			scroll:     1 << 30, // start at bottom
369		}
370	}
371	
372	func (m wbThreadModel) Init() tea.Cmd { return textinput.Blink }
373	
374	// matchesCounterparty reports whether handle (case-insensitive) names this
375	// thread's counterparty. Used by Root.Update to suppress the toast when an
376	// incoming wb is already going to be appended into the thread.
377	func (m wbThreadModel) matchesCounterparty(handle string) bool {
378		return strings.EqualFold(handle, m.cpUserID)
379	}
380	
381	func (m wbThreadModel) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
382		switch msg := msg.(type) {
383		case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
384			m.width, m.height = msg.Width, msg.Height
385			// Reserve room for prompt and a little chrome around the input.
386			m.input.Width = max(20, msg.Width-8)
387			return m, nil
388		case WBIncomingMsg:
389			if !m.matchesCounterparty(msg.FromUserID) {
390				return m, nil
391			}
392			ctx := context.Background()
393			if wb, err := m.deps.Store.WaterBalloons().GetByID(ctx, msg.ID); err == nil {
394				m.items = append(m.items, wb)
395				m.scroll = 1 << 30
396			}
397			_ = m.deps.Store.WaterBalloons().MarkRead(ctx, msg.ID)
398			return m, nil
399		case tea.KeyMsg:
400			// Clear stale error on any keypress.
401			m.err = ""
402			if m.focusInput {
403				return m.updateInputFocused(msg)
404			}
405			return m.updateScrollFocused(msg)
406		}
407		return m, nil
408	}
409	
410	// updateInputFocused handles keys while the textinput has focus. The
411	// keymap is intentionally narrow — only Esc / Tab / scroll keys / Enter
412	// are intercepted; everything else (including h, j, k, l, q, Q, c, r and
413	// punctuation) is forwarded to the textinput so the user can type freely.
414	func (m wbThreadModel) updateInputFocused(msg tea.KeyMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
415		switch msg.String() {
416		case "esc":
417			return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenWBInbox} }
418		case "tab":
419			m.focusInput = false
420			m.input.Blur()
421			return m, nil
422		case "up", "pgup":
423			if m.scroll > 0 {
424				m.scroll--
425			}
426			return m, nil
427		case "down", "pgdown":
428			m.scroll++ // clamped at render time
429			return m, nil
430		case "enter":
431			return m.submit()
432		}
433		var cmd tea.Cmd
434		m.input, cmd = m.input.Update(msg)
435		return m, cmd
436	}
437	
438	// updateScrollFocused handles keys while the scrollback has focus.
439	// Familiar list-screen vim keys; Tab returns focus to the input.
440	func (m wbThreadModel) updateScrollFocused(msg tea.KeyMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
441		switch msg.String() {
442		case "esc", "backspace", "left", "h":
443			return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenWBInbox} }
444		case "Q":
445			return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenMainMenu} }
446		case "tab":
447			m.focusInput = true
448			m.input.Focus()
449			return m, textinput.Blink
450		case "up", "k", "pgup":
451			if m.scroll > 0 {
452				m.scroll--
453			}
454		case "down", "j", "pgdown":
455			m.scroll++
456		case "g", "home":
457			m.scroll = 0
458		case "G", "end":
459			m.scroll = 1 << 30
460		}
461		return m, nil
462	}
463	
464	// submit posts the current input as a new water balloon. Mirrors
465	// wbComposeModel.submit so persistence and broker delivery behaviour
466	// stay identical — only the UI surfacing differs (inline append + clear
467	// instead of a "✓ sent" page transition).
468	func (m wbThreadModel) submit() (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
469		body := strings.TrimSpace(m.input.Value())
470		if body == "" {
471			return m, nil
472		}
473		from := m.deps.User
474		ctx := context.Background()
475		wb, err := m.deps.Store.WaterBalloons().Insert(ctx, from.ID, from.UserID, m.cpID, body, false)
476		if err != nil {
477			m.err = err.Error()
478			return m, nil
479		}
480		if m.cpID == from.ID {
481			// Self-thread / memo: skip Broker.Send (would deadlock the
482			// unbuffered msgs channel), mark read immediately. See
483			// pitfalls/water-balloon-self-send-hangs-server.md.
484			_ = m.deps.Store.WaterBalloons().MarkRead(ctx, wb.ID)
485		} else if m.deps.Broker != nil {
486			delivered := m.deps.Broker.Send(m.cpID, WBIncomingMsg{
487				ID:         wb.ID,
488				FromUserID: from.UserID,
489				Body:       body,
490			})
491			if delivered {
492				_ = m.deps.Store.WaterBalloons().MarkRead(ctx, wb.ID)
493			}
494		}
495		m.items = append(m.items, wb)
496		m.input.SetValue("")
497		m.scroll = 1 << 30
498		return m, nil
499	}
500	
501	func (m wbThreadModel) View() string {
502		var b strings.Builder
503		b.WriteString("\n")
504		headerLabel := m.cpUserID
505		if m.deps.User != nil && m.cpID == m.deps.User.ID {
506			headerLabel = "📝 yourself (memo)"
507		}
508		b.WriteString(StyleHeader.Render(fmt.Sprintf(" 對話 with %s ", headerLabel)))
509		b.WriteString("\n\n")
510	
511		if m.loadErr != nil {
512			b.WriteString("  " + StyleError.Render("⚠ "+m.loadErr.Error()) + "\n")
513			return b.String()
514		}
515	
516		// Scrollback occupies all the vertical space that isn't taken by the
517		// input row, the optional error line, the help line, and the surrounding
518		// blank rows / header.
519		lines := m.renderLines()
520		const reserved = 7 // header(2) + input(1) + help(2) + padding(2)
521		visible := m.height - reserved
522		if visible < 1 {
523			visible = len(lines)
524		}
525		if m.scroll > max(0, len(lines)-visible) {
526			m.scroll = max(0, len(lines)-visible)
527		}
528		if len(lines) == 0 {
529			b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("(empty thread — type below to start)") + "\n")
530		} else {
531			end := min(m.scroll+visible, len(lines))
532			for _, ln := range lines[m.scroll:end] {
533				b.WriteString(ln + "\n")
534			}
535		}
536		b.WriteString("\n")
537	
538		if m.err != "" {
539			b.WriteString("  " + StyleError.Render("⚠ "+m.err) + "\n")
540		}
541	
542		// Input row.
543		prefix := "  "
544		if m.focusInput {
545			prefix = "▸ "
546		}
547		b.WriteString(prefix + m.input.View() + "\n")
548	
549		help := "Tab focus scrollback · Enter send · Esc back"
550		if !m.focusInput {
551			help = "↑/↓ j/k scroll · g/G top/end · Tab focus input · Esc/←/h back · Q quit"
552		}
553		b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleHelp.Render(help))
554		return b.String()
555	}
556	
557	// renderLines flattens the message list to display lines so scroll math is
558	// straightforward. Each message contributes a header line + body lines + a
559	// blank separator. Body wrapping is left to the terminal (240-char cap on
560	// compose keeps it short in practice).
561	func (m wbThreadModel) renderLines() []string {
562		out := make([]string, 0, len(m.items)*3)
563		viewerID := int64(0)
564		if m.deps.User != nil {
565			viewerID = m.deps.User.ID
566		}
567		for _, w := range m.items {
568			var sender string
569			if w.FromUserID == viewerID {
570				sender = StyleDim.Render("you")
571			} else {
572				// Always use the current cp handle, not w.FromUserIDStr (snapshot).
573				sender = StyleHighlight.Render(m.cpUserID)
574			}
575			out = append(out, "  "+sender+"  "+StyleDim.Render(w.CreatedAt.Format("2006-01-02 15:04")))
576			out = append(out, "    "+w.Body)
577			out = append(out, "")
578		}
579		return out
580	}
581
```

1	package store
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"database/sql"
6		"errors"
7		"time"
8	)
9	
10	type Mail struct {
11		ID            int64
12		ThreadID      int64
13		ParentID      sql.NullInt64
14		FromUserID    int64
15		FromUserIDStr string
16		ToUserID      int64
17		Subject       string
18		Body          string
19		ReadAt        sql.NullTime
20		CreatedAt     time.Time
21	}
22	
23	type MailRepo struct{ s *Store }
24	
25	var ErrMailNotFound = errors.New("mail not found")
26	
27	const mailColumns = `id, thread_id, parent_id, from_user_id, from_userid, to_user_id, subject, body, read_at, created_at`
28	
29	func scanMail(row interface{ Scan(...any) error }) (*Mail, error) {
30		m := &Mail{}
31		if err := row.Scan(
32			&m.ID, &m.ThreadID, &m.ParentID, &m.FromUserID, &m.FromUserIDStr,
33			&m.ToUserID, &m.Subject, &m.Body, &m.ReadAt, &m.CreatedAt,
34		); err != nil {
35			return nil, err
36		}
37		return m, nil
38	}
39	
40	// Insert creates a new mail. If parentID is nil, this is a thread root and
41	// thread_id is back-filled to the new row's own id under writeMu so the
42	// invariant `thread_id IS NOT NULL` holds for every read.
43	func (r *MailRepo) Insert(ctx context.Context, fromID int64, fromUserID string, toID int64, subject, body string, parentID *int64) (*Mail, error) {
44		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
45		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
46	
47		var threadID sql.NullInt64
48		var parent sql.NullInt64
49		if parentID != nil {
50			parent = sql.NullInt64{Int64: *parentID, Valid: true}
51			// Look up the parent's thread_id so the new reply joins the same thread.
52			row := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, `SELECT thread_id FROM mail WHERE id = ?`, *parentID)
53			var t sql.NullInt64
54			if err := row.Scan(&t); err != nil {
55				if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
56					return nil, ErrMailNotFound
57				}
58				return nil, err
59			}
60			threadID = t
61		}
62	
63		res, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
64			`INSERT INTO mail (thread_id, parent_id, from_user_id, from_userid, to_user_id, subject, body) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)`,
65			threadID, parent, fromID, fromUserID, toID, subject, body,
66		)
67		if err != nil {
68			return nil, err
69		}
70		id, err := res.LastInsertId()
71		if err != nil {
72			return nil, err
73		}
74		if !threadID.Valid {
75			// Root mail — thread_id = id.
76			if _, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx, `UPDATE mail SET thread_id = ? WHERE id = ?`, id, id); err != nil {
77				return nil, err
78			}
79		}
80		row := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, `SELECT `+mailColumns+` FROM mail WHERE id = ?`, id)
81		return scanMail(row)
82	}
83	
84	// ListInboxFor returns the recipient's inbox: unread first, then read,
85	// each group ordered newest-first. Mirrors WaterBalloonRepo.ListInboxFor.
86	func (r *MailRepo) ListInboxFor(ctx context.Context, toUserID int64, limit int) ([]*Mail, error) {
87		if limit <= 0 {
88			limit = 50

## Survey Report: User Model & Settings Infrastructure

### 1. User Schema

**Current `users` table columns** (`internal/store/migrations/0001_init.sql:9-21`):
- `id` (INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT)
- `user_id` (TEXT UNIQUE COLLATE NOCASE)
- `password_hash` (TEXT)
- `nickname` (TEXT DEFAULT '')
- `realname` (TEXT DEFAULT '')
- `email` (TEXT DEFAULT '')
- `num_logins` (INTEGER DEFAULT 0)
- `num_posts` (INTEGER DEFAULT 0)
- `last_login_at` (DATETIME)
- `last_host` (TEXT)
- `created_at` (DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP)

**Added in 0004_add_user_roles.sql**:
- `role` (TEXT DEFAULT 'user' CHECK guest|user|mod|admin)
- `must_change_password` (INTEGER DEFAULT 0)

**User struct in Go** (`internal/store/users.go:10-24`):
```
type User struct {
  ID, UserID, PasswordHash, Nickname, RealName, Email
  NumLogins, NumPosts
  LastLoginAt, LastHost
  CreatedAt
  Role, MustChangePassword
}
```

**No existing `bio`, `signature`, or `notification_*` columns anywhere.** You're building from scratch.

**UserRepo public methods** (`internal/store/users.go`):
- `Create()` — insert new user
- `GetByID()`, `GetByUserID()` — read single user
- `NoteLogin()` — increment num_logins, update last_login_at
- `IncrementPosts()` — increment num_posts
- `InsertSystemAccount()` — seed admin/guest with role
- `SetPassword()` — update password_hash AND clear must_change_password in one statement
- `SetRole()` — update role (with validation)
- `ListAll()` — paginated user list
- `CountByRole()` — for admin last-admin guard

### 2. Auth & Password Flow

**Location**: `internal/auth/auth.go`

**Bcrypt configuration**:
- Cost: `bcryptCost = 12` (line 35)

**Password validation**:
- `ValidatePassword(password string) error` (lines 57–62): enforces 6–128 chars
- Used by `Register()` and the password-change screen

**Hash/verify functions**:
- `HashPassword(password string) (string, error)` (lines 66–72): generates bcrypt hash at cost 12
- `VerifyPasswordHash(hash, password string) error` (lines 76–78): calls `bcrypt.CompareHashAndPassword()`

**Password update function**:
- `SetPassword(ctx, id int64, passwordHash string) error` exists in `UserRepo` (lines 129–137): updates both `password_hash` AND clears `must_change_password` in one atomic update
- Callers: `VerifyLogin()` indirectly (updates stats), and the password-change screen directly

**Reserved username constraint preserved**:
- `ReservedUsernameNew = "new"` (line 19) — hard-coded in PasswordAuth
- Used by SSH password callback and makeProgramHandler to dispatch to registration flow
- Validated in `Register()` via `IsReservedUsername()` (lines 27–32)
- You must NOT allow registration with "new", "guest", or "admin"

### 3. Migrations

**Highest-numbered migration**: `0008_articles_comments_mode.sql` (dated 6 May 16:23)

Next migration should be `0009_*.sql` — follow the NNNN format.

### 4. Existing Settings/Profile Screen

**No existing user-self-edit screen.** You're building entirely from scratch.

- `screen_admin_users.go` exists but is admin-only user management, not self-edit

### 5. Session Context & User Propagation

**Auth middleware** (`internal/server/auth_middleware.go`):
- Sets `ctxKeyUserID` (int64) on the SSH session context (line 57)
- Also stores `ctxKeyRemoteHost` and `ctxKeyRegister` (bool for new accounts)

**makeProgramHandler** (`internal/server/server.go:76–150`):
- Reads `ctxKeyUserID` from session context (line 91)
- Falls back to `sess.User() == "guest"` for the none-auth path (line 97)
- Calls `st.Users().GetByID()` to load the *full* user record (line 108)

- No separate `user_id` or `username` field on Root itself
- All screens access current user via `m.deps.User` (pointer to store.User)

**Field on Root for user context**: `deps.User` of type `*store.User` — has `ID` and `UserID` fields.

1	package tui

14	type articleViewModel struct {
15		deps    Deps
16		article *store.Article
17		pushes  []*store.Push
18		width   int
19		height  int
20		scroll  int
21		loadErr error
22	
23		// rendered is the article body after running through glamour. We cache
24		// because (a) glamour's first call loads chroma's syntax highlighter
25		// (~tens of ms) and (b) View() is called on every keystroke. Re-render
26		// only when the article changes or the window width changes.
27		rendered      string
28		renderedWidth int
29	
30		// push input state

32		pushKind store.PushKind
33		pushIn   textinput.Model
34		err      string

36		// delete state. pendingDelete: showing the y/N confirm overlay.
37		// pushCursor: -1 = no push selected (D targets article); 0..len-1 = D targets that push.
38		pendingDelete bool
39		pushCursor    int
40	
41		// pickingCommentsMode: showing the 1/2/3 picker overlay for the
42		// mod-only M shortcut (open / arrows-only / locked).
43		pickingCommentsMode bool
44	}
45	
46	// renderBody runs the article body through glamour with word-wrap set
47	// to the current viewport.
48	func (m *articleViewModel) renderBody() {
49		if m.article == nil {
50			m.rendered = ""
51			m.renderedWidth = 0
52			return
53		}
54		width := m.width - 4
55		if width < 40 {
56			width = glamourFallbackWidth
57		}
58		m.rendered = renderMarkdown(m.article.Body, width)
59		m.renderedWidth = width
60	}
61	
62	func newArticleViewModel(deps Deps, articleID int64) articleViewModel {
63		ctx := context.Background()
64		a, err := deps.Store.Articles().GetByID(ctx, articleID)
65		if err != nil {
66			return articleViewModel{deps: deps, loadErr: err}
67		}
68		pushes, err := deps.Store.Pushes().ListByArticle(ctx, articleID)
69	
70		ti := textinput.New()
71		ti.Placeholder = "comment…"
72		ti.CharLimit = 80
73		ti.Width = 60
74	
75		m := articleViewModel{deps: deps, article: a, pushes: pushes, loadErr: err, pushIn: ti, pushCursor: -1}
76		// Render with the fallback width so the body is readable even before
77		// the first WindowSizeMsg arrives — Update re-renders on resize.
78		m.renderBody()
79		return m
80	}
81	
82	// canDeleteArticle returns true if the current user may delete the loaded
83	// article (author, or mod-or-above). Used both as a permission gate at
84	// the key handler and to decide whether to render the help-line hint.
85	func (m articleViewModel) canDeleteArticle() bool {
86		if m.deps.User == nil || m.article == nil {
87			return false
88		}
89		if m.deps.User.ID == m.article.AuthorID {
90			return true
91		}
92		return m.deps.User.Role.AtLeast(store.RoleMod)
93	}
94	
95	// canDeletePush mirrors canDeleteArticle for the push at index idx.
96	func (m articleViewModel) canDeletePush(idx int) bool {
97		if m.deps.User == nil || idx < 0 || idx >= len(m.pushes) {
98			return false
99		}
100		if m.deps.User.ID == m.pushes[idx].UserID {
101			return true
102		}
103		return m.deps.User.Role.AtLeast(store.RoleMod)
104	}
105	
106	// canPush returns true if the current user is allowed to add new pushes.
107	// Guests can read but not write.
108	func (m articleViewModel) canPush() bool {
109		return m.deps.User != nil && m.deps.User.Role != store.RoleGuest
110	}
111	
112	// canEditArticle mirrors canDeleteArticle: the author may edit their own
113	// post, mods and admins may edit anyone's.
114	func (m articleViewModel) canEditArticle() bool {
115		if m.deps.User == nil || m.article == nil {
116			return false
117		}
118		if m.deps.User.ID == m.article.AuthorID {
119			return true
120		}
121		return m.deps.User.Role.AtLeast(store.RoleMod)
122	}

124	// canSetCommentsMode gates the M shortcut. Mirrors board-view canPin —
125	// pinning and locking comments are both moderation actions that do NOT
126	// admit the article author. Mod+ only.
127	func (m articleViewModel) canSetCommentsMode() bool {
128		return m.deps.User != nil && m.deps.User.Role.AtLeast(store.RoleMod)
129	}

131	func (m articleViewModel) Init() tea.Cmd { return nil }
132	
133	func (m articleViewModel) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
134		switch msg := msg.(type) {
135		case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
136			m.width, m.height = msg.Width, msg.Height
137			// Only re-render when the width target actually changes — height
138			// changes don't affect glamour wrap.
139			newWidth := max(msg.Width-4, 40)
140			if newWidth != m.renderedWidth {
141				m.renderBody()
142			}
143			return m, nil
144	
145		case PushAddedMsg:
146			if m.article != nil && msg.ArticleID == m.article.ID {
147				ctx := context.Background()
148				// Re-fetch from DB so timestamps and recommend_score reflect
149				// the canonical state, not the just-synthesized broker payload.
150				if pushes, err := m.deps.Store.Pushes().ListByArticle(ctx, m.article.ID); err == nil {
151					m.pushes = pushes
152				}
153				if a, err := m.deps.Store.Articles().GetByID(ctx, m.article.ID); err == nil {
154					m.article = a
155				}
156			}
157	
158		case ArticleUpdatedMsg:
159			if m.article != nil && msg.ArticleID == m.article.ID {
160				// Edit happened in another session — refetch the canonical
161				// title/body so we render the new text.
162				if a, err := m.deps.Store.Articles().GetByID(context.Background(), m.article.ID); err == nil {
163					m.article = a
164					m.renderBody()
165					// Reset scroll if the new body is shorter than where we
166					// were parked.
167					if m.scroll > m.bodyLineCount() {
168						m.scroll = 0
169					}
170				}
171			}
172			return m, nil
173	
174		case ArticleCommentsModeChangedMsg:
175			if m.article != nil && msg.ArticleID == m.article.ID {
176				// Mode flip happened in another session — refetch so the badge
177				// and the +/-/= gates pick up the new value. No body re-render
178				// needed (only the header changes).
179				if a, err := m.deps.Store.Articles().GetByID(context.Background(), m.article.ID); err == nil {
180					m.article = a
181				}
182			}
183			return m, nil
184	
185		case tea.KeyMsg:
186			if m.pushing {
187				return m.updatePushInput(msg)
188			}
189			if m.pendingDelete {
190				return m.updateDeleteConfirm(msg)
191			}
192			if m.pickingCommentsMode {
193				return m.updateCommentsModePicker(msg)
194			}
195			switch msg.String() {
196			case "esc", "backspace", "left", "h":
197				boardID := int64(0)
198				if m.article != nil {
199					boardID = m.article.BoardID
200				}

283	func recipientName(deps Deps, userID int64) string {
284		if deps.Store == nil {
285			return fmt.Sprintf("uid=%d", userID)
286		}
287		u, err := deps.Store.Users().GetByID(context.Background(), userID)
288		if err != nil || u == nil {

290		}
291		return u.UserID

317	func newMailComposeModel(deps Deps, recipient string, parentID int64) mailComposeModel {

324		subj := textinput.New()

326		subj.CharLimit = 64
327		subj.Width = 60
328

331		body.CharLimit = 4000
332		body.SetWidth(60)
333		body.SetHeight(8)
334	
335		// On reply, default the subject from the parent (with "Re: " prefix
336		// once and only once) and pre-fill the body with the parent quoted in
337		// markdown blockquote (`> `) form so the reply reads like an email
338		// thread. Cursor lands after the trailing blank line.
339		if parentID != 0 && deps.Store != nil {
340			if parent, err := deps.Store.Mail().GetByID(context.Background(), parentID); err == nil {
341				s := parent.Subject
342				if !strings.HasPrefix(strings.ToLower(s), "re:") {
343					s = "Re: " + s
344				}
345				subj.SetValue(s)
346				body.SetValue(quoteForReply(parent))
347			}
348		}
349	
350		m := mailComposeModel{
351			deps:     deps,
352			parentID: parentID,
353			to:       to,
354			subject:  subj,
355			body:     body,
356		}
357		switch {
358		case recipient == "":
359			m.focus = mailFocusTo
360			m.to.Focus()
361		case subj.Value() == "":
362			m.focus = mailFocusSubject
363			m.subject.Focus()
364		default:
365			m.focus = mailFocusBody
366			m.body.Focus()
367		}
368		return m
369	}

Perfect! I have enough information. Let me compile the report:

## Screen-Routing Pattern Report

**1. Screen Registry** — `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/tui/messages.go:6-25`

All `Screen*` constants (iota-based enum):
```
ScreenMainMenu, ScreenBoardList, ScreenBoardView, ScreenArticleView, 
ScreenPostCompose, ScreenWBInbox, ScreenWBCompose, ScreenWBThread, 
ScreenOnline, ScreenMailInbox, ScreenMailThread, ScreenMailCompose, 
ScreenPasswordChange, ScreenAdminUsers, ScreenArticleEdit, ScreenArticleExport, 
ScreenBoardSplash, ScreenBoardBannerEdit
```

**NavigateMsg struct** (`messages.go:27-37`): Sub-models emit `NavigateMsg{To: Screen*}` with optional context fields: `BoardID`, `ArticleID`, `Recipient`, `MailID`, `MailThreadID`, `CounterpartyUserID`. Root's Update() handler catches NavigateMsg and invokes `navigate()`.

---

**2. Root Navigate Switch** — `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/tui/root.go:182-243`

The switch statement maps each `Screen*` to a constructor (`newXxxModel`). Constructor signature pattern:
- **Deps struct** (`root.go:16-22`): All constructors receive `Deps{Store, Broker, User, IsRegister, MustChangePassword}`.
- **Context-specific params**: e.g., `newBoardViewModel(deps, boardID)`, `newWBComposeModel(deps, recipient)`, `newMailComposeModel(deps, recipient, mailID)`.

After construction, `navigate()` calls `sub.Init()`, forwards the current `WindowSizeMsg` if available, and batches commands.

---

**3. Form-Screen Pattern** — Explored in `screen_register.go`, `screen_post_compose.go`, `screen_wb.go:148-230`:

**Focus management**: 
- Integer `focus` field tracks active input (iota constants: `regFieldUserID`, `composeFocusTitle`, `wbFocusTo`, etc.).
- `advance()` or `toggleFocus()` cycles focus with wraparound; calls `Focus()/Blur()` on corresponding textinput/textarea models.
- **Tab/Shift+Tab** and **Up/Down** advance focus; **Enter** in non-terminal fields advances rather than submitting.

**Text fields**: `textinput.Model` with width, placeholder, charlimit set in constructor.
**Text area**: `textarea.Model` with SetWidth/SetHeight in Update() for responsive layout.
**Submit**: Ctrl+S triggers `submit()` method (context write to store), on error sets `m.err`, on success navigates away or sets `m.sent` flag.

Key pattern (e.g., `screen_post_compose.go:65-91`): If focused on textinput, Enter advances focus; if on textarea, all keys fall through to textarea.Update(). Form screens also handle Esc → navigate back.

---

**4. Main Menu Entry Point** — `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/tui/screen_main_menu.go:24-80`

`mainMenuModel` holds `items []menuItem` (each with label, hint, target Screen). In Update(), **↑/↓ j/k** move cursor, **Enter/→/l** or **1-9** shortcuts trigger navigation. Admin role inserts an extra "管理 Admin" entry before Quit. Menu is the Root's default screen for authenticated users.

---

**5. Help Screen Integration** — `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/tui/help.go:10-42, 179-203`

Each screen contributes keybindings via `screenHelp` map (Screen → []helpSection). Example (`help.go:34-41`): ScreenMainMenu lists ↑/↓ j/k, Enter/→/l, 1-9 numeric, q/Esc. 

**Form screens are intentionally absent** from screenHelp (`help.go:29-32`) so `?` passes through to textinput as a literal character. Function `isFormScreen()` gates availability; Root blocks help overlay on form screens + register + password-change flows.

---

**6. Runewidth Helpers** — `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/tui/runewidth.go:11-56`

Four core functions: **PadRight/PadLeft** (pad/truncate to display width), **Truncate** (cut to width, append "…"), **Width** (return display width). All use `github.com/mattn/go-runewidth` internally. Also provided: `bannerTruncate()` and `bannerVisualWidth()` for CSI-escape-aware width calculations (article banners with color codes). Use PadRight/PadLeft/Width for list rendering with CJK.

1	# CLAUDE.md
2	
3	This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.
4	
5	## What this is
6	
7	A simplified PTT-style BBS (批踢踢 clone) served over SSH. Built with
8	`charmbracelet/wish` (SSH framework) + `bubbletea` (TUI), persisted in
9	SQLite via the pure-Go `modernc.org/sqlite` driver. **Vim-style navigation
10	is a deliberate product decision** — `h`/`j`/`k`/`l` work alongside arrow
11	keys and Esc/Enter on every list/menu screen. Form screens (register,
12	post compose, water-balloon compose) intentionally do *not* bind h/l so
13	they remain usable as text-edit keys.
14	
15	For the why-we-do-it-this-way notes mapping back to pttbbs internals, see
16	`docs/ptt_trace_code/`. For the testing strategy, see `docs/testing.md`.
17	
18	## Common commands
19	
20	```bash
21	make hostkey            # generate .ssh/host_ed25519 (run once before first run)
22	make run                # boots :2222, creates data/bbs.db with seed boards
23	make watch              # auto-rebuild on .go/.sql changes; SIGINT-then-restart via `air`
24	make build              # produces ./sshbbs
25	make test               # plain go test
26	make test-race          # what we treat as the CI standard
27	make cover              # per-package coverage
28	make db-reset           # rm data/bbs.db*
29	
30	# focused testing
31	go test -run TestPushes_ConcurrentScoreAtomicity -race -v ./internal/store/...
32	go test -race ./internal/chat/...
33	
34	# manual two-user smoke
35	ssh new@localhost -p 2222     # password ignored — fills the in-TUI register form
36	ssh alice@localhost -p 2222   # use the password set during register
37	```
38	
39	If `make hostkey` is re-run, clients see `Host key verification failed`.
40	Fix: `ssh-keygen -R '[localhost]:2222'`.
41	
42	## Architecture — the non-obvious parts
43	
44	### SSH-user-as-login, with `new` as the register sentinel
45	`auth.ReservedUsernameNew = "new"`. The password-auth callback in
46	`internal/server/auth_middleware.go` accepts any password for SSH user
47	`new` and stashes a "register mode" flag in `ssh.Context`; the TUI
48	handler reads it and routes straight to the register screen. For any
49	other SSH username, `auth.VerifyLogin` runs bcrypt against the DB and
50	stashes the `user_id` (int64) in the context for `makeProgramHandler`
51	to load.
52	
53	This means `auth.Register` rejects the literal username `new` — that
54	constant is referenced in two places (auth + middleware) and they must
55	not drift. `auth.go` defines it once.
56	
57	### Wish uses `MiddlewareWithProgramHandler`, not `Middleware`
58	We pick the *ProgramHandler* variant deliberately
59	(`internal/server/server.go`) because we need the `*tea.Program` pointer
60	to register the session with `chat.Broker` and to `program.Send(tea.Quit())`
61	during graceful shutdown. The simpler `Middleware(handler)` form constructs
62	the program internally and hides the pointer — you can't broadcast
63	into it. Don't switch back.
64	
65	### The chat broker takes a `Sender` interface, not `*tea.Program`
66	`chat.Sender` is `interface { Send(tea.Msg) }`. `*tea.Program` satisfies
67	it naturally. The interface exists purely so unit tests can substitute
68	a recording fake (`fakeSender` in `broker_test.go`) without spinning up
69	a real bubbletea program. If you find yourself wanting to mock the
70	broker, you probably want to add a method here, not refactor.
71	
72	### Live broadcasts are "send to everyone, filter on receive"
73	For 推文 (push) updates, `screen_article_view.updatePushInput` calls
74	`broker.SendToAll(senderUID, PushAddedMsg{ArticleID, ...})`. Every
75	connected session receives the message; each article-view model
76	ignores it unless `msg.ArticleID == m.article.ID`. This is simpler than
77	tracking who's viewing what and good enough for our scale. When the
78	filter matches, we **re-fetch from DB** rather than trust the broker
79	payload — that way timestamps and `recommend_score` are always canonical.
80	
81	Water balloons (`broker.Send(toUID, ...)`) target a single user; the
82	multi-session "雙開" case fans out to every live `Session` of that user.
83	
84	### `Store.writeMu` is process-level, not row-level
85	`internal/store/store.go` exposes a single `sync.Mutex` that's grabbed
86	inside every multi-statement repo method (e.g. `PushRepo.Create` runs
87	`INSERT INTO pushes` and `UPDATE articles SET recommend_score` in one
88	transaction under the mutex). This keeps the cached score atomic from
89	the application's perspective and avoids `SQLITE_BUSY` flapping with
90	the pure-Go driver. Don't use `sql.Tx` ad-hoc inside repo methods
91	without also taking `writeMu`. The `pushes` test
92	(`TestPushes_ConcurrentScoreAtomicity`, 50 concurrent inserts) is the
93	canary — if it ever fails under `-race`, the mutex discipline broke.
94	
95	### Migrations live under the store package, not the repo root
96	`internal/store/migrations/*.sql` is embedded with `go:embed
97	migrations/*.sql` from `internal/store/migrate.go`. `go:embed` can't
98	ascend with `..`, so the SQL files live next to the Go code that loads
99	them. Filename format `NNNN_name.sql`; `migrate.apply()` parses the
100	prefix as the version and tracks applied versions in the
101	`schema_migrations` table.
102	
103	### Data deliberately diverges from pttbbs in three places
104	1. **No recno-based article addressing** — `articles.id` (auto-increment)
105	   is opaque and never re-used. Bookmarks stay valid forever.
106	2. **bcrypt cost-12, not `crypt(3)` DES** — `auth.bcryptCost`.
107	3. **No binary shared memory / `userinfo_t` arrays** — presence is a
108	   `sync.RWMutex`-guarded `map[int64][]*Session` in `chat.Broker`.
109	
110	The full mapping (userec_t / boardheader_t / fileheader_t / `.DIR` /
111	推文 / 水球 / userinfo_t) is in `docs/ptt_trace_code/01..06_*.md`.
112	
113	## Testing layers (see `docs/testing.md` for the full doc)
114	
115	- **Layer 1**: plain `go test` for `auth` / `store` / `chat`. Most coverage lives here.
116	- **Layer 2**: direct `tea.Model.Update()` tests in `internal/tui/`. Use
117	  `package tui` (internal), not `tui_test`, to reach unexported screen
118	  constructors. Keybinding-parity tests are table-driven over
119	  `[]string{"esc","backspace","left","h"}` etc. — when adding new keys,
120	  extend the table.
121	- **No `teatest` dependency.** It's experimental (`x/exp`); we test
122	  `Update()` directly with `tea.KeyMsg{...}` synthetic input.
123	- **Always run `-race`.** Several tests (`TestPushes_ConcurrentScoreAtomicity`,
124	  `TestBroker_Concurrency`) exist specifically as race-detector bait.
125	- **Any test needing a DB** uses `storetest.New(t)` which gives a
126	  fresh `t.TempDir()`-backed SQLite. Never share stores across tests.
127	
128	## Conventions worth preserving
129	
130	- **UTF-8 throughout, no Big5.** Use `internal/tui/runewidth.go` helpers
131	  (`PadRight`, `PadLeft`, `Truncate`, `Width`) for any list rendering
132	  that mixes ASCII and CJK — `lipgloss`'s automatic width is unreliable
133	  for double-width glyphs.
134	- **Form screens skip h/l navigation** so the keys remain available for
135	  text editing. Currently: `screen_register`, `screen_post_compose`,
136	  `screen_wb` (compose half).
137	- **NavigateMsg is the only legitimate way to swap screens.** Sub-models
138	  emit it from `Update`; `Root.navigate` is the single switch statement
139	  that constructs the new sub. When you add a new screen, add the case
140	  there and a `Screen*` constant in `messages.go`.
141	- **Window-resize forwarding**: `Root.Update` always forwards
142	  `tea.WindowSizeMsg` to the active sub before its own logic. When
143	  switching to a new sub, `Root.navigate` resends the last known size
144	  so the freshly-mounted screen lays out correctly on first render.
145	
146	<!-- project-knowledge-harness:agent-guidance -->
147	<!-- Snippet for the project's agent contract file (AGENTS.md / CLAUDE.md /
148	     similar). The bundled scripts/init.sh appends this between sentinel
149	     markers; safe to re-run. -->
150	
151	### Long-term backlog → `TODO.md` + `backlog/`
152	
153	When the user surfaces an idea explicitly **not** being implemented this
154	session (signals: "maybe later", "nice to have", "if I'm interested",
155	"工程量太大需要再評估", "先記下來"), add an entry to [`TODO.md`](TODO.md) using
156	the priority + effort tag schema. Do **not** create new `ROADMAP.md` /
157	`IDEAS.md` / `BACKLOG.md` files — `TODO.md` is the single index.
158	
159	The bundled `scripts/todo-kanban.sh` validates the format. Run it
160	(`scripts/todo-kanban.sh --validate-only TODO.md`) after editing so syntax
161	drift is caught immediately.
162	
163	#### Three ways to add a TODO entry (preferred order)
164	
165	1. **Structured CLI — `scripts/add-todo.sh`** (default):
166	
167	   ```
168	   scripts/add-todo.sh --priority P3 --effort M \
169	     --title "Title" --description "Description"
170	   ```
171	
172	   Inserts a canonically-formatted line into the right `## P*` lane and
173	   re-runs the validator. Add `--backlog` to also scaffold
174	   `backlog/<slug>.md` from the bundled template.
175	
176	2. **Quick capture — `backlog/inbox.md`** (when priority/effort unclear):
177	
178	   ```
179	   echo "- maybe add docs versioning with mike" >> backlog/inbox.md
180	   ```
181	
182	   When the user asks "sweep the inbox", run
183	   `scripts/sweep-inbox.sh`. It prompts for the missing fields per loose
184	   line and calls `add-todo.sh`. Use `--batch` for non-interactive runs
185	   that only formalize lines with parseable `key=value` pairs.
186	
187	3. **Direct edit of `TODO.md`** — fine if the format is fresh; run
188	   `scripts/todo-kanban.sh --validate-only` afterwards.
189	
190	Add a `backlog/<slug>.md` companion doc when the item meets any of:
191	
192	- carries a `P?` tag (record what was tried so it doesn't need re-investigation)
193	- captures a paused troubleshooting session that you intend to fix later
194	  (preserve the error trace + root cause analysis before context evaporates)
195	- weighs multiple options (record trade-offs, not only the winner)
196	- is `[L]` or `[XL]` (architectural; needs design before code)
197	
198	`[S]` items rarely need a backlog doc — a file path in the `TODO.md` line is
199	usually enough. See [`backlog/README.md`](backlog/README.md) for the full
200	template and "when to add a doc" rules.
201	
202	When implementing a `TODO.md` item, in the same commit:
203	
204	1. Run `scripts/promote-todo.sh --title "<substring>" --summary "<what shipped>"`
205	   to move the entry into `## Done` with the dated syntax and re-validate.
206	2. Mark the corresponding `backlog/<slug>.md` (if any) `Status: shipped`
207	   and keep it as a historical record (don't delete — future-you may
208	   revisit adjacent decisions).
209	
210	`backlog/` is excluded from N/A (no packaging — these files stay in the repo) (see N/A); it
211	is repo metadata for maintainers, not user-facing config to deploy.
212	
213	### Past pitfalls → `pitfalls/`
214	
215	When you spend more than ~15 minutes debugging something that wasn't
216	googleable and the fix is non-obvious, write a `pitfalls/<slug>.md`
217	capturing:
218	
219	1. **Verbatim symptom** — copy-paste error messages exactly, do not
220	   paraphrase (preserves grep-ability for future-you / future agent)
221	2. **Root cause** — why this happens (with source / docs / upstream issue link)
222	3. **Workaround** — copy-pasteable commands or config diff
223	4. **Prevention** — how to avoid stepping on this again
224	
225	Title the doc by the **symptom**, not the root cause (you'll search by what
226	you're seeing, not by what you eventually learned). See
227	[`pitfalls/README.md`](pitfalls/README.md) for the full template and
228	when-to-add rules.
229	
230	**Pitfall vs Hard invariant**: a pitfall *graduates* to a Hard invariant in
231	this file when it (a) recurs across machines/agents/sessions despite being
232	documented, (b) silently corrupts state, or (c) the workaround is non-obvious
233	enough that "remember to do X" isn't safe. When graduating, leave the
234	`pitfalls/<slug>.md` as historical record and link to it from the new
235	invariant.
236	
237	`pitfalls/` is excluded from N/A (no packaging — these files stay in the repo) (see N/A) and
238	**not** auto-redacted; review for secrets before committing.
239	<!-- project-knowledge-harness:agent-guidance --> (end)

70		if limit <= 0 {

72		}
73		// Pinned articles surface first ((pinned_at IS NULL) sorts 0 before 1);

509			if a, err := m.deps.Store.Articles().GetByID(ctx, m.article.ID); err == nil {

512			m.pushing = false

520					Body:       body,
521				})
522			}
523			return m, nil

526		m.pushIn, cmd = m.pushIn.Update(msg)
527		return m, cmd

Good - this confirms articles do NOT have reply functionality (only new articles). Now let me compile my findings:

## Summary

Based on my thorough exploration of the codebase, here are the key findings for your notification system:

### 1. **Push System** — Article Commentary
`PushRepo.Create` (`/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/store/pushes.go:107-169`) inserts a push and atomically updates the article's `recommend_score` under `writeMu` + transaction. The **notification trigger** is at `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/tui/screen_article_view.go:516`:

```go
m.deps.Broker.SendToAll(u.ID, PushAddedMsg{
    ArticleID:  m.article.ID,
    UserUserID: u.UserID,
    Kind:       string(m.pushKind),
    Body:       body,
})
```

**To get the article author's user_id:** The article object is available as `m.article` (type `*store.Article`), which has `AuthorID` (int64) and `AuthorUserID` (string) fields. Query it from the Article struct before broadcasting the notification.

### 2. **Water Balloon Flow**
Trigger point: `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/tui/screen_wb.go:271` (compose) and `:486` (thread):

```go
delivered := m.deps.Broker.Send(target.ID, WBIncomingMsg{
    ID:         wb.ID,
    FromUserID: from.UserID,
    Body:       body,
})
```

**Self-WB constraint handled:** Lines 263-269 (compose) and 480-484 (thread) skip `Broker.Send` for self-WB and mark it read immediately — you won't notify on these.

### 3. **Mail Flow**
Mail send trigger: `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/tui/screen_mail.go:483`:

```go
m.deps.Broker.Send(target.ID, MailIncomingMsg{
    ID:         mail.ID,
    ThreadID:   mail.ThreadID,
    FromUserID: from.UserID,
    Subject:    subj,
})
```

**Self-mail constraint handled:** Lines 472-479 guard self-mail (memo), skip Broker.Send, and mark read immediately.

### 4. **Article Reply ("Re:")**
**No, articles do NOT have email-style replies.** The codebase has:
- `screen_post_compose.go` (`ScreenPostCompose`) — creates entirely new articles with title + body; no reply/quote mechanism
- `screen_mail.go` (`ScreenMailCompose`) — has "Re: " prefixing and blockquote quoting (lines 335–346: `quoteForReply` + "Re: " logic)

The mail reply exists; article reply does not exist.

### 5. **Broker Contract**
From `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/chat/broker.go`:

Public methods:
- `Register(s *Session)` — add a session
- `Unregister(userID int64, prog Sender)` — remove a session
- `Send(toUID int64, msg tea.Msg) bool` — targeted delivery; returns true if online
- `SendToAll(excludeUID int64, msg tea.Msg)` — broadcast to all (optionally skip one user)
- `LookupByUserID(name string) int64` — resolve handle to user_id (case-insensitive)
- `OnlineList() []OnlineUser` — get who's online
- `SessionsSnapshot() []*Session` — get all session pointers for graceful shutdown

### 6. **HTTP/Outbound Calls**
Zero `net/http` imports across the entire codebase. This is the **first outbound HTTP dependency** — you're introducing apprise webhook calls fresh.

```text
# apprise-go: Library Overview

## What Is It?

apprise-go is **a pure-Go reimplementation** of the original Python Apprise project. The README states it "aims to provide the same powerful notification capabilities in a standalone, compiled binary with no runtime dependencies."

It is **not** an HTTP client for a separate Apprise API server.

## Runtime Dependencies

**None.** The project explicitly emphasizes "No Python runtime required" and provides standalone binaries for multiple platforms (macOS, Linux, Windows, FreeBSD).

## Public Go API Surface

The README does not document the Go API surface (constructors, methods like Add/AddAll/Notify/Send). It only covers **CLI usage**, showing command-line examples like:

```bash
apprise -b "Hello from apprise!" "discord://webhook_id/webhook_token"
```

## Minimal Usage Example

The README provides no programmatic Go usage example—only CLI examples. To use it as a library, you would need to consult the source code in the `internal/` and `cmd/apprise/` directories, which aren't documented here.

## Key Details

- **77 GitHub stars**, 4 forks
- Latest release: v0.1.11 (Feb 1, 2026)
- Supports 100+ notification services
- Licensed under BSD 2-Clause
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="generic" data-tool-name="AskUserQuestion"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **AskUserQuestion**</summary>
**Notification 派送的執行方式要怎麼挑？**

Options:
- **Generic webhook POST (Recommended)** - User 填一個或多個 HTTP(S) URL，BBS 直接 POST JSON。要用 apprise 服務的人自己跑 caronc/apprise-api 然後填它的 /notify/<key> URL。零新依賴、最穩。
- **Vendor unraid/apprise-go library** - 直接 import Go 函式庫，原生支援 apprise:// 格式（discord://, telegram:// 等）。但 lib 在 v0.1.x alpha、Go API 無文件，要讀原始碼;未來升級可能 break。
- **Shell out to apprise binary** - User 自己裝 apprise CLI (Python 或 unraid 那顆 Go binary)，BBS 用 exec.Command 呼叫。原生支援 apprise:// 但部署多一個 binary 依賴。

**「文章回文」(類似 Email Re: 引文)要不要納入這次 scope？**

Options:
- **納入 (Recommended)** - 新增 article reply 功能 (R 鍵在文章 view 觸發)，沿用現有 mail 的 quoteForReply 邏輯。然後 push/reply 通知都能在這個 PR 一次到位。
- **不納入，只做 push 通知** - 目前文章只有「推/噓/箭頭」沒有 reply 整篇。先做 push 通知就好，reply 之後另開 issue。
- **另記到 TODO.md** - 這次完全不碰 article reply，依專案規範寫進 TODO.md (P? + 工程量 tag) + backlog/ 文件。

**通知系統要支援哪些事件 (user 可在設定頁勾選)？** _(select multiple)_

Options:
- **有人推/噓我的文章** - PushAddedMsg 觸發，notify 文章作者。
- **收到水球 (WB)** - Broker.Send WBIncomingMsg 觸發。self-WB 不通知。
- **收到站內信 (Mail)** - MailIncomingMsg 觸發。self-mail (memo) 不通知。
- **有人回文 (Re:)** - 只在「Article reply」題選『納入』時才會生效。


**Answer:** Generic webhook POST (Recommended), 納入 (Recommended), 有人推/噓我的文章, 收到水球 (WB), 收到站內信 (Mail), 有人回文 (Re:)
</details></tool-use>

1	package tui
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"strings"

12	
13	// Form-style screen routed from Root when deps.MustChangePassword is set.
14	// Like screen_register, it deliberately does NOT bind h/l so those keys
15	// remain available for text editing inside the password fields.
16	
17	const (
18		pwFieldCurrent = iota
19		pwFieldNew
20		pwFieldConfirm
21		pwFieldCount
22	)
23	
24	type passwordChangeModel struct {
25		deps    Deps
26		inputs  []textinput.Model
27		focus   int
28		err     string
29		success bool
30	}
31	
32	func newPasswordChangeModel(deps Deps) passwordChangeModel {
33		inputs := make([]textinput.Model, pwFieldCount)
34		for i := range inputs {
35			ti := textinput.New()
36			ti.EchoMode = textinput.EchoPassword
37			ti.EchoCharacter = '•'
38			ti.CharLimit = 128
39			ti.Width = 32
40			inputs[i] = ti
41		}
42		inputs[pwFieldCurrent].Placeholder = "目前密碼"
43		inputs[pwFieldNew].Placeholder = "新密碼 (≥ 6)"
44		inputs[pwFieldConfirm].Placeholder = "再次輸入新密碼"
45		inputs[pwFieldCurrent].Focus()
46		return passwordChangeModel{deps: deps, inputs: inputs}
47	}
48	
49	func (m passwordChangeModel) Init() tea.Cmd { return textinput.Blink }
50	
51	func (m passwordChangeModel) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
52		switch msg := msg.(type) {
53		case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
54			return m, nil
55	
56		case tea.KeyMsg:
57			if m.success {
58				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenMainMenu} }
59			}
60			switch msg.String() {
61			case "esc":
62				// Refusing to set a new password is allowed but ends the session;
63				// the must_change flag is still set, so reconnecting just lands
64				// back on this screen. Cleaner than letting the user limbo on a
65				// screen they refused.
66				return m, tea.Quit
67			case "tab", "down":
68				m.advance(+1)
69				return m, nil
70			case "shift+tab", "up":
71				m.advance(-1)
72				return m, nil
73			case "enter":
74				if m.focus < len(m.inputs)-1 {
75					m.advance(+1)
76					return m, nil
77				}
78				return m.submit()
79			}
80		}
81	
82		var cmd tea.Cmd
83		m.inputs[m.focus], cmd = m.inputs[m.focus].Update(msg)
84		return m, cmd
85	}
86	
87	func (m *passwordChangeModel) advance(d int) {
88		n := len(m.inputs)
89		m.focus = (m.focus + d + n) % n
90		for i := range m.inputs {
91			if i == m.focus {
92				m.inputs[i].Focus()
93			} else {
94				m.inputs[i].Blur()
95			}
96		}
97	}
98	
99	func (m passwordChangeModel) submit() (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
100		current := m.inputs[pwFieldCurrent].Value()
101		newPw := m.inputs[pwFieldNew].Value()
102		confirm := m.inputs[pwFieldConfirm].Value()
103	
104		if m.deps.User == nil {
105			m.err = "internal error: no user"
106			return m, nil
107		}
108		if err := auth.VerifyPasswordHash(m.deps.User.PasswordHash, current); err != nil {
109			m.err = "目前密碼錯誤"
110			return m, nil
111		}
112		if err := auth.ValidatePassword(newPw); err != nil {
113			m.err = err.Error()
114			return m, nil
115		}
116		if newPw != confirm {
117			m.err = "新密碼與確認不一致"
118			return m, nil
119		}
120		if newPw == current {
121			m.err = "新密碼不可與目前密碼相同"
122			return m, nil
123		}
124	
125		hash, err := auth.HashPassword(newPw)
126		if err != nil {
127			m.err = err.Error()
128			return m, nil
129		}
130		ctx := context.Background()
131		if err := m.deps.Store.Users().SetPassword(ctx, m.deps.User.ID, hash); err != nil {
132			m.err = err.Error()
133			return m, nil
134		}
135		// Refresh User in place so subsequent screens see the cleared flag.
136		if fresh, err := m.deps.Store.Users().GetByID(ctx, m.deps.User.ID); err == nil {
137			*m.deps.User = *fresh
138		}
139		m.success = true
140		return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenMainMenu} }
141	}
142	
143	func (m passwordChangeModel) View() string {
144		var b strings.Builder
145		b.WriteString("\n")
146		b.WriteString(StyleHeader.Render("  === 修改密碼 (首次登入必須修改) ==="))
147		b.WriteString("\n\n")
148	
149		labels := []string{"目前密碼 current", "新密碼 new (≥ 6)", "再次輸入 confirm"}
150		for i, label := range labels {
151			b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render(label))
152			b.WriteString("\n  ")
153			b.WriteString(m.inputs[i].View())
154			b.WriteString("\n\n")
155		}
156	
157		if m.err != "" {
158			b.WriteString("  " + StyleError.Render("⚠ "+m.err) + "\n\n")
159		}
160		if m.success {
161			b.WriteString("  " + StyleSuccess.Render("✓ 已更新，將進入主選單…") + "\n\n")
162		}
163		b.WriteString("  " + StyleHelp.Render("Tab/↓ next · Shift+Tab/↑ prev · Enter submit · Esc disconnect"))
164		b.WriteString("\n")
165		return b.String()
166	}
167

345				subj.SetValue(s)
346				body.SetValue(quoteForReply(parent))
347			}
348		}
349	
350		m := mailComposeModel{
351			deps:     deps,
352			parentID: parentID,
353			to:       to,
354			subject:  subj,
355			body:     body,
356		}
357		switch {
358		case recipient == "":
359			m.focus = mailFocusTo
360			m.to.Focus()
361		case subj.Value() == "":
362			m.focus = mailFocusSubject
363			m.subject.Focus()
364		default:
365			m.focus = mailFocusBody
366			m.body.Focus()
367		}
368		return m
369	}
370	
371	func (m mailComposeModel) Init() tea.Cmd { return textinput.Blink }
372	
373	func (m mailComposeModel) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
374		switch msg := msg.(type) {
375		case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
376			m.width, m.height = msg.Width, msg.Height
377			m.body.SetWidth(max(40, msg.Width-12))
378			return m, nil
379	
380		case tea.KeyMsg:
381			if m.sent != "" {
382				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenMailInbox} }
383			}
384			switch msg.String() {
385			case "esc":
386				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenMailInbox} }
387			case "tab":
388				m.cycleFocus()
389				return m, nil
390			case "ctrl+s":
391				return m.submit()
392			}
393	
394			switch m.focus {
395			case mailFocusTo:
396				if k := msg.String(); k == "enter" {
397					m.cycleFocus()
398					return m, nil
399				}
```

```text
1	package tui
2	
3	// Screen identifies which sub-model the Root should be showing.
4	type Screen int
5	
6	const (
7		ScreenMainMenu Screen = iota
8		ScreenBoardList
9		ScreenBoardView
10		ScreenArticleView
11		ScreenPostCompose
12		ScreenWBInbox
13		ScreenWBCompose
14		ScreenWBThread
15		ScreenOnline
16		ScreenMailInbox
17		ScreenMailThread
18		ScreenMailCompose
19		ScreenPasswordChange
20		ScreenAdminUsers
21		ScreenArticleEdit
22		ScreenArticleExport
23		ScreenBoardSplash
24		ScreenBoardBannerEdit
25	)
26	
27	// NavigateMsg is emitted by sub-screens to ask the Root to swap screens.
28	// Optional fields carry context the destination screen needs.
29	type NavigateMsg struct {
30		To           Screen
31		BoardID      int64
32		ArticleID    int64
33		Recipient          string // for ScreenWBCompose / ScreenMailCompose: prefilled recipient userid
34		MailID             int64  // for ScreenMailCompose: parent mail id when replying
35		MailThreadID       int64  // for ScreenMailThread: which thread to open
36		CounterpartyUserID int64  // for ScreenWBThread: which 1-to-1 thread to open
37	}
38	
39	// ErrorMsg surfaces an error as a transient toast.
40	type ErrorMsg struct{ Err error }
41	
42	// WBIncomingMsg is delivered by the chat broker (or replayed from DB on Init)
43	// to notify the recipient that a new water balloon arrived.
44	type WBIncomingMsg struct {
45		ID         int64
46		FromUserID string
47		Body       string
48	}
49	
50	// PushAddedMsg is broadcast by the chat broker when a push is added to an
51	// article. The article-view model checks ArticleID to decide whether to
52	// re-render.
53	type PushAddedMsg struct {
54		ArticleID  int64
55		UserUserID string
56		Kind       string
57		Body       string
58	}
59	
60	// MailIncomingMsg notifies the recipient that a new mail has arrived.
61	// Unlike WBIncomingMsg it does NOT auto-toast — mail is asynchronous; the
62	// open mail inbox can choose to refresh, and the main menu reads the unread
63	// counter from DB.
64	type MailIncomingMsg struct {
65		ID         int64
66		ThreadID   int64
67		FromUserID string
68		Subject    string
69	}
70	
71	// ArticleAddedMsg is broadcast by the chat broker when a new article is
72	// posted. Board-view models filter by BoardID and re-fetch the article
73	// list from DB so timestamps and ordering reflect canonical state.
74	type ArticleAddedMsg struct {
75		BoardID      int64
76		ArticleID    int64
77		AuthorUserID string
78		Title        string
79	}
80	
81	// ArticleUpdatedMsg is broadcast by the chat broker when an article's
82	// title or body is edited. Article-view models filter by ArticleID and
83	// re-fetch from DB so they don't render stale text.
84	type ArticleUpdatedMsg struct {
85		ArticleID int64
86	}
87	
88	// ArticlePinChangedMsg is broadcast when a mod pins or unpins an article.
89	// Board-view models filter by BoardID and re-fetch the article list so the
90	// pinned-first ordering and the [M] marker stay current across all live
91	// sessions viewing the same board. ArticleID + Pinned are advisory; the
92	// re-fetch is the source of truth.
93	type ArticlePinChangedMsg struct {
94		BoardID   int64
95		ArticleID int64
96		Pinned    bool
97	}
98	
99	// ArticleCommentsModeChangedMsg is broadcast when a mod changes an article's
100	// comments_mode (open / arrows_only / locked). Article-view models filter
101	// by ArticleID; board-view models filter by BoardID and re-fetch their list
102	// so the [鎖]/[箭] badges stay current. Mode is the new CommentsMode string;
103	// receivers should re-fetch from DB rather than trust the payload.
104	type ArticleCommentsModeChangedMsg struct {
105		BoardID   int64
106		ArticleID int64
107		Mode      string // store.CommentsMode value, kept as string for tui-package isolation
108	}
