```md
# User Settings + Notification System

## Context

Three user-visible needs land together because they share one new "個人設定" screen:

1. **Self-service password change** — today only forced rotation
   (`MustChangePassword=1` route through `ScreenPasswordChange`) is wired up.
   Users have no way to change their password voluntarily.
2. **User bio** — no profile/about field exists. Adding one is a prerequisite
   for any future "看其他用戶資料" feature and is the lightest possible
   schema change to validate the settings-screen plumbing.
3. **Notification system** — when a user is offline (or even online,
   user-controllable), they currently miss every push, water balloon, mail,
   and article reply. Hooking each event into a per-user webhook lets users
   wire up Apprise (Discord/Telegram/email/etc.) without the BBS taking on
   the apprise:// URL parsing burden.

Plus one in-scope feature requested in the same session:

4. **Article reply (Re:)** — the post-compose screen only creates new
   articles. Mail already has email-style "Re: " + blockquote-quoted reply
   (`screen_mail.go:339-348` `quoteForReply`); articles do not. We extend
   the post-compose screen to accept a parent article so an `R` press in
   article-view jumps straight to a quoted reply draft.

**Architecture decision (confirmed with user):** the BBS will not embed
`unraid/apprise-go` (alpha v0.1.x, undocumented Go API). It will POST a
small JSON payload to one or more user-configured webhook URLs. The
recommended deployment puts `caronc/apprise-api` in the same
`docker-compose.yml` as the BBS, and the user pastes
`http://apprise-api:8000/notify/<config-id>` (or any other webhook —
ntfy.sh, Discord, Slack, etc.) into the settings screen.

## File map (what changes, where)

### Schema — migration 0009

**New file**: `internal/store/migrations/0009_user_settings_and_notify.sql`

\```sql
ALTER TABLE users ADD COLUMN bio TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '';

CREATE TABLE user_notif_prefs (
    user_id    INTEGER PRIMARY KEY REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    on_push    INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,  -- 推/噓
    on_wb      INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,  -- 水球
    on_mail    INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,  -- 站內信
    on_reply   INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,  -- 文章被回 (Re:)
    only_when_offline INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0
);

CREATE TABLE user_notif_targets (
    id         INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    user_id    INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    label      TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
    url        TEXT NOT NULL,
    enabled    INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
    created_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);
CREATE INDEX idx_notif_targets_user ON user_notif_targets(user_id, enabled);
\```

`user_notif_prefs` is upserted lazily on first save; absence ≡ all-default.

### Store layer

**Edit `internal/store/users.go`** — extend `User` struct with `Bio
string`, extend `Create/GetByID/GetByUserID` SELECT/INSERT lists, add
method:

\```go
func (r *UserRepo) SetBio(ctx context.Context, id int64, bio string) error
\```

(Mirror `SetPassword` shape; take `writeMu`.) Add `auth.ValidateBio`
(≤ 1024 chars, allow newlines) in `internal/auth/auth.go`.

**New file `internal/store/notify.go`** — `NotifyRepo` exposed via
`Store.Notify()`:

\```go
type NotifyPrefs struct {
    OnPush, OnWB, OnMail, OnReply, OnlyWhenOffline bool
}
type NotifyTarget struct {
    ID         int64
    UserID     int64
    Label, URL string
    Enabled    bool
    CreatedAt  time.Time
}

func (r *NotifyRepo) GetPrefs(ctx, userID) (NotifyPrefs, error)        // returns defaults if row missing
func (r *NotifyRepo) SetPrefs(ctx, userID, NotifyPrefs) error          // upsert
func (r *NotifyRepo) ListTargets(ctx, userID) ([]NotifyTarget, error)
func (r *NotifyRepo) AddTarget(ctx, userID, label, url string) (int64, error)
func (r *NotifyRepo) UpdateTarget(ctx, id, label, url string, enabled bool) error
func (r *NotifyRepo) DeleteTarget(ctx, id, userID int64) error         // userID for guard
\```

All multi-statement methods take `Store.writeMu` per CLAUDE.md invariant.

### Notification dispatcher — new package `internal/notify/`

**New file `internal/notify/dispatcher.go`:**

\```go
type Event struct {
    Kind       string  // "push" | "wb" | "mail" | "reply"
    ToUserID   int64
    FromUserID string
    Title      string  // pre-formatted, e.g. "alice 推了 «Hello»"
    Body       string  // pre-formatted, e.g. "→ 推 cool post"
}

type Manager struct {
    store   *store.Store
    broker  *chat.Broker  // for "only when offline" check
    queue   chan Event
    client  *http.Client  // 5s timeout
}

func New(st *store.Store, br *chat.Broker) *Manager
func (m *Manager) Start(ctx context.Context)            // spins N=4 workers; cancel via ctx
func (m *Manager) Dispatch(ev Event)                    // non-blocking; drops on full buffer + logs
\```

Worker loop: read prefs+targets for `ToUserID`, filter by `Kind`-matching
flag, optionally skip if `OnlyWhenOffline && broker.IsOnline(ToUserID)`,
POST `{"title": ev.Title, "body": ev.Body}` to each enabled target URL.
Apprise-API's `/notify/<key>` endpoint accepts exactly this JSON shape,
so no payload translation needed for the recommended deployment.

`broker.IsOnline(uid int64) bool` — small new method on `chat.Broker`
(check `len(b.sessions[uid]) > 0` under RLock). Pure-read addition.

### Trigger wiring

| Event  | Trigger site                                                   | Notes                                         |
|--------|----------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------|
| push   | `screen_article_view.go:516` after `Broker.SendToAll`           | `ToUserID = m.article.AuthorID`; skip self    |
| wb     | `screen_wb.go:271` (compose), `:486` (thread)                   | self-WB already gated; reuse that branch     |
| mail   | `screen_mail.go:483` after `Broker.Send`                        | self-mail already gated; reuse                |
| reply  | new article-reply submit in `screen_post_compose.submit()`      | when `parentArticleID != 0`, fire reply-to-author |

Each call site formats `Title` + `Body` once and calls
`m.deps.Notify.Dispatch(...)`. Failures are logged, never block UI.

### Server wiring

**Edit `internal/server/server.go`:**
- construct `notify.New(st, broker)` once
- call `notifyMgr.Start(ctx)` from server startup
- pass `notifyMgr` into `tui.Deps` (new field `Notify *notify.Manager`)
- shutdown: `cancel()` on context to drain workers

### TUI screens

**Edit `internal/tui/messages.go`** — add four `Screen*` constants:

\```go
ScreenUserSettings     // top-level menu
ScreenBioEdit          // textarea form
ScreenNotifySettings   // toggles + target list
ScreenArticleReply     // optional; see below
\```

For article reply, **prefer extending `ScreenPostCompose`** over a new
screen — they're ~95% identical. Add `ParentArticleID int64` to
`NavigateMsg`. In `newPostComposeModel(deps, boardID, parentArticleID)`,
if `parentArticleID != 0`, fetch parent and prefill title (`"Re: " +
strip-existing-Re(parent.Title)`) and body (markdown blockquote of
parent body). No new screen const needed; this also avoids splitting
help-screen entries.

**New file `internal/tui/screen_user_settings.go`** — vim-style menu
(reuses the `mainMenuModel` shape from `screen_main_menu.go`):

\```
=== 個人設定 ===
  > 修改密碼  Change password
    修改 Bio  Edit bio
    通知設定  Notification settings
    返回      Back
\```

j/k/↑/↓ to move, Enter/l/→ to select, q/Esc/h/← back. **Lifts an
existing constraint:** `ScreenPasswordChange` currently quits the
session on Esc (line 64-66) because it's the must-change gate. When
reached from settings (i.e., `deps.MustChangePassword == false`), Esc
should instead `NavigateMsg{To: ScreenUserSettings}`. Branch on the
flag inside `passwordChangeModel.Update`.

**New file `internal/tui/screen_bio_edit.go`** — single textarea (reuse
the post-compose pattern: textarea + Ctrl+S to save, Esc to back). Form
screen, no h/l binding. Pre-populates from `deps.User.Bio`. On submit
calls `Store.Users().SetBio(...)` and refreshes `*deps.User` in place
(same trick as password change line 136-138).

**New file `internal/tui/screen_notify_settings.go`** — split layout:
- top half: 5 boolean toggles (push / wb / mail / reply / only-when-offline)
  rendered as `[x] 有人推/噓我的文章` lines; space toggles, j/k moves
- bottom half: target list with `a` add, `e` edit, `d` delete, `t` toggle-enabled
- "add" inlines a URL textinput (modeled on the `screen_wb.go` inline
  textinput pattern — it's the closest precedent for "modal input within
  a list screen")
- Ctrl+S saves toggles, target ops save immediately

**Edit `internal/tui/screen_main_menu.go`** — insert "個人設定 Profile"
entry between online list and quit. Numeric shortcut: next free.

**Edit `internal/tui/screen_article_view.go`** — bind `R` (uppercase to
avoid clashing with any lowercase `r` already taken; check the existing
key map in this file before assigning) to:

\```go
return m, func() tea.Msg {
    return NavigateMsg{
        To: ScreenPostCompose,
        BoardID: m.article.BoardID,
        ArticleID: m.article.ID, // becomes ParentArticleID for compose
    }
}
\```

Reuse the existing `ArticleID` field on `NavigateMsg` for the parent
pointer (no new field needed) — `screen_post_compose` is the only
consumer that would interpret it.

**Edit `internal/tui/help.go`** — add `screenHelp` entries for
`ScreenUserSettings` and `ScreenNotifySettings` (which are list screens,
not form screens — they DO contribute help). `ScreenBioEdit` is a form
screen; deliberately omit per the existing convention (`?` should pass
through to textarea).

**Edit `internal/tui/root.go`** — add 3 navigate cases (user-settings,
bio-edit, notify-settings); the post-compose case extends to read
`ArticleID` as parent.

### Deployment — addresses the user's docker-compose question

Add `docker-compose.example.yml` (or extend the existing one if any) at
repo root:

\```yaml
services:
  apprise:
    image: caronc/apprise:latest
    container_name: bbs-apprise
    ports: ["8000:8000"]
    volumes: [./apprise/config:/config]   # holds notify URLs per "config key"
    restart: unless-stopped

  bbs:
    build: .
    ports: ["2222:2222"]
    volumes: [./data:/app/data, ./.ssh:/app/.ssh]
    depends_on: [apprise]
\```

User's per-account flow:

1. `docker compose up -d`
2. browse `http://localhost:8000`, save apprise:// URLs under config key
   `mykey` (or use `apprise add`)
3. SSH into BBS → 個人設定 → 通知設定 → Add target →
   `http://apprise:8000/notify/mykey`
4. push test from another account → notification fires through apprise

Document this flow in `docs/notifications.md` (new) — short, with
copy-pasteable yaml + curl-test snippet.

## Critical files to read before implementing

- `internal/store/users.go` — `SetPassword` for the SetBio template, `Create/GetByID` SELECT lists for the `bio` column addition
- `internal/store/migrate.go` — confirm migration loader handles 0009 with no extra wiring
- `internal/store/store.go` — `writeMu` discipline; how `Notify()` accessor is exposed

- `internal/tui/screen_post_compose.go` — extend `newPostComposeModel` signature for `parentArticleID`; reuse mail's `quoteForReply` (move it to a shared helper, or duplicate — duplicating is fine if simpler)
- `internal/tui/screen_mail.go:339-348` — `quoteForReply` pattern
- `internal/tui/screen_wb.go:148-230` — inline-textinput-within-list pattern for the notify-target add UI
- `internal/chat/broker.go` — add small `IsOnline(uid) bool` method
- `internal/server/server.go:76-150` — wire `notify.Manager` into Deps and shutdown
- `internal/tui/help.go` — `screenHelp` map and `isFormScreen` gate
- `internal/tui/root.go:182-243` — navigate switch
- `internal/tui/screen_main_menu.go` — add menu entry

## Tests

- `internal/store/users_test.go` — extend with bio round-trip
- `internal/store/notify_test.go` (new) — prefs upsert, target CRUD, defaults when row missing
- `internal/notify/dispatcher_test.go` (new) — `httptest.Server` recording POST body, verify `{title, body}` JSON; verify timeout doesn't block; verify "only when offline" honoured via fake `chat.Broker`-shaped sender; verify disabled targets are skipped; cover all four event kinds
- `internal/tui/screen_user_settings_test.go` (new) — j/k/Enter navigation between sub-screens
- `internal/tui/screen_bio_edit_test.go` (new) — Ctrl+S persists, `User.Bio` refreshed in place
- `internal/tui/screen_notify_settings_test.go` (new) — toggle flips bit, add/delete target round-trips through Store
- `internal/tui/screen_password_change_test.go` (extend) — Esc-from-settings goes to `ScreenUserSettings`, not `tea.Quit`, when `MustChangePassword=false`
- `internal/tui/screen_article_view_test.go` (extend) — `R` emits `NavigateMsg{To: ScreenPostCompose, BoardID, ArticleID: parent.ID}`
- `internal/tui/screen_post_compose_test.go` (extend) — `ArticleID != 0` constructor prefills `"Re: " + ...` title and `> `-quoted body
- `internal/chat/broker_test.go` (extend) — `IsOnline` true/false

All run under `make test-race`. The notify dispatcher test is race-bait
in the same spirit as `TestPushes_ConcurrentScoreAtomicity`: fire 50
concurrent `Dispatch` calls and verify no panic / no leaked goroutines
on `Stop`.

## Verification (end-to-end)

1. `make db-reset && make build && make hostkey` (if needed)
2. `docker compose up -d apprise` (or run `caronc/apprise-api` standalone on `:8000`)
3. Configure apprise key `test` with a URL you can observe (e.g., a `webhook.site` URL or a local `nc -l 9999` for raw inspection)
4. `make run`
5. Two SSH sessions: register `alice` and `bob`
6. As `alice`: 個人設定 → 通知設定 → Add `http://localhost:8000/notify/test` → enable all four events
7. As `bob`: post an article on a board, then push on alice's article → expect notification
8. As `bob`: send 水球 / mail to alice → expect notifications
9. As `bob`: open alice's article, press `R`, send reply → expect "reply" notification (and a new article titled `Re: …` with quoted body in the same board)
10. As `alice`: 個人設定 → 修改 Bio → save; reconnect → bio survives
11. As `alice`: 個人設定 → 修改密碼 → set new password; reconnect with new password
12. `go test -race ./...`

## Approved Plan:
# User Settings + Notification System

## Context

Three user-visible needs land together because they share one new "個人設定" screen:

1. **Self-service password change** — today only forced rotation
   (`MustChangePassword=1` route through `ScreenPasswordChange`) is wired up.
   Users have no way to change their password voluntarily.
2. **User bio** — no profile/about field exists. Adding one is a prerequisite
   for any future "看其他用戶資料" feature and is the lightest possible
   schema change to validate the settings-screen plumbing.
3. **Notification system** — when a user is offline (or even online,
   user-controllable), they currently miss every push, water balloon, mail,
   and article reply. Hooking each event into a per-user webhook lets users
   wire up Apprise (Discord/Telegram/email/etc.) without the BBS taking on
   the apprise:// URL parsing burden.

Plus one in-scope feature requested in the same session:

4. **Article reply (Re:)** — the post-compose screen only creates new
   articles. Mail already has email-style "Re: " + blockquote-quoted reply
   (`screen_mail.go:339-348` `quoteForReply`); articles do not. We extend
   the post-compose screen to accept a parent article so an `R` press in
   article-view jumps straight to a quoted reply draft.

**Architecture decision (confirmed with user):** the BBS will not embed
`unraid/apprise-go` (alpha v0.1.x, undocumented Go API). It will POST a
small JSON payload to one or more user-configured webhook URLs. The
recommended deployment puts `caronc/apprise-api` in the same
`docker-compose.yml` as the BBS, and the user pastes
`http://apprise-api:8000/notify/<config-id>` (or any other webhook —
ntfy.sh, Discord, Slack, etc.) into the settings screen.

## File map (what changes, where)

### Schema — migration 0009

**New file**: `internal/store/migrations/0009_user_settings_and_notify.sql`

```sql
ALTER TABLE users ADD COLUMN bio TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '';

CREATE TABLE user_notif_prefs (
    user_id    INTEGER PRIMARY KEY REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    on_push    INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,  -- 推/噓
    on_wb      INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,  -- 水球
    on_mail    INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,  -- 站內信
    on_reply   INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,  -- 文章被回 (Re:)
    only_when_offline INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0
);

CREATE TABLE user_notif_targets (
    id         INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    user_id    INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    label      TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
    url        TEXT NOT NULL,
    enabled    INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
    created_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);
CREATE INDEX idx_notif_targets_user ON user_notif_targets(user_id, enabled);
```

`user_notif_prefs` is upserted lazily on first save; absence ≡ all-default.

### Store layer

**Edit `internal/store/users.go`** — extend `User` struct with `Bio
string`, extend `Create/GetByID/GetByUserID` SELECT/INSERT lists, add
method:

```go
func (r *UserRepo) SetBio(ctx context.Context, id int64, bio string) error
```

(Mirror `SetPassword` shape; take `writeMu`.) Add `auth.ValidateBio`
(≤ 1024 chars, allow newlines) in `internal/auth/auth.go`.

**New file `internal/store/notify.go`** — `NotifyRepo` exposed via
`Store.Notify()`:

```go
type NotifyPrefs struct {
    OnPush, OnWB, OnMail, OnReply, OnlyWhenOffline bool
}
type NotifyTarget struct {
    ID         int64
    UserID     int64
    Label, URL string
    Enabled    bool
    CreatedAt  time.Time
}

func (r *NotifyRepo) GetPrefs(ctx, userID) (NotifyPrefs, error)        // returns defaults if row missing
func (r *NotifyRepo) SetPrefs(ctx, userID, NotifyPrefs) error          // upsert
func (r *NotifyRepo) ListTargets(ctx, userID) ([]NotifyTarget, error)
func (r *NotifyRepo) AddTarget(ctx, userID, label, url string) (int64, error)
func (r *NotifyRepo) UpdateTarget(ctx, id, label, url string, enabled bool) error
func (r *NotifyRepo) DeleteTarget(ctx, id, userID int64) error         // userID for guard
```

All multi-statement methods take `Store.writeMu` per CLAUDE.md invariant.

### Notification dispatcher — new package `internal/notify/`

**New file `internal/notify/dispatcher.go`:**

```go
type Event struct {
    Kind       string  // "push" | "wb" | "mail" | "reply"
    ToUserID   int64
    FromUserID string
    Title      string  // pre-formatted, e.g. "alice 推了 «Hello»"
    Body       string  // pre-formatted, e.g. "→ 推 cool post"
}

type Manager struct {
    store   *store.Store
    broker  *chat.Broker  // for "only when offline" check
    queue   chan Event
    client  *http.Client  // 5s timeout
}

func New(st *store.Store, br *chat.Broker) *Manager
func (m *Manager) Start(ctx context.Context)            // spins N=4 workers; cancel via ctx
func (m *Manager) Dispatch(ev Event)                    // non-blocking; drops on full buffer + logs
```

Worker loop: read prefs+targets for `ToUserID`, filter by `Kind`-matching
flag, optionally skip if `OnlyWhenOffline && broker.IsOnline(ToUserID)`,
POST `{"title": ev.Title, "body": ev.Body}` to each enabled target URL.
Apprise-API's `/notify/<key>` endpoint accepts exactly this JSON shape,
so no payload translation needed for the recommended deployment.

`broker.IsOnline(uid int64) bool` — small new method on `chat.Broker`
(check `len(b.sessions[uid]) > 0` under RLock). Pure-read addition.

### Trigger wiring

| Event  | Trigger site                                                   | Notes                                         |
|--------|----------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------|
| push   | `screen_article_view.go:516` after `Broker.SendToAll`           | `ToUserID = m.article.AuthorID`; skip self    |
| wb     | `screen_wb.go:271` (compose), `:486` (thread)                   | self-WB already gated; reuse that branch     |
| mail   | `screen_mail.go:483` after `Broker.Send`                        | self-mail already gated; reuse                |
| reply  | new article-reply submit in `screen_post_compose.submit()`      | when `parentArticleID != 0`, fire reply-to-author |

Each call site formats `Title` + `Body` once and calls
`m.deps.Notify.Dispatch(...)`. Failures are logged, never block UI.

### Server wiring

**Edit `internal/server/server.go`:**
- construct `notify.New(st, broker)` once
- call `notifyMgr.Start(ctx)` from server startup
- pass `notifyMgr` into `tui.Deps` (new field `Notify *notify.Manager`)
- shutdown: `cancel()` on context to drain workers

### TUI screens

**Edit `internal/tui/messages.go`** — add four `Screen*` constants:

```go
ScreenUserSettings     // top-level menu
ScreenBioEdit          // textarea form
ScreenNotifySettings   // toggles + target list
ScreenArticleReply     // optional; see below
```

For article reply, **prefer extending `ScreenPostCompose`** over a new
screen — they're ~95% identical. Add `ParentArticleID int64` to
`NavigateMsg`. In `newPostComposeModel(deps, boardID, parentArticleID)`,
if `parentArticleID != 0`, fetch parent and prefill title (`"Re: " +
strip-existing-Re(parent.Title)`) and body (markdown blockquote of
parent body). No new screen const needed; this also avoids splitting
help-screen entries.

**New file `internal/tui/screen_user_settings.go`** — vim-style menu
(reuses the `mainMenuModel` shape from `screen_main_menu.go`):

```
=== 個人設定 ===
  > 修改密碼  Change password
    修改 Bio  Edit bio
    通知設定  Notification settings
    返回      Back
```

j/k/↑/↓ to move, Enter/l/→ to select, q/Esc/h/← back. **Lifts an
existing constraint:** `ScreenPasswordChange` currently quits the
session on Esc (line 64-66) because it's the must-change gate. When
reached from settings (i.e., `deps.MustChangePassword == false`), Esc
should instead `NavigateMsg{To: ScreenUserSettings}`. Branch on the
flag inside `passwordChangeModel.Update`.

**New file `internal/tui/screen_bio_edit.go`** — single textarea (reuse
the post-compose pattern: textarea + Ctrl+S to save, Esc to back). Form
screen, no h/l binding. Pre-populates from `deps.User.Bio`. On submit
calls `Store.Users().SetBio(...)` and refreshes `*deps.User` in place
(same trick as password change line 136-138).

**New file `internal/tui/screen_notify_settings.go`** — split layout:
- top half: 5 boolean toggles (push / wb / mail / reply / only-when-offline)
  rendered as `[x] 有人推/噓我的文章` lines; space toggles, j/k moves
- bottom half: target list with `a` add, `e` edit, `d` delete, `t` toggle-enabled
- "add" inlines a URL textinput (modeled on the `screen_wb.go` inline
  textinput pattern — it's the closest precedent for "modal input within
  a list screen")
- Ctrl+S saves toggles, target ops save immediately

**Edit `internal/tui/screen_main_menu.go`** — insert "個人設定 Profile"
entry between online list and quit. Numeric shortcut: next free.

**Edit `internal/tui/screen_article_view.go`** — bind `R` (uppercase to
avoid clashing with any lowercase `r` already taken; check the existing
key map in this file before assigning) to:

```go
return m, func() tea.Msg {
    return NavigateMsg{
        To: ScreenPostCompose,
        BoardID: m.article.BoardID,
        ArticleID: m.article.ID, // becomes ParentArticleID for compose
    }
}
```

Reuse the existing `ArticleID` field on `NavigateMsg` for the parent
pointer (no new field needed) — `screen_post_compose` is the only
consumer that would interpret it.

**Edit `internal/tui/help.go`** — add `screenHelp` entries for
`ScreenUserSettings` and `ScreenNotifySettings` (which are list screens,
not form screens — they DO contribute help). `ScreenBioEdit` is a form
screen; deliberately omit per the existing convention (`?` should pass
through to textarea).

**Edit `internal/tui/root.go`** — add 3 navigate cases (user-settings,
bio-edit, notify-settings); the post-compose case extends to read
`ArticleID` as parent.

### Deployment — addresses the user's docker-compose question

Add `docker-compose.example.yml` (or extend the existing one if any) at
repo root:

```yaml
services:
  apprise:
    image: caronc/apprise:latest
    container_name: bbs-apprise
    ports: ["8000:8000"]
    volumes: [./apprise/config:/config]   # holds notify URLs per "config key"
    restart: unless-stopped

  bbs:
    build: .
    ports: ["2222:2222"]
    volumes: [./data:/app/data, ./.ssh:/app/.ssh]
    depends_on: [apprise]
```

User's per-account flow:

1. `docker compose up -d`
2. browse `http://localhost:8000`, save apprise:// URLs under config key
   `mykey` (or use `apprise add`)
3. SSH into BBS → 個人設定 → 通知設定 → Add target →
   `http://apprise:8000/notify/mykey`
4. push test from another account → notification fires through apprise

### Documentation deliverable — `docs/notifications.md`

This is a first-class part of the PR (not optional). Outline:

1. **Overview** — what the notification system does, four event kinds,
   per-user opt-in.
2. **Architecture decision: why webhook, not embedded library** —
   capture the trade-off so future contributors don't reopen it:
   - `unraid/apprise-go` is pure-Go but v0.1.x alpha with undocumented
     Go API; binding the BBS to it locks us to its release cadence.
   - `caronc/apprise` (Python, the canonical project) supports 100+
     services and is battle-tested.
   - Webhook indirection means the BBS speaks one protocol (HTTP POST
     `{title, body}`) and any of {apprise-api, ntfy.sh, Discord webhook,
     Slack webhook, custom} works without BBS code changes.
   - Operational cost: one extra container (`caronc/apprise-api`) for
     users who want the full apprise:// surface.
3. **Recommended deployment** — the docker-compose snippet from above,
   plus a `make notif-up` target that brings up the apprise sidecar.
4. **First-time apprise-api setup** — copy-pasteable steps for adding
   a Discord webhook under config key `mykey`:
   ```bash
   curl -X POST http://localhost:8000/add/mykey \
     -d '{"urls": "discord://webhook_id/webhook_token"}'
   ```
5. **Per-user BBS configuration walk-through** — screenshots-or-ASCII of
   the 通知設定 screen showing toggle + target list.
6. **Payload schema** — exact JSON the BBS sends, so users can plug in
   non-apprise webhooks if they want:
   ```json
   {"title": "alice 推了你的文章 «Hello»", "body": "→ 推 cool post"}
   ```
7. **Troubleshooting** — what "delivered=false" looks like in the BBS
   server log, how to verify the apprise-api endpoint with `curl`, how
   to disable a noisy target without deleting it (the `enabled` toggle).
8. **Privacy / threat-model note** — webhook URLs leave your network;
   recommend self-hosted apprise-api over public webhook services for
   sensitive content. Webhook URLs are stored in plaintext in
   `data/bbs.db`; treat the DB file as a secret.

Cross-link from `README.md` (one line under "Features") and from
`CLAUDE.md` (one line in the Architecture section pointing to
`docs/notifications.md` for the full mapping). The architecture
decision (item 2) is also worth a one-liner in `pitfalls/` if we ever
revisit the embedded-library choice — but only after the doc lands;
no speculative pitfall entry yet.

## Tests

- `internal/store/users_test.go` — extend with bio round-trip
- `internal/store/notify_test.go` (new) — prefs upsert, target CRUD, defaults when row missing
- `internal/notify/dispatcher_test.go` (new) — `httptest.Server` recording POST body, verify `{title, body}` JSON; verify timeout doesn't block; verify "only when offline" honoured via fake `chat.Broker`-shaped sender; verify disabled targets are skipped; cover all four event kinds
- `internal/tui/screen_user_settings_test.go` (new) — j/k/Enter navigation between sub-screens
- `internal/tui/screen_bio_edit_test.go` (new) — Ctrl+S persists, `User.Bio` refreshed in place
- `internal/tui/screen_notify_settings_test.go` (new) — toggle flips bit, add/delete target round-trips through Store
- `internal/tui/screen_password_change_test.go` (extend) — Esc-from-settings goes to `ScreenUserSettings`, not `tea.Quit`, when `MustChangePassword=false`
- `internal/tui/screen_article_view_test.go` (extend) — `R` emits `NavigateMsg{To: ScreenPostCompose, BoardID, ArticleID: parent.ID}`
- `internal/tui/screen_post_compose_test.go` (extend) — `ArticleID != 0` constructor prefills `"Re: " + ...` title and `> `-quoted body
- `internal/chat/broker_test.go` (extend) — `IsOnline` true/false

All run under `make test-race`. The notify dispatcher test is race-bait
in the same spirit as `TestPushes_ConcurrentScoreAtomicity`: fire 50
concurrent `Dispatch` calls and verify no panic / no leaked goroutines
on `Stop`.

## Verification (end-to-end)

1. `make db-reset && make build && make hostkey` (if needed)
2. `docker compose up -d apprise` (or run `caronc/apprise-api` standalone on `:8000`)
3. Configure apprise key `test` with a URL you can observe (e.g., a `webhook.site` URL or a local `nc -l 9999` for raw inspection)
4. `make run`
5. Two SSH sessions: register `alice` and `bob`
6. As `alice`: 個人設定 → 通知設定 → Add `http://localhost:8000/notify/test` → enable all four events
7. As `bob`: post an article on a board, then push on alice's article → expect notification
8. As `bob`: send 水球 / mail to alice → expect notifications
9. As `bob`: open alice's article, press `R`, send reply → expect "reply" notification (and a new article titled `Re: …` with quoted body in the same board)
10. As `alice`: 個人設定 → 修改 Bio → save; reconnect → bio survives
11. As `alice`: 個人設定 → 修改密碼 → set new password; reconnect with new password
12. `go test -race ./...`

1	package store
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"database/sql"
6		"errors"
7		"time"
8	)
9	
10	type User struct {
11		ID                 int64
12		UserID             string
13		PasswordHash       string
14		Nickname           string
15		RealName           string
16		Email              string
17		NumLogins          int64
18		NumPosts           int64
19		LastLoginAt        sql.NullTime
20		LastHost           sql.NullString
21		CreatedAt          time.Time
22		Role               Role
23		MustChangePassword bool
24	}
25	
26	type UserRepo struct{ s *Store }
27	
28	var (
29		ErrUserNotFound = errors.New("user not found")
30		ErrInvalidRole  = errors.New("invalid role")
31	)
32	
33	const userColumns = `id, user_id, password_hash, nickname, realname, email,
34		num_logins, num_posts, last_login_at, last_host, created_at, role, must_change_password`
35	
36	func scanUser(row interface{ Scan(...any) error }) (*User, error) {
37		u := &User{}
38		var mustChange int64
39		err := row.Scan(
40			&u.ID, &u.UserID, &u.PasswordHash, &u.Nickname, &u.RealName, &u.Email,
41			&u.NumLogins, &u.NumPosts, &u.LastLoginAt, &u.LastHost, &u.CreatedAt,
42			&u.Role, &mustChange,
43		)
44		u.MustChangePassword = mustChange != 0
45		return u, err
46	}
47	
48	func (r *UserRepo) Create(ctx context.Context, userID, passwordHash, nickname, email string) (*User, error) {
49		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
50		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
51		res, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
52			`INSERT INTO users (user_id, password_hash, nickname, email) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)`,
53			userID, passwordHash, nickname, email,
54		)
55		if err != nil {
56			return nil, err
57		}
58		id, err := res.LastInsertId()
59		if err != nil {
60			return nil, err
61		}
62		return r.GetByID(ctx, id)
63	}
64	
65	func (r *UserRepo) GetByID(ctx context.Context, id int64) (*User, error) {
66		row := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, `SELECT `+userColumns+` FROM users WHERE id = ?`, id)
67		u, err := scanUser(row)
68		if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
69			return nil, ErrUserNotFound
70		}
71		return u, err
72	}
73	
74	func (r *UserRepo) GetByUserID(ctx context.Context, userID string) (*User, error) {
75		row := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, `SELECT `+userColumns+` FROM users WHERE user_id = ? COLLATE NOCASE`, userID)
76		u, err := scanUser(row)
77		if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
78			return nil, ErrUserNotFound
79		}
80		return u, err
81	}
82	
83	func (r *UserRepo) NoteLogin(ctx context.Context, id int64, host string) error {
84		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
85		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
86		_, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
87			`UPDATE users SET last_login_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP, last_host = ?, num_logins = num_logins + 1 WHERE id = ?`,
88			host, id,
89		)
90		return err
91	}
92	
93	func (r *UserRepo) IncrementPosts(ctx context.Context, id int64) error {
94		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
95		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
96		_, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
97			`UPDATE users SET num_posts = num_posts + 1 WHERE id = ?`, id,
98		)
99		return err
100	}
101	
102	// InsertSystemAccount creates a privileged user (admin or guest) with the
103	// given role and must_change_password flag, bypassing the regular Create
104	// path which always defaults role='user'. Used only by the bootstrap seed.
105	func (r *UserRepo) InsertSystemAccount(ctx context.Context, userID, passwordHash string, role Role, mustChangePassword bool) (*User, error) {
106		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
107		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
108		mc := int64(0)
109		if mustChangePassword {
110			mc = 1
111		}
112		res, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
113			`INSERT INTO users (user_id, password_hash, role, must_change_password) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)`,
114			userID, passwordHash, string(role), mc,
115		)
116		if err != nil {
117			return nil, err
118		}
119		id, err := res.LastInsertId()
120		if err != nil {
121			return nil, err
122		}
123		return r.GetByID(ctx, id)
124	}
125	
126	// SetPassword updates the password hash and clears must_change_password
127	// in one statement. The TUI password-change screen calls this after
128	// validating the current password and the new-password rules.
129	func (r *UserRepo) SetPassword(ctx context.Context, id int64, passwordHash string) error {
130		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
131		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
132		_, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
133			`UPDATE users SET password_hash = ?, must_change_password = 0 WHERE id = ?`,
134			passwordHash, id,
135		)
136		return err
137	}
138	
139	// SetRole updates the role of the given user. Callers (admin user-management
140	// screen) are responsible for enforcing higher-level invariants like the
141	// last-admin guard via CountByRole.
142	func (r *UserRepo) SetRole(ctx context.Context, id int64, role Role) error {
143		if !role.Valid() {
144			return ErrInvalidRole
145		}
146		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
147		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
148		_, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
149			`UPDATE users SET role = ? WHERE id = ?`, string(role), id,
150		)
151		return err
152	}
153	
154	// ListAll returns users ordered by id, paginated. limit<=0 defaults to 20.
155	func (r *UserRepo) ListAll(ctx context.Context, limit, offset int) ([]*User, error) {
156		if limit <= 0 {
157			limit = 20
158		}
159		if offset < 0 {
160			offset = 0
161		}
162		rows, err := r.s.db.QueryContext(ctx,
163			`SELECT `+userColumns+` FROM users ORDER BY id ASC LIMIT ? OFFSET ?`,
164			limit, offset,
165		)
166		if err != nil {
167			return nil, err
168		}
169		defer rows.Close()
170		var out []*User
171		for rows.Next() {
172			u, err := scanUser(rows)
173			if err != nil {
174				return nil, err
175			}
176			out = append(out, u)
177		}
178		return out, rows.Err()
179	}
180	
181	// CountByRole returns the number of users currently assigned a given role.
182	// Used by the admin screen's last-admin guard.
183	func (r *UserRepo) CountByRole(ctx context.Context, role Role) (int, error) {
184		var n int
185		err := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx,
186			`SELECT COUNT(*) FROM users WHERE role = ?`, string(role),
187		).Scan(&n)
188		return n, err
189	}

```text
1	package store
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"database/sql"
6		"fmt"
7		"sync"
8	
9		_ "modernc.org/sqlite"
10	)
11	
12	// Store wraps the *sql.DB plus a process-level write mutex used for
13	// multi-statement writes that need to be atomic at the application level
14	// (e.g. inserting a push and recomputing the cached recommend_score).
15	// SQLite WAL handles concurrent reads; this mutex avoids SQLITE_BUSY under
16	// modest contention without spreading sql.Tx everywhere.
17	type Store struct {
18		db      *sql.DB
19		writeMu sync.Mutex
20	
21		users          *UserRepo
22		boards         *BoardRepo
23		articles       *ArticleRepo
24		pushes         *PushRepo
25		waterBalloons  *WaterBalloonRepo
26		mail           *MailRepo
27	}
28	
29	func Open(path string) (*Store, error) {
30		dsn := fmt.Sprintf(
31			"file:%s?_pragma=journal_mode(WAL)&_pragma=foreign_keys(on)&_pragma=busy_timeout(5000)",
32			path,
33		)
34		db, err := sql.Open("sqlite", dsn)
35		if err != nil {
36			return nil, fmt.Errorf("open sqlite: %w", err)
37		}
38		if err := db.PingContext(context.Background()); err != nil {
39			_ = db.Close()
40			return nil, fmt.Errorf("ping sqlite: %w", err)
41		}
42		if err := apply(context.Background(), db); err != nil {
43			_ = db.Close()
44			return nil, fmt.Errorf("migrate: %w", err)
45		}
46	
47		s := &Store{db: db}
48		s.users = &UserRepo{s: s}
49		s.boards = &BoardRepo{s: s}
50		s.articles = &ArticleRepo{s: s}
51		s.pushes = &PushRepo{s: s}
52		s.waterBalloons = &WaterBalloonRepo{s: s}
53		s.mail = &MailRepo{s: s}
54		return s, nil
55	}
56	
57	func (s *Store) Close() error { return s.db.Close() }
58	
59	// DB exposes the raw handle for callers that need it (tests, ad-hoc SQL).
60	// Production code should go through repos.
61	func (s *Store) DB() *sql.DB { return s.db }
62	
63	func (s *Store) Users() *UserRepo                 { return s.users }
64	func (s *Store) Boards() *BoardRepo               { return s.boards }
65	func (s *Store) Articles() *ArticleRepo           { return s.articles }
66	func (s *Store) Pushes() *PushRepo                { return s.pushes }
67	func (s *Store) WaterBalloons() *WaterBalloonRepo { return s.waterBalloons }
68	func (s *Store) Mail() *MailRepo                  { return s.mail }
69

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./internal/store/migrate.go`

```text
1	package store
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"database/sql"
6		"embed"
7		"fmt"
8		"sort"
9		"strconv"
10		"strings"
11	)
12	
13	//go:embed migrations/*.sql
14	var migrationsFS embed.FS
15	
16	// apply runs every migration whose version number isn't already in
17	// schema_migrations. Filenames must be NNNN_name.sql; the leading integer is
18	// the version. Migrations are applied in ascending version order, each in
19	// its own transaction.
20	func apply(ctx context.Context, db *sql.DB) error {
21		if _, err := db.ExecContext(ctx, `
22			CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS schema_migrations (
23				version    INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
24				applied_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
25			)
26		`); err != nil {
27			return fmt.Errorf("create schema_migrations: %w", err)
28		}
29	
30		applied, err := loadApplied(ctx, db)
31		if err != nil {
32			return err
33		}
34	
35		entries, err := migrationsFS.ReadDir("migrations")
36		if err != nil {
37			return fmt.Errorf("read embedded migrations: %w", err)
38		}
39		type migration struct {
40			version  int
41			filename string
42		}
43		var pending []migration
44		for _, e := range entries {
45			if e.IsDir() || !strings.HasSuffix(e.Name(), ".sql") {
46				continue
47			}
48			v, err := parseVersion(e.Name())
49			if err != nil {
50				return err
51			}
52			if !applied[v] {
53				pending = append(pending, migration{version: v, filename: e.Name()})
54			}
55		}
56		sort.Slice(pending, func(i, j int) bool { return pending[i].version < pending[j].version })
57	
58		for _, m := range pending {
59			body, err := migrationsFS.ReadFile("migrations/" + m.filename)
60			if err != nil {
61				return fmt.Errorf("read migration %s: %w", m.filename, err)
62			}
63			if err := runOne(ctx, db, m.version, string(body)); err != nil {
64				return fmt.Errorf("apply %s: %w", m.filename, err)
65			}
66		}
67		return nil
68	}
69	
70	func loadApplied(ctx context.Context, db *sql.DB) (map[int]bool, error) {
71		rows, err := db.QueryContext(ctx, `SELECT version FROM schema_migrations`)
72		if err != nil {
73			return nil, fmt.Errorf("load applied migrations: %w", err)
74		}
75		defer rows.Close()
76		out := map[int]bool{}
77		for rows.Next() {
78			var v int
79			if err := rows.Scan(&v); err != nil {
80				return nil, err
81			}
82			out[v] = true
83		}
84		return out, rows.Err()
85	}
86	
87	func runOne(ctx context.Context, db *sql.DB, version int, body string) error {
88		tx, err := db.BeginTx(ctx, nil)
89		if err != nil {
90			return err
91		}
92		defer tx.Rollback()
93		if _, err := tx.ExecContext(ctx, body); err != nil {
94			return err
95		}
96		if _, err := tx.ExecContext(ctx, `INSERT INTO schema_migrations(version) VALUES (?)`, version); err != nil {
97			return err
98		}
99		return tx.Commit()
100	}
101	
102	func parseVersion(filename string) (int, error) {
103		// "0001_init.sql" -> 1
104		idx := strings.IndexByte(filename, '_')
105		if idx <= 0 {
106			return 0, fmt.Errorf("migration filename missing version prefix: %s", filename)
107		}
108		v, err := strconv.Atoi(filename[:idx])
109		if err != nil {
110			return 0, fmt.Errorf("parse version in %s: %w", filename, err)
111		}
112		return v, nil
113	}
114
```
</details></tool-use>

<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./internal/chat/broker.go`

```text
1	// Package chat is the in-memory hub that connects live SSH sessions for
2	// real-time features (water balloons, push broadcasts). It mirrors PTT's
3	// `userinfo_t` shared-memory table conceptually, but as a process-local
4	// map[user_id][]*Session — no SHM, no daemons.
5	package chat
6	
7	import (
8		"sync"
9	
10		tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"
11	)
12	
13	// Sender is the minimal slice of *tea.Program the broker actually needs.
14	// Decoupling lets tests substitute a recording fake without spinning up a
15	// real bubbletea program.
16	type Sender interface {
17		Send(tea.Msg)
18	}
19	
20	// Session is one live SSH connection. The same user may have multiple
21	// Sessions (PTT calls this "雙開"); the broker keeps them all and
22	// broadcasts to each.
23	type Session struct {
24		UserID    int64
25		UserIDStr string
26		Program   Sender
27	}
28	
29	// OnlineUser is the externally-visible view of a logged-in user.
30	type OnlineUser struct {
31		UserID    int64
32		UserIDStr string
33		Sessions  int
34	}
35	
36	type Broker struct {
37		mu       sync.RWMutex
38		sessions map[int64][]*Session
39	}
40	
41	func NewBroker() *Broker {
42		return &Broker{sessions: make(map[int64][]*Session)}
43	}
44	
45	func (b *Broker) Register(s *Session) {
46		b.mu.Lock()
47		defer b.mu.Unlock()
48		b.sessions[s.UserID] = append(b.sessions[s.UserID], s)
49	}
50	
51	// Unregister removes a single session for the given user (matched by
52	// Sender identity). If it was the last session, the user disappears from
53	// the online list.
54	func (b *Broker) Unregister(userID int64, prog Sender) {
55		b.mu.Lock()
56		defer b.mu.Unlock()
57		list := b.sessions[userID]
58		for i, s := range list {
59			if s.Program == prog {
60				b.sessions[userID] = append(list[:i], list[i+1:]...)
61				break
62			}
63		}
64		if len(b.sessions[userID]) == 0 {
65			delete(b.sessions, userID)
66		}
67	}
68	
69	// Send delivers msg to every live session of toUID. Returns true if at
70	// least one session received it (i.e. the recipient is online).
71	//
72	// IMPORTANT: do NOT call from inside an Update path with toUID equal to
73	// the caller's own UserID. bubbletea's tea.Program.Send writes to an
74	// unbuffered msgs channel, so a self-targeted Send while the program
75	// loop is blocked in Update will deadlock the SSH session. Application
76	// code must guard self-targeting at the call site (see
77	// pitfalls/water-balloon-self-send-hangs-server.md).
78	func (b *Broker) Send(toUID int64, msg tea.Msg) bool {
79		b.mu.RLock()
80		defer b.mu.RUnlock()
81		list := b.sessions[toUID]
82		for _, s := range list {
83			s.Program.Send(msg)
84		}
85		return len(list) > 0
86	}
87	
88	// SendToAll broadcasts to every online session. excludeUID lets the
89	// originator skip themselves (pass 0 to broadcast to everyone).
90	func (b *Broker) SendToAll(excludeUID int64, msg tea.Msg) {
91		b.mu.RLock()
92		defer b.mu.RUnlock()
93		for uid, list := range b.sessions {
94			if uid == excludeUID {
95				continue
96			}
97			for _, s := range list {
98				s.Program.Send(msg)
99			}
100		}
101	}
102	
103	// LookupByUserID returns the numeric user_id for a logged-in account name,
104	// or 0 if nobody by that name is currently online. Case-insensitive.
105	func (b *Broker) LookupByUserID(name string) int64 {
106		b.mu.RLock()
107		defer b.mu.RUnlock()
108		for uid, list := range b.sessions {
109			for _, s := range list {
110				if equalFold(s.UserIDStr, name) {
111					return uid
112				}
113			}
114		}
115		return 0
116	}
117	
118	func (b *Broker) OnlineList() []OnlineUser {
119		b.mu.RLock()
120		defer b.mu.RUnlock()
121		out := make([]OnlineUser, 0, len(b.sessions))
122		for uid, list := range b.sessions {
123			if len(list) == 0 {
124				continue
125			}
126			out = append(out, OnlineUser{
127				UserID:    uid,
128				UserIDStr: list[0].UserIDStr,
129				Sessions:  len(list),
130			})
131		}
132		return out
133	}
134	
135	// SessionsSnapshot returns a defensive copy of every session pointer for
136	// graceful shutdown (so the caller can iterate without holding the lock
137	// while blocking on per-program quits).
138	func (b *Broker) SessionsSnapshot() []*Session {
139		b.mu.RLock()
140		defer b.mu.RUnlock()
141		var out []*Session
142		for _, list := range b.sessions {
143			out = append(out, list...)
144		}
145		return out
146	}
147	
148	// equalFold is strings.EqualFold inlined to avoid the import in this tiny package.
149	func equalFold(a, b string) bool {
150		if len(a) != len(b) {
151			return false
152		}
153		for i := 0; i < len(a); i++ {
154			ca, cb := a[i], b[i]
155			if ca >= 'A' && ca <= 'Z' {
156				ca += 32
157			}
158			if cb >= 'A' && cb <= 'Z' {
159				cb += 32
160			}
161			if ca != cb {
162				return false
163			}
164		}
165		return true
166	}
167
```

<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./internal/auth/auth.go`

```text
1	package auth
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"errors"
6		"fmt"
7		"regexp"
8		"strings"
9	
10		"golang.org/x/crypto/bcrypt"
11	
12		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store"
13	)
14	
15	// Reserved SSH usernames. `new` triggers the in-TUI register flow;
16	// `guest` short-circuits to a read-only spectator account; `admin` is
17	// the bootstrapped administrator and may not be re-registered.
18	const (
19		ReservedUsernameNew   = "new"
20		ReservedUsernameGuest = "guest"
21		ReservedUsernameAdmin = "admin"
22	)
23	
24	// IsReservedUsername reports whether s matches one of the three reserved
25	// SSH usernames (case-insensitive). Used by Register to refuse the names
26	// and by the SSH password callback to dispatch to the right flow.
27	func IsReservedUsername(s string) bool {
28		s = strings.TrimSpace(s)
29		return strings.EqualFold(s, ReservedUsernameNew) ||
30			strings.EqualFold(s, ReservedUsernameGuest) ||
31			strings.EqualFold(s, ReservedUsernameAdmin)
32	}
33	
34	const (
35		bcryptCost     = 12
36		minPasswordLen = 6
37		maxPasswordLen = 128
38		maxNickname    = 32
39		maxEmail       = 128
40	)
41	
42	var (
43		ErrUserExists       = errors.New("user already exists")
44		ErrInvalidUserID    = errors.New("user id must be 3-12 chars, start with a letter, and use only letters/digits/underscore")
45		ErrInvalidPassword  = fmt.Errorf("password must be %d-%d characters", minPasswordLen, maxPasswordLen)
46		ErrNicknameTooLong  = fmt.Errorf("nickname must be %d characters or fewer", maxNickname)
47		ErrEmailTooLong     = fmt.Errorf("email must be %d characters or fewer", maxEmail)
48		ErrReservedUsername = fmt.Errorf("user id is reserved (%q / %q / %q)",
49			ReservedUsernameNew, ReservedUsernameGuest, ReservedUsernameAdmin)
50		ErrBadCredentials   = errors.New("invalid user id or password")
51	)
52	
53	var userIDRe = regexp.MustCompile(`^[A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9_]{2,11}$`)
54	
55	// ValidatePassword enforces the length rules. Used by Register and the
56	// password-change screen so the rules don't drift between call sites.
57	func ValidatePassword(password string) error {
58		if l := len(password); l < minPasswordLen || l > maxPasswordLen {
59			return ErrInvalidPassword
60		}
61		return nil
62	}
63	
64	// HashPassword returns a bcrypt hash at the package's standard cost.
65	// Callers outside auth (TUI, seed) use this so bcryptCost stays in one place.
66	func HashPassword(password string) (string, error) {
67		hash, err := bcrypt.GenerateFromPassword([]byte(password), bcryptCost)
68		if err != nil {
69			return "", fmt.Errorf("hash password: %w", err)
70		}
71		return string(hash), nil
72	}
73	
74	// VerifyPasswordHash returns nil when the hash matches the password.
75	// Callers outside auth use this instead of importing bcrypt directly.
76	func VerifyPasswordHash(hash, password string) error {
77		return bcrypt.CompareHashAndPassword([]byte(hash), []byte(password))
78	}
79	
80	// Register validates inputs, hashes the password with bcrypt, and inserts a
81	// new user. Returns ErrUserExists on UNIQUE conflict.
82	func Register(ctx context.Context, st *store.Store, userID, password, nickname, email string) (*store.User, error) {
83		userID = strings.TrimSpace(userID)
84		nickname = strings.TrimSpace(nickname)
85		email = strings.TrimSpace(email)
86	
87		if !userIDRe.MatchString(userID) {
88			return nil, ErrInvalidUserID
89		}
90		if IsReservedUsername(userID) {
91			return nil, ErrReservedUsername
92		}
93		if err := ValidatePassword(password); err != nil {
94			return nil, err
95		}
96		if len([]rune(nickname)) > maxNickname {
97			return nil, ErrNicknameTooLong
98		}
99		if len(email) > maxEmail {
100			return nil, ErrEmailTooLong
101		}
102	
103		hash, err := HashPassword(password)
104		if err != nil {
105			return nil, err
106		}
107	
108		u, err := st.Users().Create(ctx, userID, hash, nickname, email)
109		if err != nil {
110			// modernc.org/sqlite returns a wrapped UNIQUE error; check the text.
111			if strings.Contains(err.Error(), "UNIQUE") {
112				return nil, ErrUserExists
113			}
114			return nil, err
115		}
116		return u, nil
117	}
118	
119	// VerifyLogin returns the user record if the password matches, ErrBadCredentials otherwise.
120	// On success, NoteLogin is called to update last_login_at / last_host / num_logins.
121	func VerifyLogin(ctx context.Context, st *store.Store, userID, password, host string) (*store.User, error) {
122		u, err := st.Users().GetByUserID(ctx, strings.TrimSpace(userID))
123		if err != nil {
124			if errors.Is(err, store.ErrUserNotFound) {
125				// Run a dummy bcrypt check anyway to even out timing.
126				_ = bcrypt.CompareHashAndPassword([]byte("$2a$12$invalidinvalidinvalidinvalidinvalidinvalidinvalidinvali"), []byte(password))
127				return nil, ErrBadCredentials
128			}
129			return nil, err
130		}
131		if err := bcrypt.CompareHashAndPassword([]byte(u.PasswordHash), []byte(password)); err != nil {
132			return nil, ErrBadCredentials
133		}
134		// Skip NoteLogin for guest — the row is shared across every spectator
135		// session, so num_logins / last_login_at are meaningless and the write
136		// would just create contention. Defensive: the SSH password callback
137		// short-circuits guest before calling VerifyLogin in normal flow.
138		if u.Role == store.RoleGuest {
139			return u, nil
140		}
141		if err := st.Users().NoteLogin(ctx, u.ID, host); err != nil {
142			return nil, fmt.Errorf("note login: %w", err)
143		}
144		// Re-fetch so callers see the updated last_login_at / num_logins.
145		return st.Users().GetByID(ctx, u.ID)
146	}
147

1	package server
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"errors"
6	
7		tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"
8		"github.com/charmbracelet/log"
9		"github.com/charmbracelet/ssh"
10		"github.com/charmbracelet/wish"
11		"github.com/charmbracelet/wish/activeterm"
12		"github.com/charmbracelet/wish/bubbletea"
13		"github.com/charmbracelet/wish/logging"
14		"github.com/muesli/termenv"
15		gossh "golang.org/x/crypto/ssh"
16	
17		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/auth"
18		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/chat"
19		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store"
20		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/tui"
21	)
22	
23	type Config struct {
24		Addr    string
25		HostKey string
26	}
27	
28	func New(cfg Config, st *store.Store, broker *chat.Broker) (*ssh.Server, error) {
29		return wish.NewServer(
30			wish.WithAddress(cfg.Addr),
31			wish.WithHostKeyPath(cfg.HostKey),
32			wish.WithPasswordAuth(PasswordAuth(st)),
33			withReservedNoneAuth(),
34			wish.WithMiddleware(
35				bubbletea.MiddlewareWithProgramHandler(makeProgramHandler(st, broker), termenv.ANSI256),
36				activeterm.Middleware(),
37				logging.Middleware(),
38			),
39		)
40	}
41	
42	// withReservedNoneAuth enables SSH "none" authentication for the two
43	// reserved usernames where a password is meaningless: `guest` (read-only
44	// spectator) and `new` (in-TUI registration). Other usernames still go
45	// through the password callback.
46	//
47	// charmbracelet/ssh only sets NoClientAuth=true when no other handlers
48	// are configured (see its server.go config()), so we override its
49	// ServerConfigCallback to force NoClientAuth=true with a per-username
50	// callback. The library still adds HostKey + PasswordCallback on top of
51	// our config, so password auth keeps working for everyone else.
52	//
53	// Effect: `ssh guest@host -p 2222` and `ssh new@host -p 2222` connect
54	// with no password prompt. `ssh alice@host -p 2222` still prompts.
55	func withReservedNoneAuth() ssh.Option {
56		return func(s *ssh.Server) error {
57			s.ServerConfigCallback = func(ctx ssh.Context) *gossh.ServerConfig {
58				return &gossh.ServerConfig{
59					NoClientAuth: true,
60					NoClientAuthCallback: func(conn gossh.ConnMetadata) (*gossh.Permissions, error) {
61						switch conn.User() {
62						case auth.ReservedUsernameGuest, auth.ReservedUsernameNew:
63							return nil, nil
64						}
65						return nil, errors.New("auth required")
66					},
67				}
68			}
69			return nil
70		}
71	}
72	
73	// makeProgramHandler is the wish ProgramHandler. It owns *tea.Program
74	// creation so we can hand the pointer to the chat broker for live message
75	// delivery, and arrange Unregister when the SSH session ends.
76	func makeProgramHandler(st *store.Store, broker *chat.Broker) bubbletea.ProgramHandler {
77		return func(sess ssh.Session) *tea.Program {
78			ctx := sess.Context()
79	
80			// Register-mode: SSH user was "new". No DB user, no broker registration.
81			// `none` auth bypasses our PasswordHandler, so ctxKeyRegister isn't set
82			// when a client connects via the none-auth path — fall back to inspecting
83			// sess.User() directly.
84			reg, _ := ctx.Value(ctxKeyRegister).(bool)
85			if reg || sess.User() == auth.ReservedUsernameNew {
86				deps := tui.Deps{Store: st, Broker: broker, IsRegister: true}
87				m := tui.NewRoot(deps)
88				return tea.NewProgram(m, append(bubbletea.MakeOptions(sess), tea.WithAltScreen())...)
89			}
90	
91			uid, _ := ctx.Value(ctxKeyUserID).(int64)
92			if uid == 0 {
93				// SSH `none` auth path: NoClientAuthCallback succeeded for
94				// guest but didn't run our PasswordHandler, so user_id was
95				// never stashed. Resolve the guest row here. Any other
96				// username with uid==0 means a misconfiguration — reject.
97				if sess.User() == auth.ReservedUsernameGuest {
98					guest, err := st.Users().GetByUserID(context.Background(), auth.ReservedUsernameGuest)
99					if err == nil && guest.Role == store.RoleGuest {
100						uid = guest.ID
101					}
102				}
103				if uid == 0 {
104					log.Warn("session has no user_id; rejecting", "remote", sess.RemoteAddr(), "user", sess.User())
105					return nil
106				}
107			}
108			user, err := st.Users().GetByID(context.Background(), uid)
109			if err != nil {
110				log.Error("load user", "id", uid, "err", err)
111				return nil
112			}
113	
114			deps := tui.Deps{Store: st, User: user, Broker: broker}
115			if user.MustChangePassword {
116				deps.MustChangePassword = true
117			}
118			m := tui.NewRoot(deps)
119			p := tea.NewProgram(m, append(bubbletea.MakeOptions(sess), tea.WithAltScreen())...)
120	
121			// Guests are read-only spectators. We deliberately don't register
122			// them in the broker — multiple `ssh guest@...` clients would
123			// collide on the same user.ID, they can't reply to water balloons,
124			// and they don't appear in the online list.
125			if user.Role == store.RoleGuest {
126				log.Info("guest session", "host", deps.User.UserID)
127				return p
128			}
129	
130			// Hook session into the broker so other users can send to us.
131			cs := &chat.Session{UserID: user.ID, UserIDStr: user.UserID, Program: p}
132			broker.Register(cs)
133			log.Info("session registered", "user", user.UserID, "online", len(broker.OnlineList()))
134	
135			// Drain unread water balloons from DB and replay them as toasts.
136			// We do this *before* the program starts so the messages land in
137			// the model's first Update(); tea.Program.Send buffers if needed.
138			go func() {
139				unread, err := st.WaterBalloons().ListUnreadFor(context.Background(), user.ID)
140				if err != nil {
141					log.Warn("drain unread WBs", "user", user.UserID, "err", err)
142					return
143				}
144				for _, w := range unread {
145					p.Send(tui.WBIncomingMsg{ID: w.ID, FromUserID: w.FromUserIDStr, Body: w.Body})
146					_ = st.WaterBalloons().MarkRead(context.Background(), w.ID)
147				}
148			}()
149	
150			// On session end, unregister so we stop trying to deliver to a dead Program.
151			go func() {
152				<-ctx.Done()
153				broker.Unregister(user.ID, p)
154				log.Info("session unregistered", "user", user.UserID, "online", len(broker.OnlineList()))
155			}()
156	
157			return p
158		}
159	}
160
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
1	package tui
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"errors"
6		"time"
7	
8		tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"
9	
10		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/chat"
11		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store"
12	)
13	
14	// Deps bundles everything every screen may need. Pass-by-value into
15	// constructors keeps screens self-contained.
16	type Deps struct {
17		Store              *store.Store
18		Broker             *chat.Broker // nil during register
19		User               *store.User  // nil iff IsRegister
20		IsRegister         bool
21		MustChangePassword bool // true iff user must rotate password before reaching the main menu
22	}
23	
24	// Root is the top-level tea.Model. It owns the active sub-model and
25	// handles cross-cutting concerns: window size, global hotkeys, navigation,
26	// and the toast footer.
27	type Root struct {
28		deps   Deps
29		sub    tea.Model
30		width  int
31		height int
32	
33		toast      string
34		toastUntil time.Time
35	
36		// activeScreen tracks which Screen the current sub corresponds to, so
37		// the help overlay can render a context-aware keymap. Set in NewRoot
38		// for the bootstrap sub and in navigate for every subsequent swap.
39		activeScreen Screen
40		// helpVisible is true while the help overlay should render in place of
41		// the sub view. Toggled on '?' and dismissed by any key.
42		helpVisible bool
43	}
44	
45	const toastDuration = 3 * time.Second
46	
47	// NewRoot builds the Root with the right initial sub-screen.
48	//
49	// Priority: IsRegister wins over MustChangePassword wins over the regular
50	// authenticated main menu — these flags are mutually exclusive in practice
51	// (set by the SSH password callback / makeProgramHandler).
52	func NewRoot(deps Deps) Root {
53		r := Root{deps: deps}
54		switch {
55		case deps.IsRegister:
56			r.sub = newRegisterModel(deps)
57			// No Screen const for register; help is suppressed via deps.IsRegister.
58		case deps.MustChangePassword:
59			r.sub = newPasswordChangeModel(deps)
60			r.activeScreen = ScreenPasswordChange
61		default:
62			r.sub = newMainMenuModel(deps)
63			r.activeScreen = ScreenMainMenu
64		}
65		return r
66	}
67	
68	func (m Root) Init() tea.Cmd {
69		return m.sub.Init()
70	}
71	
72	func (m Root) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
73		switch msg := msg.(type) {
74		case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
75			m.width = msg.Width
76			m.height = msg.Height
77			var cmd tea.Cmd
78			m.sub, cmd = m.sub.Update(msg)
79			return m, cmd
80	
81		case tea.KeyMsg:
82			// Global ctrl+c always quits.
83			if msg.String() == "ctrl+c" {
84				return m, tea.Quit
85			}
86			// Help overlay: any key dismisses, swallow the keypress so the
87			// sub doesn't act on it. ctrl+c above is the deliberate exception.
88			if m.helpVisible {
89				m.helpVisible = false
90				return m, nil
91			}
92			// '?' opens the help overlay on screens that aren't typing-forms.
93			if msg.String() == "?" && helpAvailable(m.deps, m.activeScreen) {
94				m.helpVisible = true
95				return m, nil
96			}
97			// Global Ctrl+U opens water balloon inbox (logged-in only).
98			if msg.String() == "ctrl+u" && !m.deps.IsRegister {
99				return m.navigate(NavigateMsg{To: ScreenWBInbox})
100			}
101	
102		case NavigateMsg:
103			return m.navigate(msg)
104	
105		case ErrorMsg:
106			if msg.Err != nil {
107				m.setToast("⚠ " + msg.Err.Error())
108			}
109			return m, nil
110	
111		case WBIncomingMsg:
112			// Suppress the toast when the active screen is a wbThreadModel
113			// pointed at this exact counterparty — the thread itself appends
114			// the message, so the toast would be redundant noise.
115			showToast := true
116			if m.activeScreen == ScreenWBThread {
117				if tm, ok := m.sub.(wbThreadModel); ok && tm.matchesCounterparty(msg.FromUserID) {
118					showToast = false
119				}
120			}
121			if showToast {
122				m.setToast("💧 " + msg.FromUserID + ": " + msg.Body)
123			}
124			// Mark this WB as read so it doesn't replay on reconnect. (The
125			// thread also marks it read — both paths are idempotent.)
126			if m.deps.Store != nil && msg.ID != 0 {
127				go func(id int64) {
128					_ = m.deps.Store.WaterBalloons().MarkRead(context.Background(), id)
129				}(msg.ID)
130			}
131			// fall through so the active screen can also react
132		}
133	
134		var cmd tea.Cmd
135		m.sub, cmd = m.sub.Update(msg)
136		return m, cmd
137	}
138	
139	func (m Root) View() string {
140		if m.helpVisible {
141			// Replace the sub view entirely so the keymap reads cleanly. Toast
142			// suppressed too — would distract from a help screen.
143			return renderHelp(m.activeScreen)
144		}
145		v := m.sub.View()
146		if m.toast != "" && time.Now().Before(m.toastUntil) {
147			v += "\n" + StyleToast.Render(m.toast)
148		}
149		return v
150	}
151	
152	func (m *Root) setToast(s string) {
153		m.toast = s
154		m.toastUntil = time.Now().Add(toastDuration)
155	}
156	
157	// guestWriteBlocked returns an ErrorMsg cmd if a guest is trying to enter
158	// a write-capable screen, otherwise nil. Layer 1 of the read-only path.
159	func (m Root) guestWriteBlocked(to Screen) tea.Cmd {
160		if m.deps.User == nil || m.deps.User.Role != store.RoleGuest {
161			return nil
162		}
163		switch to {
164		case ScreenPostCompose, ScreenWBCompose, ScreenMailCompose, ScreenAdminUsers, ScreenArticleEdit:
165			return func() tea.Msg { return ErrorMsg{Err: errors.New("guest 為唯讀帳號 (read-only)")} }
166		}
167		return nil
168	}
169	
170	// navigateNonAdminBlocked returns an ErrorMsg cmd if a non-admin tries
171	// to reach the admin user-management screen.
172	func (m Root) navigateNonAdminBlocked(to Screen) tea.Cmd {
173		if to != ScreenAdminUsers {
174			return nil
175		}
176		if m.deps.User != nil && m.deps.User.Role == store.RoleAdmin {
177			return nil
178		}
179		return func() tea.Msg { return ErrorMsg{Err: errors.New("管理員專用 (admin only)")} }
180	}
181	
182	func (m Root) navigate(n NavigateMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
183		if cmd := m.guestWriteBlocked(n.To); cmd != nil {
184			return m, cmd
185		}
186		if cmd := m.navigateNonAdminBlocked(n.To); cmd != nil {
187			return m, cmd
188		}
189		var sub tea.Model
190		switch n.To {
191		case ScreenMainMenu:
192			sub = newMainMenuModel(m.deps)
193		case ScreenBoardList:
194			sub = newBoardListModel(m.deps)
195		case ScreenBoardView:
196			sub = newBoardViewModel(m.deps, n.BoardID)
197		case ScreenArticleView:
198			sub = newArticleViewModel(m.deps, n.ArticleID)
199		case ScreenPostCompose:
200			sub = newPostComposeModel(m.deps, n.BoardID)
201		case ScreenWBInbox:
202			sub = newWBInboxModel(m.deps)
203		case ScreenWBCompose:
204			sub = newWBComposeModel(m.deps, n.Recipient)
205		case ScreenWBThread:
206			sub = newWBThreadModel(m.deps, n.CounterpartyUserID)
207		case ScreenOnline:
208			sub = newOnlineModel(m.deps)
209		case ScreenMailInbox:
210			sub = newMailInboxModel(m.deps)
211		case ScreenMailThread:
212			sub = newMailThreadModel(m.deps, n.MailThreadID)
213		case ScreenMailCompose:
214			sub = newMailComposeModel(m.deps, n.Recipient, n.MailID)
215		case ScreenPasswordChange:
216			sub = newPasswordChangeModel(m.deps)
217		case ScreenAdminUsers:
218			sub = newAdminUsersModel(m.deps)
219		case ScreenArticleEdit:
220			sub = newArticleEditModel(m.deps, n.ArticleID)
221		case ScreenArticleExport:
222			sub = newArticleExportModel(m.deps, n.ArticleID)
223		case ScreenBoardSplash:
224			sub = newBoardSplashModel(m.deps, n.BoardID)
225		case ScreenBoardBannerEdit:
226			sub = newBoardBannerEditModel(m.deps, n.BoardID)
227		default:
228			// Unknown screen — leave sub untouched. Later steps add cases.
229			return m, nil
230		}
231		m.sub = sub
232		m.activeScreen = n.To
233		cmd := sub.Init()
234		if m.width > 0 {
235			// Forward current size to the new sub so it can lay out immediately.
236			var cmd2 tea.Cmd
237			m.sub, cmd2 = m.sub.Update(tea.WindowSizeMsg{Width: m.width, Height: m.height})
238			if cmd2 != nil {
239				cmd = tea.Batch(cmd, cmd2)
240			}
241		}
242		return m, cmd
243	}
244
```

```text
1	package tui
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"strings"
6	
7		"github.com/charmbracelet/bubbles/textarea"
8		"github.com/charmbracelet/bubbles/textinput"
9		tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"
10	
11		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/markdown"
12	)
13	
14	const (
15		composeFocusTitle = iota
16		composeFocusBody
17	)
18	
19	type postComposeModel struct {
20		deps    Deps
21		boardID int64
22		title   textinput.Model
23		body    textarea.Model
24		focus   int
25		width   int
26		height  int
27		err     string
28	}
29	
30	func newPostComposeModel(deps Deps, boardID int64) postComposeModel {
31		ti := textinput.New()
32		ti.Placeholder = "標題 title"
33		ti.CharLimit = 64
34		ti.Width = 60
35		ti.Focus()
36	
37		ta := textarea.New()
38		ta.Placeholder = "內容 body…"
39		ta.CharLimit = 8000
40		ta.SetWidth(72)
41		ta.SetHeight(12)
42	
43		return postComposeModel{
44			deps:    deps,
45			boardID: boardID,
46			title:   ti,
47			body:    ta,
48			focus:   composeFocusTitle,
49		}
50	}
51	
52	func (m postComposeModel) Init() tea.Cmd { return textinput.Blink }
53	
54	func (m postComposeModel) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
55		switch msg := msg.(type) {
56		case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
57			m.width, m.height = msg.Width, msg.Height
58			bodyW := max(40, msg.Width-8)
59			bodyH := max(8, msg.Height-12)
60			m.body.SetWidth(bodyW)
61			m.body.SetHeight(bodyH)
62			m.title.Width = max(20, msg.Width-12)
63			return m, nil
64	
65		case tea.KeyMsg:
66			switch msg.String() {
67			case "esc":
68				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenBoardView, BoardID: m.boardID} }
69			case "tab":
70				m.toggleFocus()
71				return m, nil
72			case "ctrl+s":
73				return m.submit()
74			case "ctrl+i":
75				m.importFromBody()
76				return m, nil
77			}
78		}
79	
80		var cmd tea.Cmd
81		if m.focus == composeFocusTitle {
82			// In title field, Enter advances to body.
83			if k, ok := msg.(tea.KeyMsg); ok && k.String() == "enter" {
84				m.toggleFocus()
85				return m, nil
86			}
87			m.title, cmd = m.title.Update(msg)
88		} else {
89			m.body, cmd = m.body.Update(msg)
90		}
91		return m, cmd
92	}
93	
94	func (m *postComposeModel) toggleFocus() {
95		if m.focus == composeFocusTitle {
96			m.focus = composeFocusBody
97			m.title.Blur()
98			m.body.Focus()
99		} else {
100			m.focus = composeFocusTitle
101			m.body.Blur()
102			m.title.Focus()
103		}
104	}
105	
106	// importFromBody parses the current body as markdown and prefills title +
107	// body from any frontmatter found. If the body has no frontmatter it's
108	// left alone (with a helpful error). Pushes in the parsed input are
109	// silently ignored — see plan: import via Ctrl+I always credits the
110	// current user as author and never restores foreign-authored pushes.
111	func (m *postComposeModel) importFromBody() {
112		raw := m.body.Value()
113		if strings.TrimSpace(raw) == "" {
114			m.err = "貼上 markdown（含 --- frontmatter ---）後再按 Ctrl+I"
115			return
116		}
117		parsed, err := markdown.Parse(raw)
118		if err != nil {
119			m.err = "parse: " + err.Error()
120			return
121		}
122		if parsed.Title == "" && parsed.Body == strings.TrimRight(raw, "\n") {
123			// Parser didn't find frontmatter — body is verbatim. Tell the
124			// user so they don't think Ctrl+I silently no-op'd.
125			m.err = "找不到 frontmatter（請貼上開頭含 --- ... --- 的 markdown）"
126			return
127		}
128		if parsed.Title != "" {
129			m.title.SetValue(parsed.Title)
130		}
131		m.body.SetValue(parsed.Body)
132		m.err = ""
133	}
134	
135	func (m postComposeModel) submit() (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
136		title := strings.TrimSpace(m.title.Value())
137		body := m.body.Value()
138		if title == "" {
139			m.err = "title is required"
140			return m, nil
141		}
142		if strings.TrimSpace(body) == "" {
143			m.err = "body is required"
144			return m, nil
145		}
146		ctx := context.Background()
147		u := m.deps.User
148		art, err := m.deps.Store.Articles().Create(ctx, m.boardID, u.ID, u.UserID, title, body)
149		if err != nil {
150			m.err = err.Error()
151			return m, nil
152		}
153		if err := m.deps.Store.Users().IncrementPosts(ctx, u.ID); err != nil {
154			m.err = err.Error()
155			return m, nil
156		}
157		// Broadcast to other live sessions; recipients filter by BoardID and
158		// re-fetch the canonical list (mirrors the PushAddedMsg pattern).
159		if m.deps.Broker != nil {
160			m.deps.Broker.SendToAll(u.ID, ArticleAddedMsg{
161				BoardID:      m.boardID,
162				ArticleID:    art.ID,
163				AuthorUserID: u.UserID,
164				Title:        art.Title,
165			})
166		}
167		return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenBoardView, BoardID: m.boardID} }
168	}
169	
170	func (m postComposeModel) View() string {
171		var b strings.Builder
172		b.WriteString("\n")
173		b.WriteString(StyleHeader.Render(" 發表新文章 New Post "))
174		b.WriteString("\n\n")
175	
176		b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("Title:") + "\n")
177		b.WriteString("  " + m.title.View() + "\n\n")
178	
179		b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("Body:") + "\n")
180		b.WriteString("  " + indent(m.body.View(), "  ") + "\n")
181	
182		if m.err != "" {
183			b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleError.Render("⚠ "+m.err) + "\n")
184		}
185		b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleHelp.Render("Tab switch field · Enter (in title) → body · Ctrl+S send · Ctrl+I import markdown · Esc cancel"))
186		b.WriteString("\n")
187		return b.String()
188	}
189	
190	func indent(s, prefix string) string {
191		lines := strings.Split(s, "\n")
192		for i, ln := range lines {
193			if i == 0 {
194				continue
195			}
196			lines[i] = prefix + ln
197		}
198		return strings.Join(lines, "\n")
199	}
200

1	package tui
2	
3	import (
4		"fmt"
5		"strings"
6	
7		tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"
8	
9		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store"
10	)
11	
12	type menuItem struct {
13		label string
14		hint  string
15		to    Screen
16	}
17	
18	type mainMenuModel struct {
19		deps   Deps
20		items  []menuItem
21		cursor int
22	}
23	
24	func newMainMenuModel(deps Deps) mainMenuModel {
25		items := []menuItem{
26			{label: "看板列表 Boards", hint: "browse and read articles", to: ScreenBoardList},
27			{label: "水球 Water Balloons", hint: "private messages with online users", to: ScreenWBInbox},
28			{label: "線上使用者 Online", hint: "see who's logged in", to: ScreenOnline},
29			{label: "信箱 Mail", hint: "persistent threaded mail", to: ScreenMailInbox},
30		}
31		// Admin gets an extra entry; positioned before the Quit row so the
32		// numeric shortcut for Quit always lands on the LAST item regardless
33		// of role (the "5 quits" shortcut becomes "6 quits" for admin).
34		if deps.User != nil && deps.User.Role == store.RoleAdmin {
35			items = append(items, menuItem{
36				label: "管理 Admin",
37				hint:  "manage user roles",
38				to:    ScreenAdminUsers,
39			})
40		}
41		items = append(items, menuItem{label: "離線 Quit", hint: "disconnect", to: -1})
42		return mainMenuModel{deps: deps, items: items}
43	}
44	
45	func (m mainMenuModel) Init() tea.Cmd { return nil }
46	
47	func (m mainMenuModel) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
48		if k, ok := msg.(tea.KeyMsg); ok {
49			switch k.String() {
50			case "q", "esc":
51				return m, tea.Quit
52			case "up", "k":
53				if m.cursor > 0 {
54					m.cursor--
55				}
56				return m, nil
57			case "down", "j":
58				if m.cursor < len(m.items)-1 {
59					m.cursor++
60				}
61				return m, nil
62			case "enter", " ", "right", "l":
63				it := m.items[m.cursor]
64				if it.to == -1 {
65					return m, tea.Quit
66				}
67				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: it.to} }
68			case "1", "2", "3", "4", "5", "6":
69				idx := int(k.String()[0] - '1')
70				if idx < len(m.items) {
71					it := m.items[idx]
72					if it.to == -1 {
73						return m, tea.Quit
74					}
75					return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: it.to} }
76				}
77			}
78		}
79		return m, nil
80	}
81	
82	func (m mainMenuModel) View() string {
83		u := m.deps.User
84		nick := u.Nickname
85		if nick == "" {
86			nick = u.UserID
87		}
88	
89		var b strings.Builder
90		b.WriteString("\n")
91		b.WriteString(StyleHeader.Render(fmt.Sprintf(" SSH-BBS · %s (%s) ", nick, u.UserID)))
92		b.WriteString("\n\n")
93	
94		if u.LastLoginAt.Valid {
95			b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render(fmt.Sprintf("上次登入 %s · 累計登入 %d 次 · 發文 %d 篇",
96				u.LastLoginAt.Time.Format("2006-01-02 15:04"),
97				u.NumLogins,
98				u.NumPosts,
99			)))
100			b.WriteString("\n\n")
101		}
102	
103		b.WriteString("  ")
104		b.WriteString(StyleHeader.Render(" 主選單 Main Menu "))
105		b.WriteString("\n\n")
106	
107		for i, it := range m.items {
108			marker := "  "
109			row := fmt.Sprintf("  %d. %s", i+1, it.label)
110			if i == m.cursor {
111				marker = "▸ "
112				row = StyleHighlight.Render(fmt.Sprintf(" %d. %-32s ", i+1, it.label))
113			}
114			b.WriteString(marker + row)
115			if it.hint != "" {
116				b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render(it.hint))
117			}
118			b.WriteString("\n")
119		}
120	
121		b.WriteString("\n  ")
122		b.WriteString(StyleHelp.Render(fmt.Sprintf("↑/↓ j/k move · Enter/→/l choose · 1-%d jump · ? help · q quit", len(m.items))))
123		b.WriteString("\n")
124		return b.String()
125	}
126

10	// HelpEntry is one row in a screen's keymap. Keys is the displayed shortcut
11	// (e.g. "Enter/→/l"), Desc the human-readable action.
12	type HelpEntry struct {
13		Keys string
14		Desc string
15	}
16	
17	// helpHeader is the optional title row shown above the keymap. Empty for
18	// most screens; some screens use it to clarify mode-specific keys.

21		Entries []HelpEntry

24	// screenHelp maps a Screen constant to the keys we want the help overlay

155				{"Esc/←/h", "back to main menu"},

157		}},

159			Heading: "Export article",
160			Entries: []HelpEntry{
161				{"1", "plain body"},
162				{"2", "body + pushes"},
163				{"3", "save to data/exports/<userid>/"},
164				{"c", "OSC52 copy to clipboard"},
165				{"Esc/←/h/q", "back"},
166			},
167		}},
168		ScreenBoardSplash: {{
169			Heading: "Board banner splash",
170			Entries: []HelpEntry{
171				{"any key", "return to board view"},
172			},
173		}},
174	}
175	
176	// helpGlobals are the cross-screen keys shown on every help page so users
177	// don't need to discover them per screen.
178	var helpGlobals = []HelpEntry{
179		{"?", "show this help (any key dismisses)"},
180		{"Ctrl+U", "open 水球 inbox (logged-in only)"},
181		{"Ctrl+C", "disconnect"},
182	}
183	
184	// isFormScreen reports whether a screen is a text-entry form whose
185	// textinput / textarea must receive '?' as a literal character. The help
186	// overlay is suppressed on these screens; the bottom help-line each form
187	// already renders is the source of truth there.
188	func isFormScreen(s Screen) bool {
189		switch s {
190		case ScreenPostCompose,
191			ScreenWBCompose,
192			ScreenMailCompose,
193			ScreenArticleEdit,
194			ScreenBoardBannerEdit,
195			ScreenPasswordChange:
196			return true
197		}
198		return false
199	}
200	
201	// helpAvailable reports whether the help overlay should respond to '?' on
202	// this screen. False for form screens and during register/MustChangePassword
203	// (those flows start before any Screen const is meaningful).
204	func helpAvailable(deps Deps, s Screen) bool {
205		if deps.IsRegister || deps.MustChangePassword {
206			return false
207		}
208		return !isFormScreen(s)
209	}
210	
211	// helpBox is the lipgloss frame around the help body. Width is set per-call
212	// so the box hugs the rendered content rather than spanning the terminal.
213	var helpBox = lipgloss.NewStyle().
214		Border(lipgloss.RoundedBorder()).
215		BorderForeground(lipgloss.Color("63")).
216		Padding(1, 2)
217	
218	// renderHelp builds the full-screen help overlay for the given screen.
219	// Includes per-screen entries (or a fallback message) plus the globals.
220	func renderHelp(s Screen) string {
221		var b strings.Builder
222		b.WriteString(StyleHeader.Render(" 鍵盤捷徑說明 Help ") + "\n\n")
223	
224		sections, ok := screenHelp[s]
225		if !ok {
226			b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("(no per-screen keymap registered for this screen)") + "\n\n")
227		} else {
228			for i, sec := range sections {
229				if i > 0 {
230					b.WriteString("\n")
231				}
232				if sec.Heading != "" {
233					b.WriteString(StyleDim.Render("  "+sec.Heading) + "\n")
234				}
235				for _, e := range sec.Entries {
236					b.WriteString(formatHelpRow(e) + "\n")
237				}
238			}
239			b.WriteString("\n")
240		}
241	
242		b.WriteString(StyleDim.Render("  Global") + "\n")
243		for _, e := range helpGlobals {
244			b.WriteString(formatHelpRow(e) + "\n")
245		}
246	
247		b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleHelp.Render("press any key to dismiss"))
248	
249		return helpBox.Render(b.String())
250	}
251	
252	// formatHelpRow pads keys to a stable column width so the descriptions
253	// align — uses the runewidth-aware PadRight because keys often contain
254	// arrows / CJK glyphs.
255	func formatHelpRow(e HelpEntry) string {
256		const keysW = 22
257		return fmt.Sprintf("    %s  %s", PadRight(e.Keys, keysW), e.Desc)
258	}
