 `./internal/store/migrations/0001_init.sql`

```text
1	-- Initial schema for the SSH BBS.
2	-- All tables use auto-increment INTEGER PRIMARY KEY plus a unique business
3	-- key where appropriate. We deliberately do NOT mimic pttbbs's recno-based
4	-- addressing (a holdover from mmap'd binary .DIR files) — a relational DB
5	-- removes the recno-tombstone fragility documented in pttbbs.
6	
7	PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;
8	
9	CREATE TABLE users (
10	    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
11	    user_id         TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE COLLATE NOCASE, -- userec_t.userid
12	    password_hash   TEXT NOT NULL,                       -- bcrypt; replaces userec_t.passwd (DES)
13	    nickname        TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',            -- userec_t.nickname
14	    realname        TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',            -- userec_t.realname
15	    email           TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',            -- userec_t.email
16	    num_logins      INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,          -- userec_t.numlogindays (loose)
17	    num_posts       INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,          -- userec_t.numposts
18	    last_login_at   DATETIME,                            -- userec_t.lastlogin
19	    last_host       TEXT,                                -- userec_t.lasthost
20	    created_at      DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
21	);
22	
23	CREATE TABLE boards (
24	    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
25	    name            TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE COLLATE NOCASE, -- boardheader_t.brdname
26	    title           TEXT NOT NULL,                       -- boardheader_t.title
27	    description     TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
28	    bm              TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',            -- boardheader_t.BM (comma-sep)
29	    attr            INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,          -- boardheader_t.brdattr (P1+)
30	    created_at      DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
31	);
32	
33	CREATE TABLE articles (
34	    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
35	    board_id        INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES boards(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
36	    author_id       INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
37	    author_userid   TEXT NOT NULL,                       -- denormalized (fileheader_t.owner)
38	    title           TEXT NOT NULL,                       -- fileheader_t.title
39	    body            TEXT NOT NULL,
40	    recommend_score INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,          -- cached sum of pushes (+1) - boos (-1)
41	    filemode        INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,          -- fileheader_t.filemode (P1+)
42	    created_at      DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
43	);
44	CREATE INDEX idx_articles_board_created ON articles(board_id, created_at DESC);
45	
46	CREATE TABLE pushes (
47	    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
48	    article_id      INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES articles(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
49	    user_id         INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
50	    user_userid     TEXT NOT NULL,                       -- denormalized
51	    kind            TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (kind IN ('push','boo','arrow')), -- 推/噓/→
52	    body            TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
53	    created_at      DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
54	);
55	CREATE INDEX idx_pushes_article ON pushes(article_id, id);
56	
57	CREATE TABLE water_balloons (
58	    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
59	    from_user_id    INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
60	    from_userid     TEXT NOT NULL,                       -- denormalized
61	    to_user_id      INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
62	    body            TEXT NOT NULL,
63	    delivered_live  INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
64	    read_at         DATETIME,
65	    created_at      DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
66	);
67	CREATE INDEX idx_wb_to_unread ON water_balloons(to_user_id, read_at);
68
```

```text
1	package tui
2	
3	import (

8		"github.com/charmbracelet/bubbles/textinput"

14	type articleViewModel struct {
15		deps    Deps
16		article *store.Article
17		pushes  []*store.Push
18		width   int
19		height  int
20		scroll  int
21		loadErr error
22	
23		// rendered is the article body after running through glamour. We cache
24		// because (a) glamour's first call loads chroma's syntax highlighter
25		// (~tens of ms) and (b) View() is called on every keystroke. Re-render
26		// only when the article changes or the window width changes.
27		rendered      string
28		renderedWidth int
29	
30		// push input state
31		pushing  bool
32		pushKind store.PushKind
33		pushIn   textinput.Model
34		err      string
35	
36		// delete state. pendingDelete: showing the y/N confirm overlay.
37		// pushCursor: -1 = no push selected (D targets article); 0..len-1 = D targets that push.
38		pendingDelete bool
39		pushCursor    int
40	
41		// pickingCommentsMode: showing the 1/2/3 picker overlay for the
42		// mod-only M shortcut (open / arrows-only / locked).
43		pickingCommentsMode bool
44	}
45	
46	// renderBody runs the article body through glamour with word-wrap set
47	// to the current viewport.
48	func (m *articleViewModel) renderBody() {
49		if m.article == nil {
50			m.rendered = ""
51			m.renderedWidth = 0
52			return
53		}
54		width := m.width - 4
55		if width < 40 {
56			width = glamourFallbackWidth
57		}
58		m.rendered = renderMarkdown(m.article.Body, width)
59		m.renderedWidth = width
60	}
61	
62	func newArticleViewModel(deps Deps, articleID int64) articleViewModel {
63		ctx := context.Background()
64		a, err := deps.Store.Articles().GetByID(ctx, articleID)
65		if err != nil {
66			return articleViewModel{deps: deps, loadErr: err}
67		}
68		pushes, err := deps.Store.Pushes().ListByArticle(ctx, articleID)
69	
70		ti := textinput.New()
71		ti.Placeholder = "comment…"
72		ti.CharLimit = 80
73		ti.Width = 60
74	
75		m := articleViewModel{deps: deps, article: a, pushes: pushes, loadErr: err, pushIn: ti, pushCursor: -1}
76		// Render with the fallback width so the body is readable even before
77		// the first WindowSizeMsg arrives — Update re-renders on resize.
78		m.renderBody()
79		return m
80	}
81	
82	// canDeleteArticle returns true if the current user may delete the loaded
83	// article (author, or mod-or-above). Used both as a permission gate at
84	// the key handler and to decide whether to render the help-line hint.
85	func (m articleViewModel) canDeleteArticle() bool {
86		if m.deps.User == nil || m.article == nil {
87			return false
88		}
89		if m.deps.User.ID == m.article.AuthorID {
90			return true
91		}
92		return m.deps.User.Role.AtLeast(store.RoleMod)
93	}
94	
95	// canDeletePush mirrors canDeleteArticle for the push at index idx.
96	func (m articleViewModel) canDeletePush(idx int) bool {
97		if m.deps.User == nil || idx < 0 || idx >= len(m.pushes) {
98			return false
99		}
100		if m.deps.User.ID == m.pushes[idx].UserID {
101			return true
102		}
103		return m.deps.User.Role.AtLeast(store.RoleMod)
104	}
105	
106	// canPush returns true if the current user is allowed to add new pushes.
107	// Guests can read but not write.
108	func (m articleViewModel) canPush() bool {
109		return m.deps.User != nil && m.deps.User.Role != store.RoleGuest
110	}
111	
112	// canEditArticle mirrors canDeleteArticle: the author may edit their own
113	// post, mods and admins may edit anyone's.
114	func (m articleViewModel) canEditArticle() bool {
115		if m.deps.User == nil || m.article == nil {
116			return false
117		}
118		if m.deps.User.ID == m.article.AuthorID {
119			return true
120		}
121		return m.deps.User.Role.AtLeast(store.RoleMod)
122	}
123	
124	// canSetCommentsMode gates the M shortcut. Mirrors board-view canPin —
125	// pinning and locking comments are both moderation actions that do NOT
126	// admit the article author. Mod+ only.
127	func (m articleViewModel) canSetCommentsMode() bool {
128		return m.deps.User != nil && m.deps.User.Role.AtLeast(store.RoleMod)
129	}
130	
131	func (m articleViewModel) Init() tea.Cmd { return nil }
132	
133	func (m articleViewModel) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
134		switch msg := msg.(type) {
135		case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
136			m.width, m.height = msg.Width, msg.Height
137			// Only re-render when the width target actually changes — height
138			// changes don't affect glamour wrap.
139			newWidth := max(msg.Width-4, 40)
140			if newWidth != m.renderedWidth {
141				m.renderBody()
142			}
143			return m, nil
144	
145		case PushAddedMsg:
146			if m.article != nil && msg.ArticleID == m.article.ID {
147				ctx := context.Background()
148				// Re-fetch from DB so timestamps and recommend_score reflect
149				// the canonical state, not the just-synthesized broker payload.
150				if pushes, err := m.deps.Store.Pushes().ListByArticle(ctx, m.article.ID); err == nil {
151					m.pushes = pushes
152				}
153				if a, err := m.deps.Store.Articles().GetByID(ctx, m.article.ID); err == nil {
154					m.article = a
155				}
156			}
157	
158		case ArticleUpdatedMsg:
159			if m.article != nil && msg.ArticleID == m.article.ID {
160				// Edit happened in another session — refetch the canonical
161				// title/body so we render the new text.
162				if a, err := m.deps.Store.Articles().GetByID(context.Background(), m.article.ID); err == nil {
163					m.article = a
164					m.renderBody()
165					// Reset scroll if the new body is shorter than where we
166					// were parked.
167					if m.scroll > m.bodyLineCount() {
168						m.scroll = 0
169					}
170				}
171			}
172			return m, nil
173	
174		case ArticleCommentsModeChangedMsg:
175			if m.article != nil && msg.ArticleID == m.article.ID {
176				// Mode flip happened in another session — refetch so the badge
177				// and the +/-/= gates pick up the new value. No body re-render
178				// needed (only the header changes).
179				if a, err := m.deps.Store.Articles().GetByID(context.Background(), m.article.ID); err == nil {
180					m.article = a
181				}
182			}
183			return m, nil
184	
185		case tea.KeyMsg:
186			if m.pushing {
187				return m.updatePushInput(msg)
188			}
189			if m.pendingDelete {
190				return m.updateDeleteConfirm(msg)
191			}
192			if m.pickingCommentsMode {
193				return m.updateCommentsModePicker(msg)
194			}
195			switch msg.String() {
196			case "esc", "backspace", "left", "h":
197				boardID := int64(0)
198				if m.article != nil {
199					boardID = m.article.BoardID
200				}
201				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenBoardView, BoardID: boardID} }
202			case "Q":
203				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenMainMenu} }
204			case "up", "k":
205				if m.scroll > 0 {
206					m.scroll--
207				}
208			case "down", "j":
209				m.scroll++
210			case "pgup", "b":
211				m.scroll = max(0, m.scroll-10)
212			case "pgdown", " ":
213				m.scroll += 10
214			case "g":
215				m.scroll = 0
216			case "G":
217				m.scroll = max(0, m.bodyLineCount()-m.viewportLines())
218			case "[":
219				if m.article == nil {
220					return m, nil
221				}
222				prev, _, err := m.deps.Store.Articles().NeighboursOf(context.Background(), m.article.BoardID, m.article.ID)
223				if err != nil || prev == 0 {
224					return m, nil
225				}
226				return m, func() tea.Msg {
227					return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenArticleView, ArticleID: prev, BoardID: m.article.BoardID}
228				}
229			case "]":
230				if m.article == nil {
231					return m, nil
232				}
233				_, next, err := m.deps.Store.Articles().NeighboursOf(context.Background(), m.article.BoardID, m.article.ID)
234				if err != nil || next == 0 {
235					return m, nil
236				}
237				return m, func() tea.Msg {
238					return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenArticleView, ArticleID: next, BoardID: m.article.BoardID}
239				}
240			case "+":
241				if !m.canPush() {
242					return m, nil
243				}
244				return m.openPush(store.PushKindPush), nil
245			case "-":
246				if !m.canPush() {
247					return m, nil
248				}
249				return m.openPush(store.PushKindBoo), nil
250			case "=":
251				if !m.canPush() {
252					return m, nil
253				}
254				return m.openPush(store.PushKindArrow), nil
255			case "p":
256				// Advance the push cursor. -1 → 0 → ... → len-1 → -1 (cycles
257				// back to "no selection, D targets the article").
258				if len(m.pushes) == 0 {
259					return m, nil
260				}
261				m.pushCursor++
262				if m.pushCursor >= len(m.pushes) {
263					m.pushCursor = -1
264				}
265				m.err = ""
266				return m, nil
267			case "P":
268				if len(m.pushes) == 0 {
269					return m, nil
270				}
271				if m.pushCursor < 0 {
272					m.pushCursor = len(m.pushes) - 1
273				} else {
274					m.pushCursor--
275				}
276				m.err = ""
277				return m, nil
278			case "D":
279				if m.pushCursor >= 0 {
280					if !m.canDeletePush(m.pushCursor) {
281						m.err = "權限不足 (only the push author or a mod can delete it)"
282						return m, nil
283					}
284				} else {
285					if !m.canDeleteArticle() {
286						m.err = "權限不足 (only the author or a mod can delete)"
287						return m, nil
288					}
289				}
290				m.pendingDelete = true
291				m.err = ""
292				return m, nil
293			case "E":
294				if m.article == nil {
295					return m, nil
296				}
297				if !m.canEditArticle() {
298					m.err = "權限不足 (only the author or a mod can edit)"
299					return m, nil
300				}
301				id := m.article.ID
302				boardID := m.article.BoardID
303				return m, func() tea.Msg {
304					return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenArticleEdit, ArticleID: id, BoardID: boardID}
305				}
306			case "y":
307				if m.article == nil {
308					return m, nil
309				}
310				id := m.article.ID
311				boardID := m.article.BoardID
312				return m, func() tea.Msg {
313					return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenArticleExport, ArticleID: id, BoardID: boardID}
314				}
315			case "M":
316				// Open the comments-mode picker. Mod+ only — silent no-op
317				// otherwise so non-mods don't get a confusing toast for a key
318				// the help line doesn't even surface to them (mirrors M on
319				// the board view).
320				if m.article == nil || !m.canSetCommentsMode() {
321					return m, nil
322				}
323				m.pickingCommentsMode = true
324				m.err = ""
325				return m, nil
326			}
327		}
328		return m, nil
329	}
330	
331	// updateDeleteConfirm handles y/n while the confirm overlay is up.
332	// y: dispatch the delete; if targeting a push, refresh the push list
333	// and the article (for the score). If targeting the article, navigate
334	// back to the board view.
335	// n / esc: cancel.
336	func (m articleViewModel) updateDeleteConfirm(msg tea.KeyMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
337		switch msg.String() {
338		case "y", "Y":
339			if m.article == nil || m.deps.User == nil {
340				m.pendingDelete = false
341				return m, nil
342			}
343			ctx := context.Background()
344			if m.pushCursor >= 0 && m.pushCursor < len(m.pushes) {
345				pushID := m.pushes[m.pushCursor].ID
346				if err := m.deps.Store.Pushes().Delete(ctx, pushID, m.deps.User.ID, m.deps.User.Role); err != nil {
347					m.err = err.Error()
348					m.pendingDelete = false
349					return m, nil
350				}
351				// Refresh the push list and re-fetch the article so its
352				// recommend_score reflects the reverted delta.
353				if pushes, err := m.deps.Store.Pushes().ListByArticle(ctx, m.article.ID); err == nil {
354					m.pushes = pushes
355				}
356				if a, err := m.deps.Store.Articles().GetByID(ctx, m.article.ID); err == nil {
357					m.article = a
358				}
359				// Clamp the cursor so the next D / p continues from a valid index.
360				if m.pushCursor >= len(m.pushes) {
361					m.pushCursor = -1
362				}
363				m.pendingDelete = false
364				return m, nil
365			}
366			if err := m.deps.Store.Articles().Delete(ctx, m.article.ID, m.deps.User.ID, m.deps.User.Role); err != nil {
367				m.err = err.Error()
368				m.pendingDelete = false
369				return m, nil
370			}
371			boardID := m.article.BoardID
372			return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenBoardView, BoardID: boardID} }
373		case "n", "N", "esc":
374			m.pendingDelete = false
375			return m, nil
376		}
377		return m, nil
378	}
379	
380	// bodyLineCount returns the number of lines View will render for the
381	// loaded article. Uses the glamour-rendered body so g/G/PgDn agree
382	// with what's actually on screen; falls back to raw if rendering hasn't
383	// run yet (shouldn't happen post-construction but defensive).
384	func (m articleViewModel) bodyLineCount() int {
385		if m.rendered != "" {
386			return len(strings.Split(m.rendered, "\n"))
387		}
388		if m.article == nil {
389			return 0
390		}
391		return len(strings.Split(m.article.Body, "\n"))
392	}
393	
394	// viewportLines mirrors the maxLines computation in View so g/G/PgDn
395	// agree on how many lines fit on screen.
396	func (m articleViewModel) viewportLines() int {
397		return max(m.height-16, 5)
398	}
399	
400	func (m articleViewModel) openPush(k store.PushKind) tea.Model {
401		// Early gate based on the cached article state. The store-side check
402		// in PushRepo.Create is the authoritative gate (handles races between
403		// sessions), but rejecting here gives a friendlier error than the
404		// raw sentinel and avoids opening the input box at all.
405		if m.article != nil {
406			switch m.article.CommentsMode {
407			case store.CommentsModeLocked:
408				m.err = "本文已鎖定留言"
409				return m
410			case store.CommentsModeArrowsOnly:
411				if k != store.PushKindArrow {
412					m.err = "本文僅開放箭頭留言"
413					return m
414				}
415			}
416		}
417		m.pushing = true
418		m.pushKind = k
419		m.pushIn.SetValue("")
420		m.pushIn.Focus()
421		m.err = ""
422		return m
423	}
424	
425	// updateCommentsModePicker handles 1/2/3/Esc while the comments-mode
426	// picker overlay is up. On a successful flip it refetches the article
427	// locally and broadcasts ArticleCommentsModeChangedMsg so other live
428	// sessions update their badges and gates.
429	func (m articleViewModel) updateCommentsModePicker(msg tea.KeyMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
430		if m.article == nil || m.deps.User == nil {
431			m.pickingCommentsMode = false
432			return m, nil
433		}
434		var mode store.CommentsMode
435		switch msg.String() {
436		case "1":
437			mode = store.CommentsModeOpen
438		case "2":
439			mode = store.CommentsModeArrowsOnly
440		case "3":
441			mode = store.CommentsModeLocked
442		case "esc", "n", "N":
443			m.pickingCommentsMode = false
444			return m, nil
445		default:
446			return m, nil
447		}
448		ctx := context.Background()
449		u := m.deps.User
450		if err := m.deps.Store.Articles().SetCommentsMode(ctx, m.article.ID, u.ID, u.Role, mode); err != nil {
451			m.err = err.Error()
452			m.pickingCommentsMode = false
453			return m, nil
454		}
455		if a, err := m.deps.Store.Articles().GetByID(ctx, m.article.ID); err == nil {
456			m.article = a
457		}
458		m.pickingCommentsMode = false
459		if m.deps.Broker != nil {
460			m.deps.Broker.SendToAll(u.ID, ArticleCommentsModeChangedMsg{
461				BoardID:   m.article.BoardID,
462				ArticleID: m.article.ID,
463				Mode:      string(mode),
464			})
465		}
466		return m, nil
467	}
468	
469	func (m articleViewModel) updatePushInput(msg tea.KeyMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
470		switch msg.String() {
471		case "esc":
472			m.pushing = false
473			m.pushIn.Blur()
474			return m, nil
475		case "enter":
476			body := strings.TrimSpace(m.pushIn.Value())
477			if body == "" && m.pushKind != store.PushKindArrow {
478				m.err = "comment required for 推/噓"
479				return m, nil
480			}
481			// Late gate: re-check comments_mode in case the cached article
482			// is stale. Server-side will also reject (PushRepo.Create), but
483			// catching here surfaces a friendlier message than the sentinel.
484			if m.article != nil {
485				switch m.article.CommentsMode {
486				case store.CommentsModeLocked:
487					m.err = "本文已鎖定留言"
488					m.pushing = false
489					m.pushIn.Blur()
490					return m, nil
491				case store.CommentsModeArrowsOnly:
492					if m.pushKind != store.PushKindArrow {
493						m.err = "本文僅開放箭頭留言"
494						m.pushing = false
495						m.pushIn.Blur()
496						return m, nil
497					}
498				}
499			}
500			ctx := context.Background()
501			u := m.deps.User
502			p, err := m.deps.Store.Pushes().Create(ctx, m.article.ID, u.ID, u.UserID, m.pushKind, body)
503			if err != nil {
504				m.err = err.Error()
505				return m, nil
506			}
507			// Append locally so the originator sees it immediately.
508			m.pushes = append(m.pushes, p)
509			if a, err := m.deps.Store.Articles().GetByID(ctx, m.article.ID); err == nil {
510				m.article = a
511			}
512			m.pushing = false
513			m.pushIn.Blur()
514			// Broadcast to other live sessions; they filter by ArticleID.
515			if m.deps.Broker != nil {
516				m.deps.Broker.SendToAll(u.ID, PushAddedMsg{
517					ArticleID:  m.article.ID,
518					UserUserID: u.UserID,
519					Kind:       string(m.pushKind),
520					Body:       body,
521				})
522			}
523			return m, nil
524		}
525		var cmd tea.Cmd
526		m.pushIn, cmd = m.pushIn.Update(msg)
527		return m, cmd
528	}
529	
530	func (m articleViewModel) View() string {
531		if m.loadErr != nil {
532			return "\n  " + StyleError.Render("⚠ "+m.loadErr.Error()) + "\n"
533		}
534		if m.article == nil {
535			return "\n  " + StyleDim.Render("(no article)") + "\n"
536		}
537		a := m.article
538	
539		var b strings.Builder
540		b.WriteString("\n")
541		b.WriteString(StyleHeader.Render(fmt.Sprintf(" 文章 #%d · %s ", a.ID, a.AuthorUserID)))
542		b.WriteString("\n\n")
543	
544		b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("Title: ") + a.Title + "\n")
545		b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("Date:  ") + a.CreatedAt.Format("2006-01-02 15:04:05") + "\n")
546		b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("Score: ") + fmt.Sprintf("%d", a.RecommendScore) + "\n")
547		switch a.CommentsMode {
548		case store.CommentsModeArrowsOnly:
549			b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("留言:  ") + StyleError.Render("[箭] 僅開放箭頭") + "\n")
550		case store.CommentsModeLocked:
551			b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("留言:  ") + StyleError.Render("[鎖] 已關閉留言") + "\n")
552		}
553		b.WriteString("\n")
554	
555		body := m.rendered
556		if body == "" {
557			body = a.Body
558		}
559		bodyLines := strings.Split(body, "\n")
560		start := min(m.scroll, len(bodyLines))
561		maxLines := max(m.height-16, 5)
562		end := min(start+maxLines, len(bodyLines))
563		for _, line := range bodyLines[start:end] {
564			b.WriteString(line + "\n")
565		}
566		if start > 0 || end < len(bodyLines) {
567			b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render(fmt.Sprintf("[lines %d-%d / %d]", start+1, end, len(bodyLines))))
568			b.WriteString("\n")
569		}
570	
571		if len(m.pushes) > 0 {
572			b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleDim.Render(fmt.Sprintf("── 推文 (%d) ──", len(m.pushes))) + "\n")
573			for i, p := range m.pushes {
574				ts := p.CreatedAt.Format("01/02 15:04")
575				gutter := "  "
576				if i == m.pushCursor {
577					gutter = "▸ "
578				}
579				line := fmt.Sprintf("%s%s %s %s  %s",
580					gutter,
581					renderPushKind(p.Kind),
582					PadRight(p.UserUserID, 14),
583					p.Body,
584					StyleDim.Render(ts),
585				)
586				if i == m.pushCursor {
587					line = StyleHighlight.Render(line)
588				}
589				b.WriteString(line + "\n")
590			}
591		}
592	
593		if m.pushing {
594			b.WriteString("\n  ")
595			b.WriteString(renderPushKind(m.pushKind) + " ")
596			b.WriteString(m.pushIn.View())
597			b.WriteString("\n")
598			if m.err != "" {
599				b.WriteString("  " + StyleError.Render("⚠ "+m.err) + "\n")
600			}
601			b.WriteString("  " + StyleHelp.Render("Enter send · Esc cancel"))
602		} else if m.pendingDelete {
603			prompt := "確定刪除這篇文章? (y/N)"
604			if m.pushCursor >= 0 && m.pushCursor < len(m.pushes) {
605				prompt = fmt.Sprintf("確定刪除推文 #%d? (y/N)", m.pushCursor+1)
606			}
607			b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleError.Render("⚠ "+prompt))
608		} else if m.pickingCommentsMode {
609			b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleHeader.Render(" 留言模式 "))
610			b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleHelp.Render("1 開放  2 僅箭頭  3 鎖文  Esc 取消"))
611		} else {
612			help := "j/k scroll · y 匯出"
613			if m.canPush() {
614				help += " · + 推 · - 噓 · = →"
615			}
616			if len(m.pushes) > 0 {
617				help += " · p/P 選推文"
618			}
619			if m.canEditArticle() {
620				help += " · E 編輯"
621			}
622			if m.canSetCommentsMode() {
623				help += " · M 留言模式"
624			}
625			if m.pushCursor >= 0 && m.canDeletePush(m.pushCursor) {
626				help += " · D 刪除推文"
627			} else if m.canDeleteArticle() {
628				help += " · D 刪除文章"
629			}
630			help += " · ? help · Esc/← back · Ctrl+C disconnect"
631			if m.err != "" {
632				b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleError.Render("⚠ "+m.err))
633			}
634			b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleHelp.Render(help))
635		}
636		b.WriteString("\n")
637		return b.String()
638	}
639	
640	func renderPushKind(k store.PushKind) string {
641		switch k {
642		case store.PushKindPush:
643			return StylePushKind.Render("推")
644		case store.PushKindBoo:
645			return StyleBooKind.Render("噓")
646		case store.PushKindArrow:
647			return StyleArrowKind.Render("→")
648		}
649		return "?"
650	}
651
```

```text
29	func newMailInboxModel(deps Deps) mailInboxModel {

36	func (m mailInboxModel) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
37		switch msg := msg.(type) {
38		case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
39			m.width, m.height = msg.Width, msg.Height
40			return m, nil
41		case tea.KeyMsg:
42			switch msg.String() {
43			case "esc", "backspace", "left", "h":
44				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenMainMenu} }
45			case "Q":
46				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenMainMenu} }
47			case "up", "k", "[":
48				if m.cursor > 0 {
49					m.cursor--
50				}
51			case "down", "j", "]":
52				if m.cursor < len(m.items)-1 {
53					m.cursor++
54				}
55			case "c":
56				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenMailCompose} }
57			case "r":
58				if len(m.items) == 0 {
59					return m, nil
60				}
61				it := m.items[m.cursor]
62				return m, func() tea.Msg {
63					return NavigateMsg{
64						To:        ScreenMailCompose,
65						Recipient: it.FromUserIDStr,
66						MailID:    it.ID,
67					}
68				}
69			case "enter", " ", "right", "l":
70				if len(m.items) == 0 {
71					return m, nil
72				}
73				it := m.items[m.cursor]
74				return m, func() tea.Msg {
75					return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenMailThread, MailThreadID: it.ThreadID}
76				}

105			PadRight("Date", dateW),

122			)

138	
139	type mailThreadModel struct {
140		deps     Deps
141		threadID int64
142		items    []*store.Mail
143		scroll   int
144		width    int
145		height   int
146		loadErr  error
147	
148		// rendered holds the glamour-formatted body for each item, parallel to
149		// items[]. Cached because (a) glamour's first call loads chroma's
150		// syntax highlighter and (b) View is called on every keystroke;
151		// re-rendering N message bodies per keypress would lag visibly.
152		// renderedWidth lets us re-render only when the viewport width changes.
153		rendered      []string
154		renderedWidth int
155	}
156	
157	func newMailThreadModel(deps Deps, threadID int64) mailThreadModel {
158		ctx := context.Background()
159		items, err := deps.Store.Mail().ListThread(ctx, threadID)
160		if err == nil {
161			// Mark every mail in the thread that's addressed to the current user
162			// as read. A thread you've opened is no longer "new".
163			for _, it := range items {
164				if it.ToUserID == deps.User.ID && !it.ReadAt.Valid {
165					_ = deps.Store.Mail().MarkRead(ctx, it.ID)
166				}
167			}
168		}
169		m := mailThreadModel{deps: deps, threadID: threadID, items: items, loadErr: err}
170		// Render at the fallback width so the body is readable even before the
171		// first WindowSizeMsg lands. Update re-renders on resize.
172		m.renderBodies()
173		return m
174	}
175	
176	// renderBodies runs each item's body through glamour at the current
177	// width. Cached on the model; cleared by passing a new width.
178	func (m *mailThreadModel) renderBodies() {
179		width := m.width - 4
180		if width < 40 {
181			width = glamourFallbackWidth
182		}
183		out := make([]string, len(m.items))
184		for i, it := range m.items {
185			out[i] = renderMarkdown(it.Body, width)
186		}
187		m.rendered = out
188		m.renderedWidth = width
189	}
190	
191	func (m mailThreadModel) Init() tea.Cmd { return nil }
192	
193	func (m mailThreadModel) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
194		switch msg := msg.(type) {
195		case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
196			m.width, m.height = msg.Width, msg.Height
197			newWidth := m.width - 4
198			if newWidth < 40 {
199				newWidth = glamourFallbackWidth
200			}
```

```text
440			return m, nil
441		}
442		if subj == "" {

445		}

449		}
450	
451		ctx := context.Background()
452		target, err := m.deps.Store.Users().GetByUserID(ctx, to)
453		if err != nil {
454			if errors.Is(err, store.ErrUserNotFound) {
455				m.err = "no such user: " + to
456			} else {
457				m.err = err.Error()
458			}
459			return m, nil
460		}
461		from := m.deps.User
462		var parentPtr *int64
463		if m.parentID != 0 {
464			p := m.parentID
465			parentPtr = &p
466		}
467		mail, err := m.deps.Store.Mail().Insert(ctx, from.ID, from.UserID, target.ID, subj, body, parentPtr)
468		if err != nil {
469			m.err = err.Error()
470			return m, nil
471		}
472		if target.ID == from.ID {
473			// Self-mail (memo). Skip Broker.Send: target is the caller, and
474			// pushing onto bubbletea's unbuffered msgs channel from inside
475			// the caller's own Update loop deadlocks the SSH session — see
476			// pitfalls/water-balloon-self-send-hangs-server.md (same root
477			// cause, different surface). Mark read immediately so the unread
478			// counter doesn't bump for a memo the user just wrote.
479			_ = m.deps.Store.Mail().MarkRead(ctx, mail.ID)
480		} else if m.deps.Broker != nil {
481			// Notify the recipient so an open inbox can refresh. Distinct from
482			// water balloons (which always toast); mail is "silent" delivery.
483			m.deps.Broker.Send(target.ID, MailIncomingMsg{
484				ID:         mail.ID,
485				ThreadID:   mail.ThreadID,
486				FromUserID: from.UserID,
487				Subject:    subj,
488			})
489		}
490		m.sent = target.UserID
491		return m, nil
492	}

494	// quoteForReply renders the parent mail as a markdown blockquote suitable
495	// for pre-filling the reply body. Format:
496	//
497	//	> alice · 2026-05-06 14:23 寫道:
498	//	>
499	//	> previous body line 1
500	//	> previous body line 2
501	//
502	//	<cursor lands here>
503	//
504	// Empty lines in the parent body are still prefixed with "> " so the
505	// blockquote stays contiguous (matches RFC 3676 / common email-client
506	// convention).
507	func quoteForReply(parent *store.Mail) string {
508		var b strings.Builder
509		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "> %s · %s 寫道:\n>\n",
510			parent.FromUserIDStr,
511			parent.CreatedAt.Format("2006-01-02 15:04"),
512		)
513		for _, line := range strings.Split(parent.Body, "\n") {
514			b.WriteString("> ")
515			b.WriteString(line)
516			b.WriteString("\n")
517		}
518		b.WriteString("\n")
519		return b.String()
520	}
```

```text
1	package store
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"database/sql"
6		"errors"
7		"strings"
8		"time"
9	)
10	
11	// CommentsMode controls who can leave 推/噓/→ pushes on an article. The
12	// values are persisted as TEXT in articles.comments_mode (see migration
13	// 0008). States are mutually exclusive — 'locked' is strictly stronger
14	// than 'arrows_only', so a single-column enum keeps the precedence rule
15	// unambiguous. Mod-only mutator: ArticleRepo.SetCommentsMode.
16	type CommentsMode string
17	
18	const (
19		CommentsModeOpen       CommentsMode = "open"        // default — push, boo, arrow all allowed
20		CommentsModeArrowsOnly CommentsMode = "arrows_only" // only neutral arrow comments
21		CommentsModeLocked     CommentsMode = "locked"      // all pushes rejected
22	)
23	
24	func (m CommentsMode) Valid() bool {
25		switch m {
26		case CommentsModeOpen, CommentsModeArrowsOnly, CommentsModeLocked:
27			return true
28		}
29		return false
30	}
31	
32	type Article struct {
33		ID             int64
34		BoardID        int64
35		AuthorID       int64
36		AuthorUserID   string
37		Title          string
38		Body           string
39		RecommendScore int64
40		Filemode       int64
41		CreatedAt      time.Time
42		UpdatedAt      sql.NullTime
43		PinnedAt       sql.NullTime
44		CommentsMode   CommentsMode
45	}
46	
47	type ArticleRepo struct{ s *Store }
48	
49	// ArticleSort selects the secondary ordering inside ListByBoardOpts. Pinned
50	// articles always float to the top regardless of sort — pin is an explicit
51	// moderation signal stronger than score or recency.
52	type ArticleSort int
53	
54	const (
55		SortNewestFirst ArticleSort = iota // default: created_at DESC, id DESC
56		SortByScoreDesc                    // recommend_score DESC, then created_at DESC, id DESC tiebreaker
57	)
58	
59	// ListArticlesOpts parameterises ListByBoardOpts. Zero value behaves
60	// identically to the legacy ListByBoard(boardID, 0): newest-first, no title
61	// filter, default 50-row limit.
62	type ListArticlesOpts struct {
63		Limit       int         // <=0 → 50
64		TitleSearch string      // empty → no filter; matched as LIKE '%q%' COLLATE NOCASE
65		Sort        ArticleSort
66	}
67	
68	// likeEscape protects user input from being interpreted as LIKE wildcards.
69	// Paired with `ESCAPE '\\'` in the SQL.
70	var likeEscape = strings.NewReplacer(`\`, `\\`, `%`, `\%`, `_`, `\_`)
71	
72	var (
73		ErrArticleNotFound     = errors.New("article not found")
74		ErrPermissionDenied    = errors.New("permission denied")
75		ErrInvalidCommentsMode = errors.New("invalid comments mode")
76	)
77	
78	const articleColumns = `id, board_id, author_id, author_userid, title, body,
79		recommend_score, filemode, created_at, updated_at, pinned_at, comments_mode`
80	
81	func scanArticle(row interface{ Scan(...any) error }) (*Article, error) {
82		a := &Article{}
83		var commentsMode string
84		err := row.Scan(
85			&a.ID, &a.BoardID, &a.AuthorID, &a.AuthorUserID, &a.Title, &a.Body,
86			&a.RecommendScore, &a.Filemode, &a.CreatedAt, &a.UpdatedAt, &a.PinnedAt,
87			&commentsMode,
88		)
89		a.CommentsMode = CommentsMode(commentsMode)
90		return a, err
91	}
92	
93	// ListByBoard returns the most-recent articles in a board, pinned first.
94	// Thin shim over ListByBoardOpts kept so existing call sites and tests
95	// (TestArticles_ListByBoard_NewestFirst, _Limit, _PinnedFirst) continue to
96	// exercise the legacy default behaviour as a regression guard.
97	func (r *ArticleRepo) ListByBoard(ctx context.Context, boardID int64, limit int) ([]*Article, error) {
98		return r.ListByBoardOpts(ctx, boardID, ListArticlesOpts{Limit: limit})
99	}
100	
101	// ListByBoardOpts is the parameterised list endpoint that supports a
102	// case-insensitive title substring filter and an alternate score-DESC sort.
103	//
104	// Pinned articles always surface first ((pinned_at IS NULL) sorts 0 before 1).
105	// Within each pinned/non-pinned group the secondary ordering depends on
106	// opts.Sort:
107	//   - SortNewestFirst:  created_at DESC, id DESC
108	//   - SortByScoreDesc:  recommend_score DESC, then created_at DESC, id DESC
109	//
110	// The created_at/id tiebreaker is essential in score mode because many fresh
111	// posts share recommend_score=0 and need a deterministic secondary order.
112	//
113	// TODO(score-vs-pushcount): SortByScoreDesc orders by recommend_score (signed
114	// sum: +1 push, -1 boo, 0 arrow). A future "raw 推文量" sort would need a
115	// separate maintained push_count column.
116	func (r *ArticleRepo) ListByBoardOpts(ctx context.Context, boardID int64, opts ListArticlesOpts) ([]*Article, error) {
117		limit := opts.Limit
118		if limit <= 0 {
119			limit = 50
120		}
121	
122		var (
123			where = "WHERE board_id = ?"
124			args  = []any{boardID}
125		)
126		if q := strings.TrimSpace(opts.TitleSearch); q != "" {
127			where += ` AND title LIKE ? ESCAPE '\' COLLATE NOCASE`
128			args = append(args, "%"+likeEscape.Replace(q)+"%")
129		}
130	
131		orderBy := `ORDER BY (pinned_at IS NULL), created_at DESC, id DESC`
132		if opts.Sort == SortByScoreDesc {
133			orderBy = `ORDER BY (pinned_at IS NULL), recommend_score DESC, created_at DESC, id DESC`
134		}
135	
136		args = append(args, limit)
137		rows, err := r.s.db.QueryContext(ctx,
138			`SELECT `+articleColumns+` FROM articles `+where+` `+orderBy+` LIMIT ?`,
139			args...,
140		)
141		if err != nil {
142			return nil, err
143		}
144		defer rows.Close()
145		var out []*Article
146		for rows.Next() {
147			a, err := scanArticle(rows)
148			if err != nil {
149				return nil, err
150			}
151			out = append(out, a)
152		}
153		return out, rows.Err()
154	}
155	
156	func (r *ArticleRepo) GetByID(ctx context.Context, id int64) (*Article, error) {
157		row := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, `SELECT `+articleColumns+` FROM articles WHERE id = ?`, id)
158		a, err := scanArticle(row)
159		if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
160			return nil, ErrArticleNotFound
161		}
162		return a, err
163	}
164	
165	// NeighboursOf returns the article IDs immediately older (prev) and newer
166	// (next) than articleID within the same board, or 0 when there is no
167	// neighbour on that side. "Older" / "newer" are by id (monotonic insert
168	// order); this matches the user-visible "[ / ]" semantics in article view.
169	func (r *ArticleRepo) NeighboursOf(ctx context.Context, boardID, articleID int64) (prev, next int64, err error) {
170		row := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx,
171			`SELECT id FROM articles WHERE board_id = ? AND id < ? ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 1`,
172			boardID, articleID,
173		)
174		if scanErr := row.Scan(&prev); scanErr != nil && !errors.Is(scanErr, sql.ErrNoRows) {
175			return 0, 0, scanErr
176		}
177		row = r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx,
178			`SELECT id FROM articles WHERE board_id = ? AND id > ? ORDER BY id ASC LIMIT 1`,
179			boardID, articleID,
180		)
181		if scanErr := row.Scan(&next); scanErr != nil && !errors.Is(scanErr, sql.ErrNoRows) {
182			return 0, 0, scanErr
183		}
184		return prev, next, nil
185	}
186	
187	func (r *ArticleRepo) Create(ctx context.Context, boardID, authorID int64, authorUserID, title, body string) (*Article, error) {
188		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
189		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
190		res, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
191			`INSERT INTO articles (board_id, author_id, author_userid, title, body) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)`,
192			boardID, authorID, authorUserID, title, body,
193		)
194		if err != nil {
195			return nil, err
196		}
197		id, err := res.LastInsertId()
198		if err != nil {
199			return nil, err
200		}
201		row := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, `SELECT `+articleColumns+` FROM articles WHERE id = ?`, id)
202		return scanArticle(row)
203	}
204	
205	// Delete hard-deletes the article. Permission: requester must be the author
206	// OR have a role at-or-above mod. Returns ErrArticleNotFound when the row
207	// is gone, ErrPermissionDenied when the requester is unauthorized.
208	// Cascades to pushes via the FK on pushes.article_id.
209	func (r *ArticleRepo) Delete(ctx context.Context, articleID, requesterID int64, requesterRole Role) error {
210		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
211		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
212		var authorID int64
213		err := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx,
214			`SELECT author_id FROM articles WHERE id = ?`, articleID,
215		).Scan(&authorID)
216		if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
217			return ErrArticleNotFound
218		}
219		if err != nil {
220			return err
221		}
222		if authorID != requesterID && !requesterRole.AtLeast(RoleMod) {
223			return ErrPermissionDenied
224		}
225		_, err = r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx, `DELETE FROM articles WHERE id = ?`, articleID)
226		return err
227	}
228	
229	// Update mutates the article's title and body and sets updated_at to now.
230	// Permission mirrors Delete: requester must be the author OR have a role
231	// at-or-above mod. Returns ErrArticleNotFound / ErrPermissionDenied. Score,
232	// board, and author_id are intentionally not editable here — those would be
233	// separate concerns (move-board / re-attribute) outside the user-facing
234	// edit flow.
235	func (r *ArticleRepo) Update(ctx context.Context, articleID, requesterID int64, requesterRole Role, newTitle, newBody string) error {
236		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
237		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
238		var authorID int64
239		err := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx,
240			`SELECT author_id FROM articles WHERE id = ?`, articleID,
241		).Scan(&authorID)
242		if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
243			return ErrArticleNotFound
244		}
245		if err != nil {
246			return err
247		}
248		if authorID != requesterID && !requesterRole.AtLeast(RoleMod) {
249			return ErrPermissionDenied
250		}
251		_, err = r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
252			`UPDATE articles SET title = ?, body = ?, updated_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP WHERE id = ?`,
253			newTitle, newBody, articleID,
254		)
255		return err
256	}
257	
258	// SetPinned pins (pinned=true) or unpins (pinned=false) an article. Permission
259	// shape mirrors BoardRepo.UpdateBanner: requesterRole must be at-or-above
260	// mod. Pinning is a moderation action — unlike Update/Delete it does NOT
261	// admit the article author. Returns ErrArticleNotFound when the row is gone,
262	// ErrPermissionDenied when the requester is unauthorized.
263	//
264	// Multi-pin per board is supported: the call simply sets pinned_at; nothing
265	// rejects a second pinned row on the same board.
266	func (r *ArticleRepo) SetPinned(ctx context.Context, articleID, requesterID int64, requesterRole Role, pinned bool) error {
267		if !requesterRole.AtLeast(RoleMod) {
268			return ErrPermissionDenied
269		}
270		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
271		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
272		var exists int64
273		err := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx,
274			`SELECT id FROM articles WHERE id = ?`, articleID,
275		).Scan(&exists)
276		if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
277			return ErrArticleNotFound
278		}
279		if err != nil {
280			return err
281		}
282		if pinned {
283			_, err = r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
284				`UPDATE articles SET pinned_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP WHERE id = ?`, articleID,
285			)
286		} else {
287			_, err = r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
288				`UPDATE articles SET pinned_at = NULL WHERE id = ?`, articleID,
289			)
290		}
291		return err
292	}
293	
294	// SetCommentsMode flips an article's comments_mode (open / arrows_only /
295	// locked). Permission shape mirrors SetPinned: requesterRole must be
296	// at-or-above mod, and unlike Update/Delete this does NOT admit the
297	// article author — locking comments is a moderation action. Returns
298	// ErrInvalidCommentsMode if mode is not one of the three constants,
299	// ErrPermissionDenied / ErrArticleNotFound otherwise. requesterID is
300	// accepted for signature parity with the other mutators (Pin/Update/Delete)
301	// but unused for the gate.
302	func (r *ArticleRepo) SetCommentsMode(ctx context.Context, articleID, requesterID int64, requesterRole Role, mode CommentsMode) error {
303		if !mode.Valid() {
304			return ErrInvalidCommentsMode
305		}
306		if !requesterRole.AtLeast(RoleMod) {
307			return ErrPermissionDenied
308		}
309		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
310		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
311		var exists int64
312		err := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx,
313			`SELECT id FROM articles WHERE id = ?`, articleID,
314		).Scan(&exists)
315		if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
316			return ErrArticleNotFound
317		}
318		if err != nil {
319			return err
320		}
321		_, err = r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
322			`UPDATE articles SET comments_mode = ? WHERE id = ?`, string(mode), articleID,
323		)
324		return err
325	}
326
```
</details></tool-use>

```sql
-- User settings (bio) plus a per-user notification system.
-- bio is a free-form profile blurb. Empty default keeps existing rows valid.
-- user_notif_prefs holds the four event toggles (push / wb / mail / reply)
-- plus only_when_offline. Row is upserted lazily; absence ≡ all-default.
-- user_notif_targets stores per-user webhook URLs that the dispatcher
-- POSTs `{title, body}` JSON to. Compatible with caronc/apprise-api's
-- /notify/<key> endpoint and any other simple webhook (Discord, ntfy.sh,
-- Slack, custom). The BBS deliberately does NOT parse apprise:// URLs —
-- that is delegated to whatever webhook target the user points us at.

ALTER TABLE users ADD COLUMN bio TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '';

CREATE TABLE user_notif_prefs (
    user_id           INTEGER PRIMARY KEY REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    on_push           INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1, -- 推/噓/→ on my article
    on_wb             INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1, -- 水球
    on_mail           INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1, -- 站內信
    on_reply          INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1, -- new article whose parent_id = mine
    only_when_offline INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0  -- skip dispatch if any live session exists
);

CREATE TABLE user_notif_targets (
    id         INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    user_id    INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    label      TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
    url        TEXT NOT NULL,
    enabled    INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
    created_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);
CREATE INDEX idx_notif_targets_user ON user_notif_targets(user_id, enabled);

```

```go
package store

import (
	"context"
	"database/sql"
	"errors"
	"strings"
	"time"
)

// NotifyPrefs is the per-user notification toggle bag. The boolean fields map
// 1:1 to the `on_*` columns in user_notif_prefs. Defaults (all event toggles
// on, only_when_offline off) are returned when the row is absent — the row is
// upserted lazily on the first SetPrefs call.
type NotifyPrefs struct {
	OnPush          bool
	OnWB            bool
	OnMail          bool
	OnReply         bool
	OnlyWhenOffline bool
}

// DefaultNotifyPrefs is what GetPrefs returns for users that have never
// touched the notify settings screen. Mirrors the column DEFAULTs in
// migrations/0009_user_settings_and_notify.sql.
func DefaultNotifyPrefs() NotifyPrefs {
	return NotifyPrefs{OnPush: true, OnWB: true, OnMail: true, OnReply: true}
}

// NotifyTarget is one configured webhook URL for a user. The dispatcher
// POSTs `{title, body}` JSON to URL when an event fires; Enabled lets the
// user pause a target without losing it.
type NotifyTarget struct {
	ID        int64
	UserID    int64
	Label     string
	URL       string
	Enabled   bool
	CreatedAt time.Time
}

// NotifyRepo owns the user_notif_prefs and user_notif_targets tables.
type NotifyRepo struct{ s *Store }

var (
	ErrNotifyTargetNotFound = errors.New("notify target not found")
	ErrInvalidNotifyURL     = errors.New("notify target url must be http(s)://...")
)

// ValidateNotifyURL enforces the bare minimum: scheme must be http or https.
// Anything beyond that (DNS, reachability) is the dispatcher's problem at
// delivery time — failing here would block legitimate self-hosted endpoints
// behind VPNs / private DNS.
func ValidateNotifyURL(u string) error {
	u = strings.TrimSpace(u)
	if strings.HasPrefix(u, "http://") || strings.HasPrefix(u, "https://") {
		return nil
	}
	return ErrInvalidNotifyURL
}

// GetPrefs returns the user's stored prefs, or DefaultNotifyPrefs() when
// the row is absent.
func (r *NotifyRepo) GetPrefs(ctx context.Context, userID int64) (NotifyPrefs, error) {
	row := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx,
		`SELECT on_push, on_wb, on_mail, on_reply, only_when_offline
		 FROM user_notif_prefs WHERE user_id = ?`, userID,
	)
	var onPush, onWB, onMail, onReply, onlyOffline int64
	err := row.Scan(&onPush, &onWB, &onMail, &onReply, &onlyOffline)
	if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
		return DefaultNotifyPrefs(), nil
	}
	if err != nil {
		return NotifyPrefs{}, err
	}
	return NotifyPrefs{
		OnPush:          onPush != 0,
		OnWB:            onWB != 0,
		OnMail:          onMail != 0,
		OnReply:         onReply != 0,
		OnlyWhenOffline: onlyOffline != 0,
	}, nil
}

// SetPrefs upserts the prefs row for a user.
func (r *NotifyRepo) SetPrefs(ctx context.Context, userID int64, p NotifyPrefs) error {
	r.s.writeMu.Lock()
	defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
	_, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
		`INSERT INTO user_notif_prefs
		   (user_id, on_push, on_wb, on_mail, on_reply, only_when_offline)
		 VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
		 ON CONFLICT(user_id) DO UPDATE SET
		   on_push=excluded.on_push,
		   on_wb=excluded.on_wb,
		   on_mail=excluded.on_mail,
		   on_reply=excluded.on_reply,
		   only_when_offline=excluded.only_when_offline`,
		userID, b2i(p.OnPush), b2i(p.OnWB), b2i(p.OnMail), b2i(p.OnReply), b2i(p.OnlyWhenOffline),
	)
	return err
}

// ListTargets returns every target row for a user, oldest-first so the
// settings screen renders a stable list.
func (r *NotifyRepo) ListTargets(ctx context.Context, userID int64) ([]*NotifyTarget, error) {
	rows, err := r.s.db.QueryContext(ctx,
		`SELECT id, user_id, label, url, enabled, created_at
		 FROM user_notif_targets WHERE user_id = ? ORDER BY id ASC`, userID,
	)
	if err != nil {
		return nil, err
	}
	defer rows.Close()
	var out []*NotifyTarget
	for rows.Next() {
		t := &NotifyTarget{}
		var enabled int64
		if err := rows.Scan(&t.ID, &t.UserID, &t.Label, &t.URL, &enabled, &t.CreatedAt); err != nil {
			return nil, err
		}
		t.Enabled = enabled != 0
		out = append(out, t)
	}
	return out, rows.Err()
}

// ListEnabledTargets is the dispatcher's hot path: only enabled rows.
func (r *NotifyRepo) ListEnabledTargets(ctx context.Context, userID int64) ([]*NotifyTarget, error) {
	rows, err := r.s.db.QueryContext(ctx,
		`SELECT id, user_id, label, url, enabled, created_at
		 FROM user_notif_targets WHERE user_id = ? AND enabled = 1 ORDER BY id ASC`, userID,
	)
	if err != nil {
		return nil, err
	}
	defer rows.Close()
	var out []*NotifyTarget
	for rows.Next() {
		t := &NotifyTarget{}
		var enabled int64
		if err := rows.Scan(&t.ID, &t.UserID, &t.Label, &t.URL, &enabled, &t.CreatedAt); err != nil {
			return nil, err
		}
		t.Enabled = enabled != 0
		out = append(out, t)
	}
	return out, rows.Err()
}

// AddTarget validates the URL and inserts a new row. Returns the assigned id.
func (r *NotifyRepo) AddTarget(ctx context.Context, userID int64, label, url string) (int64, error) {
	if err := ValidateNotifyURL(url); err != nil {
		return 0, err
	}
	r.s.writeMu.Lock()
	defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
	res, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
		`INSERT INTO user_notif_targets (user_id, label, url) VALUES (?, ?, ?)`,
		userID, strings.TrimSpace(label), strings.TrimSpace(url),
	)
	if err != nil {
		return 0, err
	}
	return res.LastInsertId()
}

// UpdateTarget mutates label/url/enabled in place. URL is validated.
// requesterUserID is checked against the row's user_id so a malicious
// caller can't update someone else's target by guessing the id.
func (r *NotifyRepo) UpdateTarget(ctx context.Context, id, requesterUserID int64, label, url string, enabled bool) error {
	if err := ValidateNotifyURL(url); err != nil {
		return err
	}
	r.s.writeMu.Lock()
	defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
	res, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
		`UPDATE user_notif_targets SET label = ?, url = ?, enabled = ?
		 WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?`,
		strings.TrimSpace(label), strings.TrimSpace(url), b2i(enabled), id, requesterUserID,
	)
	if err != nil {
		return err
	}
	n, _ := res.RowsAffected()
	if n == 0 {
		return ErrNotifyTargetNotFound
	}
	return nil
}

// SetTargetEnabled is the lightweight toggle path used by the `t` shortcut
// in the settings screen — separate from UpdateTarget to avoid re-asserting
// label/url validation on every keystroke.
func (r *NotifyRepo) SetTargetEnabled(ctx context.Context, id, requesterUserID int64, enabled bool) error {
	r.s.writeMu.Lock()
	defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
	res, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
		`UPDATE user_notif_targets SET enabled = ?
		 WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?`,
		b2i(enabled), id, requesterUserID,
	)
	if err != nil {
		return err
	}
	n, _ := res.RowsAffected()
	if n == 0 {
		return ErrNotifyTargetNotFound
	}
	return nil
}

// DeleteTarget removes a row, gated on user_id so the caller can only
// delete their own targets.
func (r *NotifyRepo) DeleteTarget(ctx context.Context, id, requesterUserID int64) error {
	r.s.writeMu.Lock()
	defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
	res, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
		`DELETE FROM user_notif_targets WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?`,
		id, requesterUserID,
	)
	if err != nil {
		return err
	}
	n, _ := res.RowsAffected()
	if n == 0 {
		return ErrNotifyTargetNotFound
	}
	return nil
}

func b2i(b bool) int64 {
	if b {
		return 1
	}
	return 0
}

```

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```go
// Package notify is the per-user webhook fan-out for BBS events
// (push / 水球 / mail / Re:). The dispatcher reads each user's prefs +
// enabled targets from the store and POSTs `{title, body}` JSON to every
// target — compatible with caronc/apprise-api's /notify/<key> endpoint
// and any other simple webhook receiver (Discord, Slack, ntfy.sh, etc.).
//
// The BBS deliberately does NOT parse apprise:// URLs or vendor an apprise
// library. That choice keeps the BBS dependency-light and lets users plug
// in any webhook stack. See docs/notifications.md for the architectural
// rationale.
package notify

import (
	"bytes"
	"context"
	"encoding/json"
	"net/http"
	"sync"
	"time"

	"github.com/charmbracelet/log"

	"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store"
)

// Kind names the four event types user_notif_prefs gates on.
type Kind string

const (
	KindPush  Kind = "push"  // someone pushed/booed/arrowed my article
	KindWB    Kind = "wb"    // someone sent me a 水球
	KindMail  Kind = "mail"  // someone sent me a 站內信
	KindReply Kind = "reply" // someone posted a Re: reply targeting my article
)

// Event is one notification request handed to Dispatch. Title and Body are
// pre-formatted display strings — the dispatcher does not own message
// formatting; call sites do, so the user sees consistent text whether the
// event lands as a 水球 toast or a webhook delivery.
type Event struct {
	Kind       Kind
	ToUserID   int64
	FromUserID string // for logging only
	Title      string
	Body       string
}

// OnlineChecker is the slice of *chat.Broker the dispatcher needs for
// the only_when_offline pref. Decoupled so tests can substitute a fake.
type OnlineChecker interface {
	IsOnline(uid int64) bool
}

// Manager is the dispatcher. Construct with New, start workers with Start,
// and call Dispatch from event call sites. Dispatch is non-blocking — a
// full queue drops the event and logs (we never want notification I/O to
// stall an Update path).
type Manager struct {
	store  *store.Store
	online OnlineChecker

	queue   chan Event
	client  *http.Client
	workers int

	wg     sync.WaitGroup
	stopMu sync.Mutex
	closed bool
}

// payload is the JSON body POSTed to every target. Matches apprise-api's
// /notify/<key> contract; non-apprise webhook receivers either consume
// these fields directly (most tooling does) or template against them.
type payload struct {
	Title string `json:"title"`
	Body  string `json:"body"`
}

const (
	defaultQueue   = 256
	defaultWorkers = 4
	defaultTimeout = 5 * time.Second
)

// New builds a Manager. online may be nil for tests that don't care about
// the only_when_offline pref.
func New(st *store.Store, online OnlineChecker) *Manager {
	return &Manager{
		store:   st,
		online:  online,
		queue:   make(chan Event, defaultQueue),
		client:  &http.Client{Timeout: defaultTimeout},
		workers: defaultWorkers,
	}
}

// Start spawns N worker goroutines that read from the queue and POST
// notifications until ctx is cancelled. Returns immediately. Call Stop()
// from the server's shutdown path to drain in-flight events.
func (m *Manager) Start(ctx context.Context) {
	for i := 0; i < m.workers; i++ {
		m.wg.Add(1)
		go m.worker(ctx)
	}
}

// Stop closes the queue and waits for workers to drain. Safe to call
// multiple times. Does NOT cancel in-flight HTTP requests — the http.Client
// timeout is the upper bound on how long Stop blocks per worker.
func (m *Manager) Stop() {
	m.stopMu.Lock()
	if m.closed {
		m.stopMu.Unlock()
		return
	}
	m.closed = true
	close(m.queue)
	m.stopMu.Unlock()
	m.wg.Wait()
}

// Dispatch enqueues an event for asynchronous delivery. Non-blocking: on a
// full queue (slow webhook upstream + many concurrent events) we drop the
// event and log a warning. We deliberately prefer drop-with-log over
// blocking the caller because the call sites are inside bubbletea Update
// paths — blocking there would freeze the SSH session.
func (m *Manager) Dispatch(ev Event) {
	m.stopMu.Lock()
	closed := m.closed
	m.stopMu.Unlock()
	if closed {
		return
	}
	select {
	case m.queue <- ev:
	default:
		log.Warn("notify: queue full, dropping event",
			"kind", ev.Kind, "to", ev.ToUserID, "from", ev.FromUserID)
	}
}

func (m *Manager) worker(ctx context.Context) {
	defer m.wg.Done()
	for {
		select {
		case <-ctx.Done():
			return
		case ev, ok := <-m.queue:
			if !ok {
				return
			}
			m.deliver(ctx, ev)
		}
	}
}

func (m *Manager) deliver(ctx context.Context, ev Event) {
	prefs, err := m.store.Notify().GetPrefs(ctx, ev.ToUserID)
	if err != nil {
		log.Warn("notify: load prefs", "to", ev.ToUserID, "err", err)
		return
	}
	if !prefsAllow(prefs, ev.Kind) {
		return
	}
	if prefs.OnlyWhenOffline && m.online != nil && m.online.IsOnline(ev.ToUserID) {
		return
	}
	targets, err := m.store.Notify().ListEnabledTargets(ctx, ev.ToUserID)
	if err != nil {
		log.Warn("notify: load targets", "to", ev.ToUserID, "err", err)
		return
	}
	if len(targets) == 0 {
		return
	}
	body, err := json.Marshal(payload{Title: ev.Title, Body: ev.Body})
	if err != nil {
		log.Warn("notify: marshal", "err", err)
		return
	}
	for _, t := range targets {
		m.post(ctx, t, body, ev)
	}
}

func (m *Manager) post(ctx context.Context, t *store.NotifyTarget, body []byte, ev Event) {
	req, err := http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, http.MethodPost, t.URL, bytes.NewReader(body))
	if err != nil {
		log.Warn("notify: build request", "target", t.ID, "err", err)
		return
	}
	req.Header.Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
	req.Header.Set("User-Agent", "sshbbs-notify/1")
	resp, err := m.client.Do(req)
	if err != nil {
		log.Warn("notify: post failed",
			"target", t.ID, "url", t.URL, "kind", ev.Kind, "err", err)
		return
	}
	defer resp.Body.Close()
	if resp.StatusCode >= 400 {
		log.Warn("notify: target rejected",
			"target", t.ID, "url", t.URL, "status", resp.StatusCode, "kind", ev.Kind)
	}
}

func prefsAllow(p store.NotifyPrefs, k Kind) bool {
	switch k {
	case KindPush:
		return p.OnPush
	case KindWB:
		return p.OnWB
	case KindMail:
		return p.OnMail
	case KindReply:
		return p.OnReply
	}
	return false
}

```

```text
20		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/server"
21		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store"
22		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/version"
23	)
24	
25	func main() {
26		// Subcommand dispatch: anything that isn't a flag is treated as a
27		// subcommand. Today only `import` exists, but the shape leaves room
28		// for future ops tools (e.g. `export`, `dump`).
29		if len(os.Args) >= 2 && !strings.HasPrefix(os.Args[1], "-") {
30			switch os.Args[1] {
31			case "import":
32				os.Exit(runImport(os.Args[2:]))
33			default:
34				log.Fatalf("unknown subcommand: %s", os.Args[1])
35			}
36		}
37	
38		addr := flag.String("addr", ":2222", "SSH listen address")
39		dbPath := flag.String("db", "data/bbs.db", "SQLite database path")
40		hostkey := flag.String("hostkey", ".ssh/host_ed25519", "SSH host key path")
41		adminPassword := flag.String("admin-password", "",
42			"override admin password on first seed; empty uses the baked default 'admin' with must_change_password=1")
43		flag.Parse()
44	
45		st, err := store.Open(*dbPath)
46		if err != nil {
47			log.Fatalf("store.Open: %v", err)
48		}
49		defer st.Close()
50		if err := st.Boards().SeedDefaults(context.Background()); err != nil {
51			log.Fatalf("seed boards: %v", err)
52		}
53		if err := auth.SeedSystemAccounts(context.Background(), st, auth.SeedOpts{
54			AdminPassword: *adminPassword,
55		}); err != nil {
56			log.Fatalf("seed system accounts: %v", err)
57		}
58		if err := seed.Articles(context.Background(), st, auth.ReservedUsernameAdmin); err != nil {
59			log.Fatalf("seed articles: %v", err)
60		}
61		if err := seed.Banners(context.Background(), st, auth.ReservedUsernameAdmin); err != nil {
62			log.Fatalf("seed banners: %v", err)
63		}
64		log.Printf("storage ready at %s", *dbPath)
65	
66		broker := chat.NewBroker()
67	
68		srv, err := server.New(server.Config{Addr: *addr, HostKey: *hostkey}, st, broker)
69		if err != nil {
70			log.Fatalf("server.New: %v", err)
71		}
72	
73		ctx, stop := signal.NotifyContext(context.Background(), os.Interrupt, syscall.SIGTERM)
74		defer stop()
75	
76		errCh := make(chan error, 1)
77		go func() {
78			log.Printf("sshbbs %s listening on %s", version.Version, *addr)
79			errCh <- srv.ListenAndServe()
80		}()
81	
82		select {
83		case err := <-errCh:
84			if err != nil && !errors.Is(err, ssh.ErrServerClosed) {
85				log.Fatalf("listen: %v", err)
86			}
87		case <-ctx.Done():
88			log.Println("shutdown signal received")
89			shutdown(srv, broker)
90		}
91	}
92	
93	// shutdown closes the listener (refuses new connections), then asks every
94	// live bubbletea program to quit, then closes the SSH server hard. The DB
95	// closes on the deferred st.Close() in main.
96	func shutdown(srv *ssh.Server, broker *chat.Broker) {
97		const drainTimeout = 3 * time.Second
98	
99		// 1. Stop accepting new connections.
100		listenerCtx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), drainTimeout)
101		defer cancel()
102		if err := srv.Shutdown(listenerCtx); err != nil {
103			log.Printf("server.Shutdown: %v", err)
104		}
105	
106		// 2. Tell every active session to quit.
107		sessions := broker.SessionsSnapshot()
108		if len(sessions) == 0 {
109			return
110		}
111		log.Printf("draining %d active session(s)", len(sessions))
112		for _, s := range sessions {
113			s.Program.Send(tea.Quit())
114		}
115	
116		// 3. Give them a moment to flush their final view, then force-close.
117		timer := time.NewTimer(drainTimeout)
118		defer timer.Stop()
119		for {
120			if len(broker.SessionsSnapshot()) == 0 {
121				log.Println("all sessions drained")
122				return
123			}
124			select {
125			case <-timer.C:
126				log.Printf("drain timeout; %d session(s) still attached", len(broker.SessionsSnapshot()))
127				return
128			case <-time.After(100 * time.Millisecond):
129			}
130		}
131	}
```

```go
package tui

import (
	"fmt"
	"strings"

	tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"
)

// userSettingsModel is the "個人設定" sub-menu reached from the main menu.
// Three entries: change password, edit bio, edit notification settings.
// Layout and key handling mirror mainMenuModel deliberately so muscle memory
// transfers across BBS-internal menus.
type userSettingsModel struct {
	deps   Deps
	items  []menuItem
	cursor int
}

func newUserSettingsModel(deps Deps) userSettingsModel {
	items := []menuItem{
		{label: "修改密碼 Change password", hint: "current → new → confirm", to: ScreenPasswordChange},
		{label: "修改 Bio Edit bio", hint: "free-form profile blurb", to: ScreenBioEdit},
		{label: "通知設定 Notification settings", hint: "webhook targets + per-event toggles", to: ScreenNotifySettings},
		{label: "返回 Back", hint: "main menu", to: ScreenMainMenu},
	}
	return userSettingsModel{deps: deps, items: items}
}

func (m userSettingsModel) Init() tea.Cmd { return nil }

func (m userSettingsModel) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
	if k, ok := msg.(tea.KeyMsg); ok {
		switch k.String() {
		case "esc", "q", "left", "h", "backspace":
			return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenMainMenu} }
		case "Q":
			return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenMainMenu} }
		case "up", "k":
			if m.cursor > 0 {
				m.cursor--
			}
			return m, nil
		case "down", "j":
			if m.cursor < len(m.items)-1 {
				m.cursor++
			}
			return m, nil
		case "enter", " ", "right", "l":
			it := m.items[m.cursor]
			return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: it.to} }
		case "1", "2", "3", "4":
			idx := int(k.String()[0] - '1')
			if idx < len(m.items) {
				it := m.items[idx]
				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: it.to} }
			}
		}
	}
	return m, nil
}

func (m userSettingsModel) View() string {
	u := m.deps.User
	var b strings.Builder
	b.WriteString("\n")
	b.WriteString(StyleHeader.Render(" 個人設定 User Settings "))
	b.WriteString("\n\n")

	if u != nil {
		b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render(fmt.Sprintf("帳號 %s · 角色 %s", u.UserID, u.Role)))
		b.WriteString("\n")
		bio := strings.TrimSpace(u.Bio)
		if bio == "" {
			b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("(尚未填寫 bio)"))
		} else {
			// Show only the first line of bio in the header so the menu
			// itself stays compact; the bio-edit screen owns the full view.
			firstLine := bio
			if i := strings.IndexByte(firstLine, '\n'); i >= 0 {
				firstLine = firstLine[:i]
			}
			b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("Bio: ") + Truncate(firstLine, 60))
		}
		b.WriteString("\n\n")
	}

	for i, it := range m.items {
		marker := "  "
		row := fmt.Sprintf("  %d. %s", i+1, it.label)
		if i == m.cursor {
			marker = "▸ "
			row = StyleHighlight.Render(fmt.Sprintf(" %d. %-40s ", i+1, it.label))
		}
		b.WriteString(marker + row)
		if it.hint != "" {
			b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render(it.hint))
		}
		b.WriteString("\n")
	}

	b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleHelp.Render("↑/↓ j/k move · Enter/→/l choose · 1-4 jump · Esc/←/h back"))
	b.WriteString("\n")
	return b.String()
}
```

```go
import (
	"context"
	"strings"

	"github.com/charmbracelet/bubbles/textarea"
	tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"

	"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/auth"
)

// bioEditModel is a single-textarea form screen reached from the user
// settings menu. Form-screen convention (CLAUDE.md): no h/l navigation —
// those keys must remain available for text editing.
type bioEditModel struct {
	deps    Deps
	body    textarea.Model
	width   int
	height  int
	err     string
	success bool
}

func newBioEditModel(deps Deps) bioEditModel {
	ta := textarea.New()
	ta.Placeholder = "你的 bio…（最多 1024 字元，可換行）"
	ta.CharLimit = 4096 // generous client-side; auth.ValidateBio is the source of truth (rune count)
	ta.SetWidth(72)
	ta.SetHeight(10)
	if deps.User != nil && deps.User.Bio != "" {
		ta.SetValue(deps.User.Bio)
	}
	ta.Focus()
	return bioEditModel{deps: deps, body: ta}
}

func (m bioEditModel) Init() tea.Cmd { return textarea.Blink }

func (m bioEditModel) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
	switch msg := msg.(type) {
	case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
		m.width, m.height = msg.Width, msg.Height
		m.body.SetWidth(max(40, msg.Width-8))
		m.body.SetHeight(max(8, msg.Height-12))
		return m, nil

	case tea.KeyMsg:
		if m.success {
			return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenUserSettings} }
		}
		switch msg.String() {
		case "esc":
			return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenUserSettings} }
		case "ctrl+s":
			return m.submit()
		}
	}
	var cmd tea.Cmd
	m.body, cmd = m.body.Update(msg)
	return m, cmd
}

func (m bioEditModel) submit() (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
	bio := strings.TrimRight(m.body.Value(), "\n")
	if err := auth.ValidateBio(bio); err != nil {
		m.err = err.Error()
		return m, nil
	}
	if m.deps.User == nil {
		m.err = "internal error: no user"
		return m, nil
	}
	ctx := context.Background()
	if err := m.deps.Store.Users().SetBio(ctx, m.deps.User.ID, bio); err != nil {
		m.err = err.Error()
		return m, nil
	}
	// Refresh User in place so the settings screen header and any future
	// reads see the canonical bio without a re-login.
	if fresh, err := m.deps.Store.Users().GetByID(ctx, m.deps.User.ID); err == nil {
		*m.deps.User = *fresh
	}
	m.success = true
	return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenUserSettings} }
}

func (m bioEditModel) View() string {
	var b strings.Builder
	b.WriteString("\n")
	b.WriteString(StyleHeader.Render(" 修改 Bio Edit bio "))
	b.WriteString("\n\n")

	b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("Bio (free-form, ≤ 1024 chars; newlines OK):") + "\n")
	b.WriteString("  " + indent(m.body.View(), "  ") + "\n")

	if m.err != "" {
		b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleError.Render("⚠ "+m.err))
	}
	if m.success {
		b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleSuccess.Render("✓ saved"))
	}
	b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleHelp.Render("Ctrl+S save · Esc cancel"))
	b.WriteString("\n")
	return b.String()
}
```
